message.content:
You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). Tools: Bash, Read.

STEP 0 — SKIP IF DONE. Run: test -s /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_44.tsv && echo DONE || echo TODO
If it prints DONE, this shard is already complete — reply exactly "shard 44: cached" and STOP. Do nothing else.

STEP 1 — get your file list: /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; print(chr(10).join(json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[44]))"
It prints lines "relpath:START-END" relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent.

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every assigned file/range line by line with the Read tool (offset/limit from START-END; use limit up to 2000 per call to minimize calls). Do NOT grep-and-skim — read the code.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (file/module, class, func/method, var, const, enum value) that looks UNPROFESSIONAL in an external review:
  - marketing/quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best
  - temporal/version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(meaning 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes/informal/vague/slang: junk, garbage, hack, spy, stub(as a real name), yolo, stuff, misc, foo/bar, doit, thing, blah, landmine, nuke, magic-number-as-word, terse cryptic abbreviations
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME.** Only KEEP-BUT-NOTED for unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a REAL domain term genuinely implemented (finalize, proper_noun, evict_older_than, SmartArt only if it truly makes SmartArt).

STEP 4 — APPEND TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_44.tsv (one per flagged name), using printf with real tabs:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY<TAB>SUGGESTED_NAME
  VERDICT=RENAME or KEEP-NOTED; KIND=file/class/func/var/const/enum. If a file is fully clean, write: NONE<TAB>-<TAB>relpath<TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-

Reply ONLY: "shard 44: <R> renames, <K> kept across <F> files".

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	"""I-beatboth-011 keystone-F1 (#1284, task #505) — the RELATIONAL-QUANTIFIER guard (F1-2).
2	
3	A pure, deterministic guard over a candidate multi-cited sentence + its supporting ``ClaimBasket``.
4	``strict_verify`` / span-grounding can confirm that every FACT in a sentence is span-true, but it
5	CANNOT verify a RELATIONAL QUANTIFIER: "studies CONSISTENTLY show X", "MOST studies find X", "X higher
6	than Y", "n of m" — every cited fact can be span-true while the aggregate word is invented across the
7	basket's members. The faithfulness engine has no hook for that aggregate predicate; this guard is the
8	COMPOSITION-LAYER check that closes the gap WITHOUT touching the engine (design basis §32-46,
9	PHASE4_DESIGN_BASIS_2026.md; `lane_synthesis_baskets_design.md` §2.3).
10	
11	CONTRACT (binding):
12	  * The guard NEVER edits or relaxes ``strict_verify`` / NLI / 4-role / span-grounding. It runs OUTSIDE
13	    the engine on already-composed prose; its only effect is to STRIP an UNLICENSED relational
14	    quantifier so the residue carries no aggregate claim the basket does not license. A stripped
15	    sentence still carries its original ``[#ev:...]`` provenance tokens unchanged, so it re-passes the
16	    UNCHANGED ``strict_verify`` (the caller re-verifies — this module asserts nothing).
17	  * UNDER-RELAX IS SAFE; OVER-RELAX IS LETHAL (design §40). When the licensing state is uncertain the
18	    guard STRIPS the quantifier (the conservative direction). A genuinely-licensed quantifier (the
19	    whole basket agrees, zero refuters, independence weight-mass above the env threshold) is KEPT —
20	    but the increment-1 producer never EMITS one, so in practice the guard only ever fires on an LLM
21	    writer's fabricated predicate.
22	  * DETERMINISTIC LEXICON: a high-precision multi-word aggregate-predicate lexicon (LAW VI threshold
23	    via ``PG_RELATIONAL_QUANTIFIER_AGREEMENT_MIN``). A lexicon may miss a paraphrase; the F1-0 harness
24	    is the escape-catcher (design §53). It is PRECISION-first — it strips a known aggregate predicate,
25	    it does not touch ordinary prose.
26	  * PURE: no input mutation, no network, no model, no faithfulness-file import. snake_case. LAW VI.
27	
28	KNOWN BOUND (honest, for a future writer author) — the verbatim-preservation check
29	(``_is_verbatim_source_text``) is WHOLE-CLAUSE: it preserves a source quantifier when the ENTIRE
30	candidate clause (sans provenance token + terminal punctuation) is a substring of one of the basket's
31	member spans. This fully covers the two currently-wired writer configs:
32	  * DEFAULT writer ``build_short_member_sentence`` returns a PURE verbatim span -> whole-substring match
33	    -> the source's quantifier is preserved.
34	  * ABSTRACTIVE writer (``PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER`` on) is keyed by ``claim_cluster_id`` and returns a
35	    WHOLE-basket draft; the within-basket producer's single-member sub-basket lookup fails the
36	    single-member scoped pool -> ``_member_writer_clause`` returns None -> the producer uses the
37	    UNGUARDED verbatim K-span fallback -> the guard never runs.
38	What it does NOT cover: a PARTIALLY-synthesized clause that EMBEDS a verbatim source quantifier inside a
39	FABRICATED frame (e.g. "Studies consistently show that most patients recovered [#ev]" over a span "most
40	patients recovered") — the whole candidate is longer than the span, so it routes to ``_strip_quantifiers``
41	which would strip the embedded "most" too. This path is UNREACHABLE on the current default/abstractive
42	wiring (above). A FUTURE per-member-keyed writer that produces single-member clauses passing the scoped
43	pool while embedding a source quantifier in synthesized framing MUST re-verify this case (add
44	per-occurrence span-presence logic then, against a behavioral test on that writer's real output).
45	"""
46	from __future__ import annotations
47	
48	import os
49	import re
50	from typing import Any, Optional
51	
52	_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"", "0", "false", "off", "no"})
53	
54	# LAW VI: the independence-weighted agreement threshold a basket must clear for a relational quantifier
55	# to be LICENSED (design §52 — no 2026 paper gives the formula, so it is an env-tunable). The agreement
56	# proxy is the share of the basket's members that are isolated-``SUPPORTS`` (corroborating).
57	#
58	# INCREMENT-1 DEFAULT = STRIP-ALWAYS (the boundary contract, advisor 2026-06-21 + brief §35-43):
59	# ``strict_verify`` / span-grounding CANNOT verify a relational quantifier, and this increment's producer
60	# composes per-MEMBER single-source clauses — it NEVER deliberately computes-and-emits a whole-basket
61	# consensus statement. So ANY quantifier reaching the guard is a fabrication of the (LLM) writer over a
62	# single member's clause, and must be STRIPPED. The default ``2.0`` is UNREACHABLE (agreement is in
63	# [0,1]), so the guard ALWAYS strips an unlicensed-by-construction quantifier — "under-relax is safe;
64	# over-relax is the lethal direction". The env knob is the seam a LATER increment uses (a producer that
65	# deliberately composes a disclosed consensus statement after the independence weight-mass computation
66	# can lower the threshold to, e.g., ``1.0`` = every member corroborates AND zero refuters); until then
67	# the gate stays closed. A value in [0,1] enables the licensing branch (zero refuters AND agreement >=
68	# threshold); the default keeps it closed.
69	_ENV_AGREEMENT_MIN = "PG_RELATIONAL_QUANTIFIER_AGREEMENT_MIN"
70	_DEFAULT_AGREEMENT_MIN = 2.0  # UNREACHABLE by construction => strip-always in increment 1 (boundary contract)
71	
72	# A provenance token: ``[#ev:<evidence_id>:<start>-<end>]`` (the same shape strict_verify parses).
73	_EV_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:[^\]]*\]")
74	
75	
76	def _agreement_min() -> float:
77	    """The configured independence-weighted agreement minimum (LAW VI). Returns the STRICT default
78	    (``2.0`` = unreachable = strip-always) when unset or on any unparseable / negative value
79	    (fail-CONSERVATIVE — a bad config strips MORE, never less). A value the operator deliberately sets
80	    in [0,1] is honored (it OPENS the licensing branch for a later consensus-statement producer); a
81	    value > 1.0 keeps the branch closed (strip-always)."""
82	    raw = os.environ.get(_ENV_AGREEMENT_MIN, "").strip()
83	    if raw == "":
84	        return _DEFAULT_AGREEMENT_MIN
85	    try:
86	        val = float(raw)
87	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
88	        return _DEFAULT_AGREEMENT_MIN  # unparseable -> strict default
89	    if val != val or val < 0.0:  # NaN / negative -> strict default (never licenses MORE on bad input)
90	        return _DEFAULT_AGREEMENT_MIN
91	    return val
92	
93	
94	# ── The aggregate-predicate lexicon (high-precision, multi-word where possible) ────────────────────
95	#
96	# Each entry is a compiled regex that matches an aggregate/relational predicate AS IT APPEARS in prose.
97	# Two repair shapes:
98	#   * LEADING predicates ("Most studies show that ...", "Studies consistently show that ...") — the
99	#     match is a sentence-leading clause; stripping it + re-capitalizing the residue yields the faithful
100	#     single-claim sentence.
101	#   * INLINE quantifiers ("most", "the majority of", "consistently", "broadly") — stripped in place.
102	# The lexicon is PRECISION-first: it targets aggregate predicates, never ordinary determiners. "a", "the",
103	# "some", "several" are NOT aggregate consensus claims and are left untouched.
104	
105	# Leading aggregate-attribution clauses: "<quantifier> studies/sources/researchers <verb> that".
106	_LEADING_ATTRIBUTION_RE = re.compile(
107	    r"^\s*(?:the\s+)?"
108	    r"(?:most|many|the\s+majority\s+of|nearly\s+all|almost\s+all|the\s+bulk\s+of|"
109	    r"a\s+(?:broad|wide)\s+(?:range|body)\s+of|numerous|several|multiple)?\s*"
110	    r"(?:studies|sources|researchers|papers|analyses|reports|experts|authors|works)\s+"
111	    r"(?:have\s+)?(?:consistently\s+|broadly\s+|overwhelmingly\s+|universally\s+|generally\s+|"
112	    r"largely\s+)?"
113	    r"(?:show|shows|showed|find|finds|found|report|reports|reported|agree|agrees|agreed|"
114	    r"demonstrate|demonstrates|demonstrated|conclude|concludes|concluded|suggest|suggests|"
115	    r"suggested|indicate|indicates|indicated|confirm|confirms|confirmed)\s+that\s+",
116	    re.IGNORECASE,
117	)
118	
119	# Standalone "broad/wide consensus" / "growing consensus" attributions.
120	_CONSENSUS_LEADING_RE = re.compile(
121	    r"^\s*(?:there\s+is\s+|there\s+exists\s+)?"
122	    r"(?:a\s+)?(?:broad|wide|growing|strong|general|emerging)\s+consensus\s+(?:that|exists\s+that)\s+",
123	    re.IGNORECASE,
124	)
125	
126	# Inline aggregate quantifiers/adverbs that assert cross-member agreement. Stripped in place. Bounded by
127	# word boundaries; multi-word phrases first so the longest match wins.
128	_INLINE_QUANTIFIERS = (
129	    r"the\s+majority\s+of\s+",
130	    r"the\s+vast\s+majority\s+of\s+",
131	    r"a\s+(?:broad|wide)\s+(?:majority|consensus|range)\s+of\s+",
132	    r"most\s+of\s+the\s+",
133	    r"most\s+",
134	    r"consistently\s+",
135	    r"overwhelmingly\s+",
136	    r"universally\s+",
137	    r"broadly\s+",
138	    r"unanimously\s+",
139	)
140	_INLINE_QUANTIFIER_RE = re.compile(
141	    r"(?<![A-Za-z])(?:" + "|".join(_INLINE_QUANTIFIERS) + r")",
142	    re.IGNORECASE,
143	)
144	
145	# "n of m" / "X of Y studies" relational counts (an aggregate over the basket's members).
146	_N_OF_M_RE = re.compile(
147	    r"(?<![A-Za-z0-9])"
148	    r"(?:\d+|one|two|three|four|five|six|seven|eight|nine|ten)\s+(?:out\s+of|of)\s+"
149	    r"(?:\d+|two|three|four|five|six|seven|eight|nine|ten)\s+"
150	    r"(?:studies|sources|researchers|papers|analyses|reports|trials)\s+",
151	    re.IGNORECASE,
152	)
153	
154	# CROSS-MEMBER COMPARATIVE adverbs ("X higher than Y", "more than", "greater than") that assert a
155	# RELATION the basket's members do NOT individually license — a comparative across two members' spans is
156	# an aggregate the engine cannot verify. DETECTION-ONLY in increment 1: a comparative is reported by
157	# ``contains_relational_quantifier`` so the producer never silently co-locates one as a SYNTHESIZED
158	# clause, but ``_strip_quantifiers`` does NOT attempt to rewrite a comparative (deleting "higher than"
159	# mangles the sentence). Repair = drop the synthesized comparative clause to its VERBATIM K-span fallback
160	# (the producer's member-level fallback already does this when the synthesized clause is annihilated).
161	# A comparative INSIDE a single member's verbatim span (the source's own comparison) is faithful and
162	# preserved (the guard never runs on a verbatim K-span). Comparative full-rewrite is a DEFERRED increment.
163	_COMPARATIVE_RE = re.compile(
164	    r"(?<![A-Za-z])"
165	    r"(?:higher|greater|larger|lower|smaller|stronger|weaker|faster|slower|better|worse|more|less|"
166	    r"longer|shorter)\s+than\b",
167	    re.IGNORECASE,
168	)
169	
170	
171	# I-deepfix-001 M6 — the CLOSED set of cross-source analytical connective PHRASES + their relation key.
172	# These mirror ``cross_source_synthesis.LICENSED_CONNECTIVES`` (kept in sync). A connective is LICENSED
173	# upstream by a certified relation engine (``claim_graph`` ContradictionEdge / ``consolidation_nli`` /
174	# equivalence); the composer passes the licensed relation(s) via ``licensed_relations`` so the guard can
175	# NEUTRALIZE any connective whose relation was NOT licensed back to pure juxtaposition — "a wrong
176	# 'in contrast' can never render". The neutral phrase asserts no relation, so it is never neutralized.
177	_ANALYTICAL_CONNECTIVE_RELATION: dict[str, str] = {
178	    "in contrast": "conflict",
179	    "consistent with this": "agreement",
180	    "extending this": "extension",
181	}
182	_NEUTRAL_CONNECTIVE_PHRASE = "separately"
183	# Each relation phrase as it appears inside the connective ``; <phrase>, `` — captured WITH its leading
184	# "; " and trailing ", " so a neutralized phrase swaps cleanly to ``; separately, ``.
185	_ANALYTICAL_CONNECTIVE_RE = re.compile(
186	    r";\s*(?P<phrase>in contrast|consistent with this|extending this)\s*,\s*",
187	    re.IGNORECASE,
188	)
189	
190	
191	def _neutralize_unlicensed_connectives(sentence: str, licensed_relations: "set[str]") -> str:
192	    """Replace any analytical connective whose relation is NOT in ``licensed_relations`` with the neutral
193	    juxtaposition connective ``; separately, ``. A connective whose relation IS licensed is preserved
194	    verbatim. Pure; deterministic. The neutral connective itself is never matched (it asserts no
195	    relation), so over-application is impossible."""
196	    def _swap(m: "re.Match") -> str:
197	        phrase = m.group("phrase").strip().lower()
198	        relation = _ANALYTICAL_CONNECTIVE_RELATION.get(phrase, "")
199	        if relation and relation in licensed_relations:
200	            return m.group(0)  # licensed -> keep verbatim
201	        return f"; {_NEUTRAL_CONNECTIVE_PHRASE}, "  # unlicensed -> neutralize
202	    return _ANALYTICAL_CONNECTIVE_RE.sub(_swap, sentence)
203	
204	
205	def contains_relational_quantifier(sentence: str) -> bool:
206	    """True iff ``sentence`` carries a detectable aggregate/relational quantifier (lexicon hit).
207	
208	    Detection-only — used by the harness + the producer to decide whether the guard needs to act.
209	    A sentence with NO aggregate predicate returns False and is returned unchanged by the repair."""
210	    text = sentence or ""
211	    return bool(
212	        _LEADING_ATTRIBUTION_RE.search(text)
213	        or _CONSENSUS_LEADING_RE.search(text)
214	        or _INLINE_QUANTIFIER_RE.search(text)
215	        or _N_OF_M_RE.search(text)
216	        or _COMPARATIVE_RE.search(text)
217	    )
218	
219	
220	def _basket_licenses_quantifier(basket: Any) -> bool:
221	    """True iff the basket's consensus state LICENSES a relational quantifier (design §2.3):
222	      (a) ZERO refuters / ContradictionEdge among its members (an unrefuted claim), AND
223	      (b) the independence-weighted agreement (share of SUPPORTS members) is >= the env threshold.
224	
225	    Conservative on missing data: an empty / unreadable basket does NOT license (returns False ⇒ the
226	    quantifier is stripped). UNDER-relax is safe; over-relax is lethal."""
227	    if basket is None:
228	        return False
229	    # (a) refuters / contradiction references mark the basket contested -> NEVER license a consensus word.
230	    refuters = getattr(basket, "refuter_cluster_ids", None) or ()
231	    try:
232	        if len(tuple(refuters)) > 0:
233	            return False
234	    except TypeError:
235	        return False
236	    verdict = str(getattr(basket, "basket_verdict", "") or "").strip().lower()
237	    if verdict == "contested":
238	        return False
239	    # (b) agreement = SUPPORTS share of the basket's members (independence proxy). A relational
240	    # quantifier asserts the WHOLE basket agrees; a basket with any non-SUPPORTS member is below 1.0.
241	    members = list(getattr(basket, "supporting_members", None) or [])
242	    if not members:
243	        return False
244	    supports = sum(1 for m in members
245	                   if str(getattr(m, "span_verdict", "") or "").upper() == "SUPPORTS")
246	    agreement = supports / float(len(members))
247	    return agreement >= _agreement_min()
248	
249	
250	def _strip_provenance_tokens(sentence: str) -> str:
251	    """The sentence with its ``[#ev:...]`` provenance token(s) removed + whitespace normalized — the
252	    bare prose to compare against the source span. Pure."""
253	    bare = _EV_TOKEN_RE.sub("", sentence or "")
254	    return re.sub(r"\s+", " ", bare).strip()
255	
256	
257	def _basket_source_spans(basket: Any) -> list[str]:
258	    """The whitespace-normalized ``direct_quote`` span text of every SUPPORTS member of the basket —
259	    the verbatim source words the writer may legitimately quote. Used to tell a SOURCE-WRITTEN quantifier
260	    (must be preserved) from a SYNTHESIZED one (must be stripped). Pure; empty list on a memberless
261	    basket."""
262	    spans: list[str] = []
263	    for m in (getattr(basket, "supporting_members", None) or []):
264	        if str(getattr(m, "span_verdict", "") or "").upper() != "SUPPORTS":
265	            continue
266	        quote = str(getattr(m, "direct_quote", "") or "")
267	        norm = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", quote).strip()
268	        if norm:
269	            spans.append(norm)
270	    return spans
271	
272	
273	def _is_verbatim_source_text(sentence: str, basket: Any) -> bool:
274	    """True iff ``sentence`` (sans provenance tokens + terminal punctuation) is a VERBATIM substring of
275	    one of the basket's SUPPORTS member spans — i.e. the writer QUOTED the source rather than synthesized.
276	
277	    A quantifier inside such a quotation ("Most of the surveyed firms ...") is the SOURCE's own word, NOT
278	    a fabricated aggregate — the guard MUST NOT strip it (that would misquote the source). The default
279	    production writer ``build_short_member_sentence`` returns exactly such a verbatim span, so this check
280	    is what keeps the guard off the source's words on the default path. Case-insensitive on the prose, but
281	    only after token-stripping — the span comparison is on text the source actually wrote. Pure."""
282	    bare = _strip_provenance_tokens(sentence)
283	    # Drop a single trailing terminal so "X." matches a source span "X" (the producer adds the period).
284	    if bare[-1:] in ".!?":
285	        bare = bare[:-1].rstrip()
286	    if not bare:
287	        return False
288	    bare_low = bare.lower()
289	    for span in _basket_source_spans(basket):
290	        if bare_low in span.lower():
291	            return True
292	    return False
293	
294	
295	def _recapitalize(text: str) -> str:
296	    """Capitalize the FIRST alphabetic character of ``text`` (after a leading clause was stripped, the
297	    residue must read as a proper sentence). No-op on empty / non-alpha-leading text."""
298	    s = (text or "").lstrip()
299	    for i, ch in enumerate(s):
300	        if ch.isalpha():
301	            return s[:i] + ch.upper() + s[i + 1:]
302	        if ch.isalnum():
303	            return s  # leading digit — nothing to capitalize
304	    return s
305	
306	
307	def _strip_quantifiers(sentence: str) -> str:
308	    """Remove every detected aggregate/relational predicate from ``sentence`` (the conservative repair).
309	
310	    LEADING attribution clauses ("Most studies show that ...") are removed and the residue
311	    re-capitalized; INLINE quantifiers ("most", "the majority of", "consistently") are removed in place;
312	    "n of m studies" counts are removed. The ``[#ev:...]`` provenance tokens are NEVER touched, so the
313	    residue re-passes the UNCHANGED strict_verify. Whitespace is normalized. Pure."""
314	    text = sentence or ""
315	    # 1) Leading attribution / consensus clauses (strip then re-capitalize the residue).
316	    leading_stripped = False
317	    m = _LEADING_ATTRIBUTION_RE.search(text)
318	    if m and m.start() == 0:
319	        text = text[m.end():]
320	        leading_stripped = True
321	    else:
322	        m = _CONSENSUS_LEADING_RE.search(text)
323	        if m and m.start() == 0:
324	            text = text[m.end():]
325	            leading_stripped = True
326	    # 2) Inline quantifiers + n-of-m counts (remove in place; iterate to convergence for stacked hits).
327	    prev = None
328	    while prev != text:
329	        prev = text
330	        text = _INLINE_QUANTIFIER_RE.sub("", text)
331	        text = _N_OF_M_RE.sub("", text)
332	    # 3) Normalize whitespace introduced by removals (keep token spacing intact).
333	    text = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", text).strip()
334	    # 4) Re-capitalize when a leading clause was removed (the residue is now the sentence head). Also
335	    #    capitalize when an inline leading quantifier ("Most studies ...") left a lowercased head.
336	    if leading_stripped or (sentence[:1].isupper() and text[:1].islower()):
337	        text = _recapitalize(text)
338	    return text
339	
340	
341	def guard_relational_quantifier(
342	    sentence: str,
343	    basket: Any,
344	    *,
345	    licensed_relations: "set[str] | None" = None,
346	) -> Optional[str]:
347	    """The guard: given a candidate sentence + its supporting basket, return the sentence with any
348	    UNLICENSED relational quantifier STRIPPED (faithful residue), else the sentence unchanged.
349	
350	    Decision:
351	      * No detectable quantifier  -> return ``sentence`` unchanged (the common, fast path).
352	      * Quantifier present AND the basket LICENSES it (zero refuters, agreement >= threshold) -> KEEP
353	        (return unchanged). [Increment-1 producers never emit one, so this branch is rarely taken.]
354	      * Quantifier present AND NOT licensed -> STRIP it, returning the faithful residue (its provenance
355	        tokens intact, so the caller's UNCHANGED strict_verify re-passes it).
356	
357	    I-deepfix-001 M6 — ``licensed_relations`` (cross-source analytical path): when supplied (a set of
358	    engine-licensed relation keys, e.g. ``{"conflict"}``), the sentence is a COMPOSED analytical
359	    sentence whose two atoms were ALREADY individually guarded/verified when built; the guard's ONLY job
360	    here is to NEUTRALIZE any analytical connective whose relation was NOT licensed (a wrong "in
361	    contrast" -> "; separately, "). It does NOT re-run the per-clause aggregate strip on the multi-basket
362	    sentence (that would mis-handle a verbatim comparative inside an atom). ``licensed_relations=None``
363	    (default) is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the legacy single-clause behavior.
364	
365	    Returns ``None`` ONLY when stripping leaves NO content-bearing residue (the sentence was nothing but
366	    an aggregate predicate with no span-grounded claim) — the caller then drops that unit (an aggregate
367	    predicate with no underlying span is not a faithful claim to render). A residue that still carries a
368	    provenance token is ALWAYS returned (never annihilated). This module NEVER asserts the residue is
369	    verified — the caller re-runs the UNCHANGED strict_verify."""
370	    text = sentence or ""
371	    if not text.strip():
372	        return None
373	    if licensed_relations is not None:
374	        # Cross-source analytical sentence: license-check the connective only (atoms already guarded).
375	        neutralized = _neutralize_unlicensed_connectives(text, set(licensed_relations))
376	        return neutralized if neutralized.strip() else None
377	    if not contains_relational_quantifier(text):
378	        return text
379	    # VERBATIM SOURCE TEXT is faithful-by-construction: a quantifier the SOURCE itself wrote (the candidate
380	    # is a substring of one of the basket's member spans) is a QUOTATION, never a fabricated aggregate.
381	    # NEVER strip it — that would misquote the source (the lethal direction). The default production writer
382	    # ``build_short_member_sentence`` returns exactly such a verbatim span, so this is the load-bearing
383	    # check that keeps the guard off the source's own words. Only SYNTHESIZED prose (absent from every
384	    # member span) reaches the strip/drop branches below.
385	    if _is_verbatim_source_text(text, basket):
386	        return text
387	    if _basket_licenses_quantifier(basket):
388	        # Genuinely licensed by the whole-basket consensus state — keep as composed (the engine still
389	        # gates the underlying facts; the quantifier is the disclosed consensus state).
390	        return text
391	    # A SYNTHESIZED comparative ("X higher than Y") cannot be cleanly word-deleted without mangling the
392	    # sentence; full comparative rewrite is DEFERRED. DROP the synthesized clause (return None) so the
393	    # caller falls back to the member's VERBATIM K-span — under-relax is safe. (A comparative inside a
394	    # verbatim span never reaches the guard: the producer only guards synthesized writer output.)
395	    if _COMPARATIVE_RE.search(text):
396	        return None
397	    repaired = _strip_quantifiers(text)
398	    # Never annihilate a span-grounded claim: if a provenance token survives, return the residue. Only a
399	    # residue with NO token left (a pure predicate, no underlying span) returns None (caller drops it).
400	    if not repaired or not _EV_TOKEN_RE.search(repaired):
401	        return None
402	    return repaired
403

1	"""SURE-RAG-style three-way per-citation relevance judge (I-beatboth-003, #1280).
2	
3	WHY (helps both boards). ``strict_verify`` (provenance_generator) is relevance-BLIND:
4	invariant #3's ``>=2-content-word`` overlap (``PG_PROVENANCE_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP``) PASSES
5	a source that shares two incidental words without establishing the required RELATION —
6	the "off-topic-but-topical" case (right entity named, wrong relation). These off-topic
7	citations spike DeepTRACE citation-imprecision AND dilute DeepResearch-Bench-II relevance.
8	
9	WHAT this module is. A thin, INJECTABLE, three-way per-citation judge that runs ALONGSIDE
10	strict_verify (never replacing it, never an input to ``is_verified`` or the six strict_verify
11	checks). It returns one of three LABELS per (sentence, cited-span) pair:
12	
13	  * SUPPORTED   — the span establishes the required relation for the claim -> keep as a
14	                  support citation.
15	  * INSUFFICIENT — the span mentions the right entity WITHOUT establishing the required
16	                  relation -> DEMOTE the citation to "listed, not load-bearing"
17	                  (provenance_generator render layer drops it from the inline support set).
18	  * REFUTED     — the span CONTRADICTS the claim -> route to a contradiction flag (the
19	                  citation is removed from the support set; the sentence still ships).
20	
21	CRITICAL faithfulness invariants (CLAUDE.md §-1.3 / §-1.4 + the #1280 brief):
22	
23	  1. ALWAYS-RELEASE. This is a per-citation LABEL, NEVER a hold / abstain. SURE-RAG natively
24	     ABSTAINS below threshold; POLARIS does NOT import the abstain. The report ALWAYS ships.
25	  2. NEVER strand a statement uncited (minimum-retention). This module only LABELS; the
26	     provenance_generator render layer enforces the minimum-retention guard (demote only if
27	     >=1 support citation remains, else keep the citation + mark the statement weak).
28	  3. The faithfulness ENGINE (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role D8 / span-grounding) is the ONLY
29	     hard gate and is NEVER touched here. The relevance label is a NEW added dimension.
30	  4. Default-OFF + byte-identical when off. ``relevance_gate_enabled()`` defaults False; with
31	     the flag OFF the judge is NEVER instantiated and NO LLM call is made.
32	  5. Judge model + threshold env-configurable (LAW VI). Default GLM-5.2 via OpenRouter.
33	  6. Fail-loud means the HARNESS / acceptance fails on a silent no-op (the effect must FIRE
34	     in the real output) — it does NOT mean runtime report suppression. On a judge TRANSPORT
35	     / parse error at runtime the judge returns SUPPORTED (keep the cite the strict_verify gate
36	     already passed) + a reason, so always-release holds (a runtime error never demotes or
37	     holds; it only declines to add the relevance dimension for that one citation).
38	
39	The judge is INJECTABLE via a callable ``relevance_judge_fn(claim, span) -> (label, reason)``
40	so the §-1.4 fail-loud replay harness can mock it deterministically with NO model spend.
41	"""
42	
43	from __future__ import annotations
44	
45	import json
46	import logging
47	import os
48	import time
49	from typing import Callable, Optional
50	
51	# I-deepfix-001 (§9.1.8 anti-starvation, Codex diff-gate iter1 P0): this is the FOURTH live glm side
52	# judge (the W2 content-relevance escalation is DEFAULT-ON and run_gate_b force-enables it). GLM ignores
53	# reasoning.effort, so a provider that runs reasoning long can eat the whole max_tokens budget and blank
54	# the verdict. The shared leaf helper puts the PROVEN D8-Mirror NUMERIC reasoning cap
55	# (reasoning_cap << max_tokens) on a glm judge body; a non-glm model (e.g. a PG_RELEVANCE_MODEL=kimi
56	# override) keeps its byte-identical {effort} shape. Stdlib-light leaf import.
57	from src.polaris_graph.llm.judge_reasoning_block import build_judge_reasoning_block as _build_reasoning_block
58	
59	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.relevance_judge")

61	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
62	# Public label taxonomy (NOT the NLI ENTAILED/NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED taxonomy —
63	# this is the SURE-RAG relevance/answerability taxonomy, deliberately distinct).
64	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
65	LABEL_SUPPORTED = "SUPPORTED"
66	LABEL_INSUFFICIENT = "INSUFFICIENT"
67	LABEL_REFUTED = "REFUTED"
68	_VALID_LABELS = frozenset({LABEL_SUPPORTED, LABEL_INSUFFICIENT, LABEL_REFUTED})

70	# The signature of an injectable judge: (claim_text, cited_span_text) -> (label, reason).
71	RelevanceJudgeFn = Callable[[str, str], "tuple[str, str]"]

74	# Env knobs (LAW VI — zero hard-coding). Read at call time so tests / the harness
75	# can toggle without re-import.
76	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
77	_ENV_FLAG = "PG_RELEVANCE_GATE"  # master ON/OFF; default OFF -> byte-identical
78	_ENV_MODEL = "PG_RELEVANCE_MODEL"  # judge model; default GLM-5.2 via OpenRouter
79	_ENV_REASONING_EFFORT = "PG_RELEVANCE_REASONING_EFFORT"
80	_ENV_MAX_TOKENS = "PG_RELEVANCE_MAX_TOKENS"
81	_ENV_TIMEOUT_S = "PG_RELEVANCE_TIMEOUT_S"

83	# call; under all-GLM the generator IS GLM too, so check_family_segregation WOULD raise. The
84	# campaign deliberately overrides §9.1.1 (MASTER_PLAN all-GLM-5.2 single-family lock). This
85	# flag rides the SAME documented override rather than a new silent bypass: when set, the
86	# relevance judge SKIPS the family-segregation guard. Default 0 -> guard stays ON.
87	_ENV_ALLOW_SAME_FAMILY = "PG_RELEVANCE_ALLOW_SAME_FAMILY"

91	# before any LIVE run; the harness mocks the judge so the slug is not load-bearing here.
92	_DEFAULT_MODEL = "z-ai/glm-5.2"
93	_DEFAULT_REASONING_EFFORT = "high"
94	# I-deepfix-001 (§9.1.8 anti-starvation, Codex diff-gate iter1 P0): the OLD default 256 STARVED the
95	# glm-5.2 reasoning judge — the reasoning burst ate the whole 256-token budget so message.content came
96	# back EMPTY (finish_reason=length). This judge fires by default via the W2 content-relevance escalation
97	# (PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE default-ON, force-enabled at run_gate_b.py:1364), so the starvation hit
98	# every paid run. §9.1.8 "max_tokens ALWAYS go to the model REAL max": glm-5.2's mirror-chain MIN
99	# completion cap is 131072 — the SAME real cap the credibility / entailment / semantic-conflict side
100	# judges resolved from a LIVE OpenRouter read 2026-06-14 (_CREDIBILITY_MAX_TOKENS_CHAIN_MIN). max_tokens
101	# is a CAP billed by ACTUAL usage (~a few hundred tokens for a relevance verdict), so a generous cap is
102	# free insurance, never a pre-allocated spend. Env-overridable per LAW VI (PG_RELEVANCE_MAX_TOKENS). The
103	# numeric reasoning cap (build_judge_reasoning_block) then keeps the reasoning burst strictly below this
104	# so the JSON verdict always lands.
105	_DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS = 131072
106	_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_S = 30.0

108	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
109	# I-deepfix-001 Item-12 (DNS resilience). The W2 content-relevance escalation is DEFAULT-ON and
110	# force-enabled by run_gate_b, so this per-citation judge fires on every paid run. A TRANSIENT DNS /
111	# name-resolution blip (getaddrinfo "Temporary failure in name resolution" -> httpx.ConnectError)
112	# previously fell straight through to the always-release SUPPORTED keep on the FIRST hit, silently
113	# dropping the relevance dimension for that citation (Item-12: ~5x/run, ~25s wasted each). Add a
114	# BOUNDED retry-with-backoff for DNS / transient-CONNECT faults ONLY. A parse / empty /
115	# unparseable-verdict / HTTP-status fault keeps the byte-identical single-attempt always-release path;
116	# a BudgetExceededError still propagates (never retried, never masked).
117	#
118	# FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL (§9.1 the faithfulness ENGINE is the only hard gate; §-1.3 weight-not-filter):
119	# the relevance label is NEVER the faithfulness gate — it runs ALONGSIDE strict_verify on cites that
120	# ALREADY cleared the hard gate, and its only downstream effect is to DEMOTE a citation's weight
121	# (never DROP the source). On retry exhaustion the SAME always-release SUPPORTED keep fires (existing
122	# behavior, unchanged). So the retry can only RECOVER a real verdict on a transient blip (which may
123	# DEMOTE = strengthen) or be a no-op; it never relaxes faithfulness and never drops a credible source.
124	# Env-driven per LAW VI (read at call time so the harness can toggle without re-import).
125	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
126	_ENV_DNS_RETRY_ATTEMPTS = "PG_RELEVANCE_DNS_RETRY_ATTEMPTS"       # extra retries after the first attempt
127	_ENV_DNS_RETRY_BACKOFF_S = "PG_RELEVANCE_DNS_RETRY_BACKOFF_S"     # base backoff seconds (exponential)
128	_ENV_DNS_RETRY_BACKOFF_CAP_S = "PG_RELEVANCE_DNS_RETRY_BACKOFF_CAP_S"  # per-wait ceiling seconds
129	_DEFAULT_DNS_RETRY_ATTEMPTS = 2       # -> up to 3 total attempts (the task's "2-3x")
130	_DEFAULT_DNS_RETRY_BACKOFF_S = 1.0    # short backoff: 1s, 2s, ... (exponential, capped)
131	_DEFAULT_DNS_RETRY_BACKOFF_CAP_S = 10.0
132	
133	# DNS / name-resolution / transient-connection markers (host-agnostic, lowercase substrings). Mirrors
134	# the connectivity subset of entailment_judge._RATE_LIMIT_CONNECTIVITY_MARKERS. A getaddrinfo failure
135	# surfaces as httpx.ConnectError wrapping socket.gaierror; the string markers catch the same fault when
136	# it arrives inside a differently-typed exception (e.g. a wrapped/re-raised transport error).
137	_DNS_CONNECT_MARKERS: tuple[str, ...] = (
138	    "getaddrinfo",
139	    "temporary failure in name resolution",
140	    "name or service not known",
141	    "nodename nor servname",
142	    "name resolution",
143	    "failed to resolve",
144	)

146	
147	def _is_dns_or_transient_connect_error(exc: BaseException) -> bool:
148	    """True iff ``exc`` is a DNS / name-resolution / transient-CONNECT transport fault a bounded retry
149	    can heal — ``httpx.ConnectError`` / ``httpx.ConnectTimeout`` (a getaddrinfo failure surfaces here), a
150	    raw ``socket.gaierror``, or any exception whose type/string carries a DNS marker. A parse / empty /
151	    HTTP-status / read-timeout / bad-verdict fault returns False, so the caller keeps its single-attempt
152	    always-release path for those. Never raises (a predicate must never break the judge)."""
153	    try:
154	        import httpx  # local import: keep the OFF-path import cost zero
155	        if isinstance(exc, (httpx.ConnectError, httpx.ConnectTimeout)):
156	            return True
157	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — httpx import/type checks must never break the predicate
158	        pass
159	    try:
160	        import socket
161	        if isinstance(exc, socket.gaierror):
162	            return True
163	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
164	        pass
165	    try:
166	        haystack = f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}".lower()
167	        return any(marker in haystack for marker in _DNS_CONNECT_MARKERS)
168	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
169	        return False

172	def relevance_gate_enabled() -> bool:
173	    """True iff the SURE-RAG relevance gate is ON (``PG_RELEVANCE_GATE`` truthy).
174	
175	    Default OFF -> the per-citation labeling block is skipped entirely and the verify ->
176	    render path is byte-identical to HEAD. Read at call time (no import-time capture) so the
177	    harness can flip it per-case."""
178	    v = os.getenv(_ENV_FLAG, "0").strip().lower()
179	    return v in ("1", "true", "yes", "on", "enabled")

184	    return v in ("1", "true", "yes", "on", "enabled")

187	def normalize_label(raw: object) -> Optional[str]:
188	    """Coerce a judge's raw verdict string to one of the three canonical labels, or None
189	    when it is unrecognizable (the caller then treats it as a judge error -> SUPPORTED keep).
190	
191	    Accepts case-insensitive Supported/Insufficient/Refuted plus a few obvious synonyms
192	    seen from reasoning models (e.g. "support", "irrelevant", "contradicted")."""
193	    if raw is None:
194	        return None
195	    s = str(raw).strip().upper()
196	    if not s:
197	        return None
198	    if s in _VALID_LABELS:
199	        return s
200	    if s.startswith("SUPPORT"):
201	        return LABEL_SUPPORTED
202	    if s.startswith("INSUFFICIENT") or s.startswith("IRRELEVANT") or s.startswith("OFF"):
203	        return LABEL_INSUFFICIENT
204	    if s.startswith("REFUT") or s.startswith("CONTRADICT"):
205	        return LABEL_REFUTED
206	    return None

210	# The relevance prompt — SURE-RAG taxonomy, distinct from the NLI entailment prompt.
211	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
212	_RELEVANCE_PROMPT = (
213	    "You are a strict citation-relevance judge. You are given a CLAIM and a SPAN of source "
214	    "text that is cited to support that claim. Decide whether the SPAN establishes the "
215	    "specific RELATION the CLAIM asserts.\n\n"
216	    "Return EXACTLY one label:\n"
217	    "- SUPPORTED: the SPAN establishes the relation the CLAIM asserts (a conservative "
218	    "paraphrase counts).\n"
219	    "- INSUFFICIENT: the SPAN mentions the right entity or topic but does NOT establish the "
220	    "required relation (right entity, wrong relation — off-topic-but-topical).\n"
221	    "- REFUTED: the SPAN contradicts the CLAIM.\n\n"
222	    "CLAIM:\n{claim}\n\nSPAN:\n{span}\n\n"
223	    'Respond with ONLY a JSON object: {{"label": "SUPPORTED|INSUFFICIENT|REFUTED", '
224	    '"reason": "<one short sentence>"}}'
225	)

228	# is a DIFFERENT task from the citation-claim prompt above: at the post-fetch /
229	# pre-tier seam there are NO generated claims yet, only the research QUESTION, so
230	# the judge must decide whether a fetched passage is RELEVANT EVIDENCE for the
231	# question — NOT whether a span supports a claim. It MUST mirror the Qwen3-
232	# Reranker's question-passage semantics (the reranker asks "does the Document
233	# meet the Query") so the two stages judge the SAME thing and escalation is
234	# coherent. Reuses the SAME SUPPORTED/INSUFFICIENT/REFUTED label vocabulary so
235	# the W2 caller's mapping is unchanged (SUPPORTED=keep, INSUFFICIENT/REFUTED=
236	# demote). The {claim} key carries the research QUESTION; {span} carries the
237	# fetched passage (the format keys are reused for transport compatibility).
238	_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_PROMPT = (
239	    "You are a strict content-relevance judge for a research pipeline. You are "
240	    "given a research QUESTION and a PASSAGE of fetched source text. Decide "
241	    "whether the PASSAGE contains information that is RELEVANT EVIDENCE for "
242	    "answering the QUESTION.\n\n"
243	    "Return EXACTLY one label:\n"
244	    "- SUPPORTED: the PASSAGE contains substantive information that helps answer "
245	    "the QUESTION (facts, findings, data, or authoritative statements on the "
246	    "question's topic — a partial but on-point answer counts).\n"
247	    "- INSUFFICIENT: the PASSAGE is on the general topic but does NOT contain "
248	    "information that answers the QUESTION (topical-but-useless: navigation/"
249	    "boilerplate/marketing, a generic background sentence, or the right subject "
250	    "with no answering content).\n"
251	    "- REFUTED: the PASSAGE is off-topic / unrelated to the QUESTION, or is "
252	    "non-content chrome (cookie notice, login wall, ads).\n\n"
253	    "QUESTION:\n{claim}\n\nPASSAGE:\n{span}\n\n"
254	    'Respond with ONLY a JSON object: {{"label": "SUPPORTED|INSUFFICIENT|REFUTED", '
255	    '"reason": "<one short sentence>"}}'
256	)

258	
259	def make_content_relevance_judge() -> "_RelevanceJudge":
260	    """Build a `_RelevanceJudge` wired with the content-relevance (question<->
261	    passage) prompt for I-wire-001 W2. Reuses the tested transport / budget
262	    ledger / family override / fail-to-KEEP path; only the prompt differs. NOT
263	    gated by PG_RELEVANCE_GATE (W2 is gated solely by PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE)."""
264	    return _RelevanceJudge(prompt_template=_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_PROMPT)
265	
266	
267	def _extract_first_json_object(content: object) -> dict:
268	    """Pull the first ``{...}`` JSON object out of a model response that may prepend
269	    reasoning text. Mirrors entailment_judge._extract_first_json_object intent."""
270	    text = content if isinstance(content, str) else str(content or "")
271	    start = text.find("{")
272	    if start < 0:
273	        return {}
274	    depth = 0
275	    for i in range(start, len(text)):
276	        c = text[i]
277	        if c == "{":
278	            depth += 1
279	        elif c == "}":
280	            depth -= 1
281	            if depth == 0:
282	                try:
283	                    obj = json.loads(text[start:i + 1])
284	                    return obj if isinstance(obj, dict) else {}
285	                except (ValueError, TypeError):
286	                    return {}
287	    return {}
288

290	class _RelevanceJudge:
291	    """Synchronous httpx wrapper around an OpenRouter relevance call.
292	
293	    Lazy-initialized + only ever constructed when ``relevance_gate_enabled()`` is True AND
294	    no judge fn was injected, so import-time / OFF-path cost is zero. Single-attempt + simple
295	    by design (this is a per-citation LABEL, not the hard gate); a runtime transport/parse
296	    fault returns SUPPORTED (keep the strict_verify-passed cite) so always-release holds."""
297	
298	    def __init__(self, prompt_template: str | None = None) -> None:
299	        # I-wire-001 W2 (#1311): an OPTIONAL prompt template lets a DIFFERENT
300	        # relevance task reuse this judge's tested transport (httpx client, budget
301	        # ledger, family-segregation override, fail-to-KEEP-on-error). Default =
302	        # the #1280 citation-claim prompt, so the existing path is BYTE-IDENTICAL.
303	        # The template must accept {claim} and {span} format keys.
304	        self._prompt_template = prompt_template or _RELEVANCE_PROMPT
305	        api_key = os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY", "").strip()
306	        if not api_key:
307	            raise RuntimeError(
308	                f"{_ENV_FLAG} requires OPENROUTER_API_KEY (the relevance judge calls OpenRouter)"
309	            )
310	        self._api_key = api_key
311	        base_url = os.environ.get(
312	            "OPENROUTER_BASE_URL", "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1"
313	        ).rstrip("/")
314	        self._endpoint = f"{base_url}/chat/completions"
315	        self._model = os.environ.get(_ENV_MODEL, _DEFAULT_MODEL)
316	        # Two-family invariant (§9.1.1): the relevance judge is an evaluator-family call. Under
317	        # the all-GLM-5.2 campaign override the generator IS GLM too, so the guard WOULD raise.
318	        # Ride the SAME documented override (PG_RELEVANCE_ALLOW_SAME_FAMILY) — never a silent
319	        # bypass. Default: the guard runs and fails loud on an un-overridden same-family config.
320	        if not _allow_same_family():
321	            from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (  # noqa: PLC0415
322	                check_family_segregation,
323	            )
324	            check_family_segregation(evaluator_model=self._model)
325	        else:
326	            logger.info(
327	                "[relevance] %s set: skipping check_family_segregation under the "
328	                "all-GLM single-family campaign override", _ENV_ALLOW_SAME_FAMILY,
329	            )
330	        try:
331	            self._timeout_s = float(os.environ.get(_ENV_TIMEOUT_S, _DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_S))
332	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
333	            self._timeout_s = _DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_S
334	        self._client = self._build_client()
335	
336	    def _build_client(self):
337	        import httpx  # local import: avoid forcing the dep on the OFF path
338	
339	        from src.utils.shared_ssl_context import get_shared_ssl_context
340	        return httpx.Client(
341	            verify=get_shared_ssl_context(),
342	            timeout=httpx.Timeout(self._timeout_s),
343	        )
344	
345	    def _dns_retry_attempts(self) -> int:
346	        """I-deepfix-001 Item-12: extra retries after the first attempt on a DNS / transient-connect
347	        fault (env-driven per LAW VI; read at call time). A bad value falls back to the default."""
348	        try:
349	            return max(0, int(os.environ.get(_ENV_DNS_RETRY_ATTEMPTS, _DEFAULT_DNS_RETRY_ATTEMPTS)))
350	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
351	            return _DEFAULT_DNS_RETRY_ATTEMPTS
352	
353	    def _dns_retry_wait_s(self, attempt: int) -> float:
354	        """I-deepfix-001 Item-12: exponential short backoff (base * 2**attempt) capped at the ceiling
355	        (env-driven per LAW VI). Returns >= 0.0; a bad env value falls back to the defaults."""
356	        try:
357	            base = float(os.environ.get(_ENV_DNS_RETRY_BACKOFF_S, _DEFAULT_DNS_RETRY_BACKOFF_S))
358	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
359	            base = _DEFAULT_DNS_RETRY_BACKOFF_S
360	        try:
361	            cap = float(os.environ.get(_ENV_DNS_RETRY_BACKOFF_CAP_S, _DEFAULT_DNS_RETRY_BACKOFF_CAP_S))
362	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
363	            cap = _DEFAULT_DNS_RETRY_BACKOFF_CAP_S
364	        return max(0.0, min(base * (2 ** attempt), cap))
365	
366	    def judge(self, claim: str, span: str) -> "tuple[str, str]":
367	        """Return (label, reason). label is one of SUPPORTED/INSUFFICIENT/REFUTED.
368	
369	        On any transport/parse/empty fault -> ("SUPPORTED", "judge_error: <reason>"): the
370	        citation already cleared the strict_verify hard gate, so declining to add the
371	        relevance dimension keeps the pre-existing verified state (always-release; never a
372	        runtime demotion or hold on a judge error)."""
373	        prompt = self._prompt_template.format(claim=claim, span=span)
374	        effort = (os.environ.get(_ENV_REASONING_EFFORT, "").strip().lower()
375	                  or _DEFAULT_REASONING_EFFORT)
376	        if effort not in ("low", "medium", "high", "xhigh"):
377	            effort = _DEFAULT_REASONING_EFFORT
378	        try:
379	            max_tokens = max(64, int(os.environ.get(_ENV_MAX_TOKENS, _DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS)
380	                                     or _DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS))
381	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
382	            max_tokens = _DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS
383	        json_body = {
384	            "model": self._model,
385	            "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
386	            "temperature": 0.0,
387	            "max_tokens": max_tokens,
388	            # I-deepfix-001 (§9.1.8): a glm judge gets a NUMERIC reasoning cap (reasoning_cap << max_tokens)
389	            # so a provider that runs reasoning long cannot blank the verdict; a non-glm model (e.g. a
390	            # PG_RELEVANCE_MODEL=kimi override) keeps the {effort} shape. Reasoning stays ON either way.
391	            "reasoning": _build_reasoning_block(self._model, effort, max_tokens),
392	        }
393	        headers = {
394	            "Authorization": f"Bearer {self._api_key}",
395	            "Content-Type": "application/json",
396	        }
397	        started = time.monotonic()
398	        # I-deepfix-001 Item-12 (DNS resilience): a bounded SAME-endpoint retry-with-backoff around the
399	        # POST+parse. ONLY a DNS / transient-connect fault is retried (a getaddrinfo blip that would
400	        # otherwise waste ~25s and silently drop the relevance dimension); every OTHER fault keeps the
401	        # byte-identical single-attempt always-release path, and BudgetExceededError propagates. On retry
402	        # exhaustion the SAME always-release SUPPORTED keep fires — faithfulness-neutral (this label is
403	        # never the hard gate; it only DEMOTES weight on an already-verified cite, never drops a source).
404	        attempts = self._dns_retry_attempts()
405	        last_exc: Optional[BaseException] = None
406	        for attempt in range(attempts + 1):
407	            try:
408	                response = self._client.post(self._endpoint, headers=headers, json=json_body)
409	                response.raise_for_status()
410	                data = response.json()
411	                # Record judge spend against the per-run budget cap + cost ledger, mirroring the
412	                # entailment judge. BudgetExceededError propagates (a cap breach aborts cleanly).
413	                try:
414	                    from src.polaris_graph.llm import openrouter_client as _orc  # noqa: PLC0415
415	                    _usage = (data or {}).get("usage") or {}
416	                    _cost = _usage.get("cost")
417	                    if _cost is not None:
418	                        _orc._add_run_cost(float(_cost))
419	                        _orc.check_run_budget(0)
420	                except ImportError:
421	                    pass
422	                choices = (data or {}).get("choices") or []
423	                content = ""
424	                if choices:
425	                    content = ((choices[0] or {}).get("message") or {}).get("content") or ""
426	                obj = _extract_first_json_object(content)
427	                label = normalize_label(obj.get("label"))
428	                reason = str(obj.get("reason") or "")[:200]
429	                if label is None:
430	                    return (LABEL_SUPPORTED, f"judge_error: unparseable_verdict:{content[:80]!r}")
431	                return (label, reason)
432	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail to SUPPORTED (keep cite), never hold
433	                from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (  # noqa: PLC0415
434	                    BudgetExceededError,
435	                )
436	                if isinstance(exc, BudgetExceededError):
437	                    raise
438	                last_exc = exc
439	                # Retry ONLY a DNS / transient-connect blip; a parse / empty / HTTP-status / bad-verdict
440	                # fault keeps the single-attempt always-release path (byte-identical to pre-fix).
441	                if attempt < attempts and _is_dns_or_transient_connect_error(exc):
442	                    wait = self._dns_retry_wait_s(attempt)
443	                    logger.warning(
444	                        "[relevance] DNS/transient-connect fault -> retry %d/%d in %.1fs: %s",
445	                        attempt + 1, attempts, wait, str(exc)[:120],
446	                    )
447	                    if wait > 0:
448	                        time.sleep(wait)
449	                    continue
450	                dur_ms = int((time.monotonic() - started) * 1000)
451	                logger.warning(
452	                    "[relevance] judge transport/parse fault (%dms) -> SUPPORTED keep: %s",
453	                    dur_ms, exc,
454	                )
455	                return (LABEL_SUPPORTED, f"judge_error: {type(exc).__name__}:{str(exc)[:120]}")
456	        # Unreachable in the current flow (every loop iteration returns or continues, and the final
457	        # attempt's except returns), but keep a fail-open-KEEP backstop so a future refactor can never
458	        # strand the caller on a runtime fault. §-1.3 always-release preserved.
459	        dur_ms = int((time.monotonic() - started) * 1000)
460	        logger.warning(
461	            "[relevance] judge retry loop exhausted (%dms) -> SUPPORTED keep: %s",
462	            dur_ms, last_exc,
463	        )
464	        return (
465	            LABEL_SUPPORTED,
466	            f"judge_error: {type(last_exc).__name__ if last_exc else 'unknown'}:{str(last_exc)[:120]}",
467	        )
468	
469	
470	_JUDGE_SINGLETON: Optional[_RelevanceJudge] = None
471	
472	
473	def _get_judge() -> _RelevanceJudge:
474	    """Process-lifetime singleton, built lazily on first use (only when the gate is ON and no
475	    judge fn was injected). Never constructed on the OFF path."""
476	    global _JUDGE_SINGLETON
477	    if _JUDGE_SINGLETON is None:
478	        _JUDGE_SINGLETON = _RelevanceJudge()
479	    return _JUDGE_SINGLETON
480	
481	
482	def reset_judge_singleton() -> None:
483	    """Test/harness hook: drop the cached judge so a re-config takes effect."""
484	    global _JUDGE_SINGLETON
485	    _JUDGE_SINGLETON = None
486	
487	
488	def judge_citation_relevance(
489	    claim: str,
490	    span: str,
491	    *,
492	    relevance_judge_fn: Optional[RelevanceJudgeFn] = None,
493	) -> "tuple[str, str]":
494	    """Label one (claim, cited-span) pair SUPPORTED / INSUFFICIENT / REFUTED.
495	
496	    ``relevance_judge_fn`` (injectable, default None) lets the §-1.4 fail-loud harness mock
497	    the judge deterministically (NO model spend). When None, the live OpenRouter GLM-5.2 judge
498	    is used. The returned label is ALWAYS one of the three canonical labels (an unrecognizable
499	    verdict from an injected fn is coerced to SUPPORTED + a judge_error reason — keep the cite,
500	    never strand) so the caller never has to handle an out-of-taxonomy value."""
501	    if relevance_judge_fn is not None:
502	        try:
503	            raw_label, reason = relevance_judge_fn(claim, span)
504	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — an injected fn fault keeps the cite
505	            return (LABEL_SUPPORTED, f"judge_error: injected_fn:{type(exc).__name__}")
506	        label = normalize_label(raw_label)
507	        if label is None:
508	            return (LABEL_SUPPORTED, f"judge_error: injected_unparseable:{raw_label!r}")
509	        return (label, str(reason or "")[:200])
510	    return _get_judge().judge(claim, span)
511

1	"""Render-only cosmetic repetition dedup for the final assembled ``report.md``.
2	
3	This pass runs at the VERY END of the sweep, on the on-disk ``report.md``
4	markdown TEXT, AFTER ``strict_verify`` / NLI / 4-role D8 / provenance /
5	span-grounding and ALL faithfulness accounting are complete. It operates on the
6	output markdown STRING only. It NEVER touches any faithfulness-engine input or
7	carrier — not ``SectionResult``, not ``kept_sentences_pre_resolve``, not
8	``verification.tokens``, not the evidence pool. Because it runs strictly
9	downstream of the faithfulness engine and reads/writes only the rendered bytes,
10	it CANNOT change any verdict, count, or verified flag.
11	
12	Per CLAUDE.md §-1.3, repetition is a QUALITY concern, NOT a faithfulness
13	concern. The good machinery (basket consolidation, strict_verify, D8) is left
14	untouched; this is a purely presentational last-mile clean-up of the rendered
15	artifact.
16	
17	Behaviour (high-precision, fail-open):
18	  * A prose sentence in an ordinary body section that is a VERBATIM
19	    (citation-stripped, emphasis-stripped, whitespace/case-normalized)
20	    duplicate of an EARLIER-rendered body sentence is dropped at its later
21	    occurrence. Any ``[N]`` citation markers the dropped copy carried that the
22	    kept copy lacks are MERGED into the kept copy's visible text, so NO source
23	    is ever lost (§-1.3 CONSOLIDATE, never a hard drop).
24	  * FRONT summary sections (Abstract, Key Findings, Executive Summary, ...) are
25	    EXEMPT from the cross-section dedup: their sentences are never registered as
26	    the global kept first occurrence and are never dropped for matching a body
27	    sentence. The front "## Key Findings" block extracts body sentences verbatim,
28	    so a keep-first dedup that registered them would gut the body — exemption
29	    prevents that.
30	  * BACK-TO-BACK collapse (I-deepfix-001 A3): a prose sentence that is a verbatim
31	    duplicate of the IMMEDIATELY-PRECEDING eligible sentence in the SAME section
32	    is dropped at its later occurrence — and this fires in EVERY section,
33	    INCLUDING the exempt front summaries. This catches the same placeholder /
34	    claim sentence emitted 2-3x in a row (e.g. a redaction-gap notice repeated
35	    back-to-back inside "## Key Findings"), which the cross-section dedup cannot
36	    touch there. Only an adjacent identical sentence collapses, so a summary's
37	    single re-presentation of a body sentence is left intact. Citations are
38	    merged into the kept copy exactly as in the cross-section path.
39	  * A section is NEVER emptied: if every eligible prose sentence in a section
40	    was dropped as a duplicate, its first such sentence is restored (fail-open).
41	  * Section headings (``#``..``######``), tables, code fences, block quotes,
42	    horizontal rules, blank lines and the References/Bibliography section are
43	    passed through byte-identical. ``references_mode`` is PER-SECTION (I-deepfix-001
44	    A3 P1): a References/Bibliography/Sources heading byte-preserves ONLY its own
45	    section (so numbered ``[N]`` entries never collapse and orphan a body marker),
46	    and the NEXT non-references heading — e.g. the appended "## Source corroboration
47	    (per claim)" prose block — EXITS byte-preserve so the back-to-back collapse can
48	    reach it. A ``back_matter`` latch (True from the first references heading onward)
49	    keeps every back-matter section EXEMPT from the CROSS-SECTION dedup, exactly like
50	    the front summaries: back matter intentionally re-presents body claim sentences
51	    (the per-claim corroboration headers), so it must never be gutted — but the A3
52	    back-to-back collapse still fires there.
53	  * Short / near-empty sentences are never deduped (fail-open precision guard).
54	
55	When the report contains no eligible duplicate, the input is returned
56	byte-for-byte unchanged (round-trip invariant). Pure function.
57	"""
58	from __future__ import annotations
59	
60	import os
61	import re
62	
63	# Numeric citation markers as they appear in a FINAL rendered report.md:
64	# ``[12]``, ``[12, 13]``, ``[12-14]``. Used both to strip citations before
65	# comparing sentence text AND to merge citations across duplicate copies.
66	_RENDER_CITATION_RE = re.compile(r"\[\d+(?:\s*[,;–-]\s*\d+)*\]")
67	
68	# Broader marker set used ONLY for the normalization key, so a residual
69	# provenance token (``[#ev:...]`` / ``[CITE]``) never blocks a genuine
70	# text-duplicate match. Mirrors the assemble-stage _CITATION_MARKER_RE.
71	_ANY_MARKER_RE = re.compile(
72	    r"\[#?ev:[^\]]*\]|\[\d+(?:\s*[,;–-]\s*\d+)*\]|\[CITE\]",
73	    re.IGNORECASE,
74	)
75	
76	_WHITESPACE_RUN_RE = re.compile(r"\s+")
77	_EMPHASIS_RE = re.compile(r"[*_`]+")
78	_HEADER_RE = re.compile(r"^\s*#{1,6}\s")
79	_HORIZONTAL_RULE_RE = re.compile(r"^\s*([-*_])(?:\s*\1){2,}\s*$")
80	_LEADING_BULLET_RE = re.compile(r"^(?:[-*+]\s+|\d+[.)]\s+)")
81	
82	# Headings whose bodies are INTENTIONAL verbatim re-presentations of body
83	# sentences (a summary repeats the body — that is the point). Matched liberally
84	# by prefix because OVER-exempting is fail-open (just less dedup), while
85	# UNDER-exempting risks gutting the body from a front summary.
86	_EXEMPT_TITLE_PREFIXES = (
87	    "abstract",
88	    "key finding",
89	    "executive summary",
90	    "summary",
91	    "key takeaway",
92	    "highlights",
93	    "tl;dr",
94	    "tldr",
95	)
96	
97	_REFERENCES_HEADER_RE = re.compile(
98	    r"^\s*#{1,6}\s+.*\b(references|bibliography|sources|works cited|citations)\b",
99	    re.IGNORECASE,
100	)
101	
102	# I-deepfix-001 wave-2 (PG_DEDUP_REFHEADER_STRICT, default OFF): a STANDALONE
103	# references-section heading whose text is EXACTLY a references keyword (optionally
104	# surrounded by whitespace) — e.g. "## References", "## Bibliography", "### Sources".
105	# Anchored ``^...$`` so a heading that merely CONTAINS a keyword does NOT match. The
106	# loose _REFERENCES_HEADER_RE above matches ANY heading containing the keyword,
107	# INCLUDING a long level-1 document TITLE that says "...specific literature sources...";
108	# that false-positive latches back_matter=True at heading 1 and globally exempts the
109	# WHOLE report from cross-section dedup. This anchored pattern gates ONLY the
110	# back_matter latch when the flag is ON, so a long title no longer disables body dedup.
111	_REFERENCES_SECTION_STANDALONE_RE = re.compile(
112	    r"^\s*#{1,6}\s*(?:references|bibliography|sources|works cited|citations)\s*$",
113	    re.IGNORECASE,
114	)
115	
116	
117	def _refheader_strict_enabled() -> bool:
118	    """True iff PG_DEDUP_REFHEADER_STRICT is set to an explicit truthy value.
119	    Default (unset / empty / 0 / false / no / off) is OFF => byte-identical: the
120	    back_matter latch keeps its legacy loose-regex behaviour when this is OFF."""
121	    return os.getenv("PG_DEDUP_REFHEADER_STRICT", "").strip().lower() in (
122	        "1",
123	        "true",
124	        "yes",
125	        "on",
126	    )
127	
128	
129	def _refheader_max_heading_len() -> int:
130	    """Max heading-text length (chars) that may latch back_matter under strict mode.
131	    A real references-section heading is a few words ("References", "References and
132	    Notes", "7. References", "Cited Sources"); the false-positive is a 1104-char level-1
133	    TITLE that merely CONTAINS "...literature sources...". Default 80 comfortably admits
134	    every real variant while rejecting the long title. Env PG_DEDUP_REFHEADER_MAX_LEN."""
135	    try:
136	        n = int(os.getenv("PG_DEDUP_REFHEADER_MAX_LEN", "80"))
137	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
138	        return 80
139	    return n if n > 0 else 80
140	
141	
142	# Fail-open precision guard: never dedup a sentence with fewer than this many
143	# alphanumeric content words. A genuine repeated claim sentence is long.
144	MIN_CONTENT_WORDS = 5
145	
146	# Trailing sentence-terminal punctuation (with optional closing quotes/brackets),
147	# used to insert merged citation markers BEFORE the period rather than after it.
148	_TRAILING_TERMINAL_RE = re.compile(r"[.!?]+[\"'\]\)]*\s*$")
149	
150	
151	def _normalize_sentence(sentence: str) -> str:
152	    """Citation/emphasis/whitespace/case-normalized key for duplicate matching."""
153	    text = _ANY_MARKER_RE.sub("", sentence or "")
154	    text = _EMPHASIS_RE.sub("", text)
155	    text = _WHITESPACE_RUN_RE.sub(" ", text).strip().lower()
156	    text = _LEADING_BULLET_RE.sub("", text)
157	    return text.strip(" \t.-–—:;,")
158	
159	
160	def _normalize_heading_title(line: str) -> str:
161	    """Lower-cased heading text with the leading ``#`` run and markers removed."""
162	    text = line.strip().lstrip("#").strip()
163	    text = _ANY_MARKER_RE.sub("", text)
164	    text = _EMPHASIS_RE.sub("", text)
165	    return _WHITESPACE_RUN_RE.sub(" ", text).strip().lower()
166	
167	
168	def _is_exempt_title(title: str) -> bool:
169	    return any(title.startswith(prefix) for prefix in _EXEMPT_TITLE_PREFIXES)
170	
171	
172	def _dedup_eligible(normalized_key: str) -> bool:
173	    if not normalized_key:
174	        return False
175	    words = [w for w in normalized_key.split() if any(c.isalnum() for c in w)]
176	    return len(words) >= MIN_CONTENT_WORDS
177	
178	
179	def _cite_numbers(text: str) -> list:
180	    """Individual numeric citation ids present in ``text`` (order-preserving)."""
181	    numbers = []
182	    for marker in _RENDER_CITATION_RE.finditer(text or ""):
183	        for digits in re.findall(r"\d+", marker.group()):
184	            numbers.append(int(digits))
185	    return numbers
186	
187	
188	def _merge_citations(kept_slot: dict, dropped_sentence: str) -> bool:
189	    """Append to the kept copy any citation ids the dropped copy carried but the
190	    kept copy lacks. Guarantees NO source is lost when a duplicate is removed.
191	    Returns True iff at least one new citation id was merged in."""
192	    kept_numbers = set(_cite_numbers(kept_slot["text"]))
193	    missing = [n for n in _cite_numbers(dropped_sentence) if n not in kept_numbers]
194	    if not missing:
195	        return False
196	    added = "".join(f"[{n}]" for n in missing)
197	    # I-deepfix-001 i-fix: insert the merged citation markers BEFORE any trailing
198	    # terminal punctuation so the kept copy reads "...guidelines [3][7]." — never a
199	    # malformed "...guidelines [3].[7]" with a marker stranded after the period.
200	    text = kept_slot["text"]
201	    stripped = text.rstrip()
202	    trailing = text[len(stripped):]
203	    match = _TRAILING_TERMINAL_RE.search(stripped)
204	    if match:
205	        kept_slot["text"] = stripped[: match.start()] + added + stripped[match.start():] + trailing
206	    else:
207	        kept_slot["text"] = stripped + added + trailing
208	    return True
209	
210	
211	def _split_sentences(line: str) -> list:
212	    """Split a prose line into ``(sentence, trailing_separator)`` pairs whose
213	    concatenation reproduces ``line`` byte-for-byte.
214	
215	    A boundary is a terminal ``.``/``!``/``?`` — optionally followed by closing
216	    quotes/brackets and inline citation markers that belong to the sentence —
217	    then a run of horizontal whitespace (assigned to the separator). This keeps
218	    trailing ``[N]`` citations attached to their sentence and lets a no-drop
219	    line round-trip exactly."""
220	    results = []
221	    length = len(line)
222	    index = 0
223	    start = 0
224	    while index < length:
225	        if line[index] in ".!?":
226	            cursor = index + 1
227	            # Consume closing quotes/brackets + citation markers glued to the
228	            # terminal punctuation ("claim.[12]" / 'claim."').
229	            while cursor < length:
230	                char = line[cursor]
231	                if char in ')"\'’”':
232	                    cursor += 1
233	                    continue
234	                if char == "[":
235	                    close = line.find("]", cursor)
236	                    if close != -1:
237	                        cursor = close + 1
238	                        continue
239	                break
240	            whitespace_end = cursor
241	            while whitespace_end < length and line[whitespace_end] in " \t":
242	                whitespace_end += 1
243	            if whitespace_end > cursor:  # whitespace follows -> real boundary
244	                results.append((line[start:cursor], line[cursor:whitespace_end]))
245	                start = whitespace_end
246	                index = whitespace_end
247	                continue
248	        index += 1
249	    if start < length:
250	        results.append((line[start:], ""))
251	    if not results:  # empty line
252	        results.append((line, ""))
253	    return results
254	
255	
256	def dedup_rendered_report_markdown(report_md: str) -> str:
257	    """Return ``report_md`` with later verbatim-duplicate body sentences dropped
258	    (citations merged into the kept copy). Pure; see module docstring."""
259	    if not report_md or not isinstance(report_md, str):
260	        return report_md
261	
262	    lines = report_md.split("\n")
263	    items = []  # {"kind": "raw", "text"} | {"kind": "prose", "slots": [...]}
264	    seen = {}  # normalized_key -> kept sentence slot (cross-section, non-exempt)
265	    section_prose_slots = {}  # section_id -> [all prose slots]
266	    # I-deepfix-001 A3: normalized_key of the most recent KEPT eligible sentence
267	    # in the current section, plus its kept slot. Used for the back-to-back
268	    # collapse that fires in EVERY section, incl. exempt front summaries.
269	    section_last_eligible = {}  # section_id -> (normalized_key, kept_slot)
270	
271	    in_code_fence = False
272	    references_mode = False
273	    # I-deepfix-001 A3 P1: latched True from the FIRST references/bibliography/sources
274	    # heading onward. Back matter (the numbered bibliography, the per-claim "Source
275	    # corroboration" block, the disclosed-source lists) is EXEMPT from the cross-section
276	    # dedup — it re-presents body claim sentences on purpose and must never be gutted —
277	    # while STILL getting the A3 back-to-back collapse in its prose sub-blocks.
278	    back_matter = False
279	    exempt_section = False
280	    section_id = 0
281	    refheader_strict = _refheader_strict_enabled()
282	
283	    for line in lines:
284	        stripped = line.strip()
285	
286	        # Code fences: toggle and pass everything inside through untouched.
287	        if stripped.startswith("```") or stripped.startswith("~~~"):
288	            in_code_fence = not in_code_fence
289	            items.append({"kind": "raw", "text": line})
290	            continue
291	        if in_code_fence:
292	            items.append({"kind": "raw", "text": line})
293	            continue
294	
295	        # Headings define sections and set the exemption state.
296	        if _HEADER_RE.match(line):
297	            section_id += 1
298	            # I-deepfix-001 A3 P1: references_mode is now re-evaluated PER heading (not
299	            # latched forever). A references/bibliography/sources heading byte-preserves
300	            # its OWN section — protecting the numbered ``[N]`` entries — but the next
301	            # non-references heading (e.g. "## Source corroboration (per claim)") exits
302	            # byte-preserve so the A3 back-to-back collapse can reach that prose block.
303	            is_references_heading = bool(_REFERENCES_HEADER_RE.match(line))
304	            # I-deepfix-001 wave-2 (PG_DEDUP_REFHEADER_STRICT, default OFF): the back_matter
305	            # latch is the dominant blocker — under the OFF (legacy) path a long level-1 TITLE
306	            # merely CONTAINING "...literature sources..." loose-matches _REFERENCES_HEADER_RE and
307	            # latches back_matter=True at heading 1, globally exempting the entire report from the
308	            # cross-section dedup. When the flag is ON, the latch additionally requires a STANDALONE
309	            # short references-section heading (heading text IS the keyword, e.g. "## Bibliography"),
310	            # so the document title no longer disables body dedup while a real references heading still
311	            # latches. ``references_mode`` (the per-section byte-preserve protecting numbered ``[N]``
312	            # entries) is deliberately left on the loose regex, so OFF stays byte-identical and no
313	            # genuine references list ever loses its verbatim byte-preserve.
314	            if is_references_heading:
315	                # Latch under strict mode iff the heading is a real (short) references
316	                # heading — an exact standalone keyword OR any references-keyword heading
317	                # whose text is short. This admits every real variant ("## References and
318	                # Notes", "## 7. References", "## Cited Sources") while rejecting the long
319	                # level-1 title (Codex+Fable P2: length guard > exact-keyword anchor).
320	                heading_text = line.lstrip("#").strip()
321	                if (
322	                    not refheader_strict
323	                    or bool(_REFERENCES_SECTION_STANDALONE_RE.match(line))
324	                    or len(heading_text) <= _refheader_max_heading_len()
325	                ):
326	                    back_matter = True
327	            references_mode = is_references_heading
328	            # A back-matter section (corroboration / disclosure lists) is EXEMPT from the
329	            # cross-section dedup for the SAME reason a front summary is: it re-presents
330	            # body claim sentences by design. references_mode is subsumed by back_matter
331	            # here (a references section is always back matter); it is left in the OR only
332	            # for clarity — a references line never reaches the prose path below anyway.
333	            exempt_section = (
334	                references_mode
335	                or back_matter
336	                or _is_exempt_title(_normalize_heading_title(line))
337	            )
338	            items.append({"kind": "raw", "text": line})
339	            continue
340
