341	        # A References/Bibliography/Sources section is byte-preserved verbatim (protects
342	        # the numbered ``[N]`` entries). Non-references back-matter prose (the per-claim
343	        # corroboration block) falls through to the dedup path below — exempt from the
344	        # cross-section pass but still eligible for the A3 back-to-back collapse.
345	        if references_mode:
346	            items.append({"kind": "raw", "text": line})
347	            continue
348	
349	        # Structural / non-prose lines pass through byte-identical.
350	        if (
351	            not stripped
352	            or stripped.startswith("|")
353	            or stripped.startswith(">")
354	            or _HORIZONTAL_RULE_RE.match(line)
355	        ):
356	            items.append({"kind": "raw", "text": line})
357	            continue
358	
359	        # Prose line: dedup at sentence granularity, rebuild THIS line only.
360	        slots = []
361	        for sentence, separator in _split_sentences(line):
362	            slot = {"text": sentence, "sep": separator, "kept": True}
363	            key = _normalize_sentence(sentence)
364	            if _dedup_eligible(key):
365	                dropped = False
366	                # (1) Cross-section verbatim dedup — non-exempt sections only. A
367	                # later body sentence that repeats an EARLIER-rendered body
368	                # sentence is dropped; front summaries are exempt so they may
369	                # legitimately re-present a body sentence.
370	                if not exempt_section and key in seen:
371	                    slot["contributed_merge"] = _merge_citations(seen[key], sentence)
372	                    slot["kept"] = False
373	                    dropped = True
374	                # (2) I-deepfix-001 A3: back-to-back collapse WITHIN a section —
375	                # fires in EVERY section, INCLUDING exempt front summaries. An
376	                # eligible sentence identical to the immediately-preceding kept
377	                # eligible sentence in the same section is dropped (its citations
378	                # merged into that kept copy). Only an ADJACENT identical sentence
379	                # collapses, so distinct sentences and a summary's single
380	                # re-presentation of a body sentence are untouched.
381	                elif section_id in section_last_eligible:
382	                    prev_key, prev_slot = section_last_eligible[section_id]
383	                    if prev_key == key:
384	                        slot["contributed_merge"] = _merge_citations(prev_slot, sentence)
385	                        slot["kept"] = False
386	                        dropped = True
387	                if not dropped:
388	                    if not exempt_section:
389	                        seen[key] = slot
390	                    section_last_eligible[section_id] = (key, slot)
391	            # I-deepfix-001 i-fix: register EVERY prose sentence for the fail-open
392	            # guard, not only dedup-eligible ones. A short/ineligible sentence
393	            # (never dropped, always kept) keeps its section non-empty, so a
394	            # dropped duplicate that was the section's only LONG sentence is NOT
395	            # wrongly restored when real content still survives beside it.
396	            section_prose_slots.setdefault(section_id, []).append(slot)
397	            slots.append(slot)
398	        items.append({"kind": "prose", "slots": slots})
399	
400	    # Fail-open guard against a bare heading: if EVERY prose slot in a section was
401	    # dropped, restore its first slot so the section is not left as an empty heading.
402	    # But a slot whose drop MERGED a new citation into the kept copy elsewhere is NOT
403	    # restored — its unique information is already preserved, and restoring it would
404	    # re-introduce a near-duplicate that differs only by a citation already merged.
405	    # So we restore only a PURE-identical duplicate (contributed no new citation).
406	    for slots in section_prose_slots.values():
407	        if not slots or any(slot["kept"] for slot in slots):
408	            continue
409	        for slot in slots:
410	            if not slot.get("contributed_merge"):
411	                slot["kept"] = True
412	                break
413	
414	    out_lines = []
415	    for item in items:
416	        if item["kind"] == "raw":
417	            out_lines.append(item["text"])
418	        else:
419	            out_lines.append(
420	                "".join(
421	                    slot["text"] + slot["sep"] for slot in item["slots"] if slot["kept"]
422	                )
423	            )
424	    return "\n".join(out_lines)
425

1	"""Closed ARCHETYPE registry + pure report-shape helpers (GENERALIZED Fix 4).
2	
3	This module holds the deterministic, closed archetype table and the three pure
4	functions that shape a report's block ORDER + framing heading. It is the
5	generalized replacement for the journal-literal ``litreview`` shaping that landed
6	at ``d44ee36`` (hardcoded ``## Introduction and Scope`` + a fixed
7	sections->depth->KF->biblio order).
8	
9	Design constraints (from GATE_GENERALIZE_FIX45_PLAN.md §2):
10	  * PURE. No I/O, no LLM, no ``provenance_generator`` import — nothing here can
11	    reach the frozen faithfulness engine. The functions arrange OPAQUE block
12	    strings (they never read or re-compose a verified sentence).
13	  * CLOSED table, not a generative skeleton (3/3 reviewer convergence). A kind
14	    that matches no synonym is preserved VERBATIM as an opaque term and falls
15	    back to the least-wrong universal ``review`` shape (disclosed as an
16	    assumption by the caller) — never dropped, never invented.
17	  * Only two things vary per archetype: the Key-Findings POSITION and the
18	    framing HEADING title. The machinery set is archetype-invariant EXCEPT that
19	    ``methods`` stays in the scored body when a ``contract.sections`` requirement
20	    matches it (the systematic-review / PRISMA carve-out).
21	  * ``order_report_blocks`` is a pure PERMUTATION of the render blocks it is
22	    handed — count-invariant, nothing deleted, interiors byte-identical.
23	
24	NO ``journal`` / ``review`` / ``Introduction and Scope`` LITERAL appears in
25	control flow here — those live only as DATA in ``ARCHETYPES`` / ``KIND_SYNONYMS``
26	(the anti-hardcode grep test enforces this).
27	"""
28	
29	from __future__ import annotations
30	
31	from dataclasses import dataclass
32	from typing import Any
33	
34	
35	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
36	# The closed archetype table (DATA, not control flow)
37	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
38	
39	
40	@dataclass(frozen=True)
41	class Archetype:
42	    """One report shape. ``framing_title`` is the ``## {title}`` heading emitted
43	    over the claim-free framing paragraph (empty => no framing section, e.g. a
44	    BLUF memo). ``kf_position`` is where the Key-Findings recap sits relative to
45	    the thematic body: ``lead`` (BLUF, before framing), ``after_framing``
46	    (default review shape), or ``tail`` (after the synthesis). ``kf_title`` is the
47	    Key-Findings HEADER label (chrome only — bullets are byte-identical across
48	    archetypes)."""
49	
50	    key: str
51	    framing_title: str
52	    kf_position: str  # "lead" | "after_framing" | "tail"
53	    kf_title: str = "Key Findings"
54	
55	
56	# key -> Archetype. ``review`` is the default (== the landed shape for a review
57	# contract, so an ON-path review run is byte-identical to d44ee36).
58	ARCHETYPES: dict[str, Archetype] = {
59	    "review": Archetype("review", "Introduction and Scope", "after_framing", "Key Findings"),
60	    "systematic_review": Archetype(
61	        "systematic_review", "Introduction and Scope", "after_framing", "Key Findings"
62	    ),
63	    "memo": Archetype("memo", "", "lead", "Bottom Line"),
64	    "brief": Archetype("brief", "Executive Summary", "lead", "Key Findings"),
65	    "comparison": Archetype("comparison", "Scope and Criteria", "after_framing", "Key Findings"),
66	    "explainer": Archetype("explainer", "Overview", "tail", "Key Findings"),
67	}
68	
69	# normalized deliverable.kind value -> archetype key. Same synonym-table pattern
70	# as retrieval_projection._resolve_facet_id (LAW VI: no new vocabulary; longest
71	# matching synonym wins; an unmapped value stays opaque and falls back to
72	# DEFAULT_ARCHETYPE). Keys are lower-cased at resolution time.
73	KIND_SYNONYMS: dict[str, str] = {
74	    "literature review": "review",
75	    "lit review": "review",
76	    "survey": "review",
77	    "review": "review",
78	    "systematic review": "systematic_review",
79	    "systematic literature review": "systematic_review",
80	    "meta-analysis": "systematic_review",
81	    "memo": "memo",
82	    "decision memo": "memo",
83	    "briefing memo": "memo",
84	    "brief": "brief",
85	    "policy brief": "brief",
86	    "executive brief": "brief",
87	    "comparison": "comparison",
88	    "comparative analysis": "comparison",
89	    "market scan": "comparison",
90	    "explainer": "explainer",
91	    "primer": "explainer",
92	    "overview": "explainer",
93	}
94	
95	DEFAULT_ARCHETYPE = "review"  # least-wrong universal shape; disclosed when assumed
96	
97	
98	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
99	# COMPOSE DIRECTIVES (DATA, not control flow) — the per-archetype doc-type
100	# framing the section writer's system prompt reads as an ADDITIVE preamble.
101	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
102	# Keyed by the SAME archetype key as ``ARCHETYPES`` (resolved via
103	# ``KIND_SYNONYMS``). Each string is DIRECTIVE-ONLY prose: it frames HOW the
104	# deliverable reads (structure/voice/scope emphasis), states NO fact, cites NO
105	# source, carries NO digit and NO ``ev_`` id, and asks for NO heading. It is a
106	# SUFFIX on the frozen section system prompt — never a gate, never a truncation.
107	# An unmapped kind resolves to ``DEFAULT_ARCHETYPE`` (disclosed by the caller);
108	# the raw kind is preserved verbatim in the projection. NO per-kind literal
109	# lives in control flow — only in this table (the anti-hardcode grep enforces it).
110	COMPOSE_DIRECTIVES: dict[str, str] = {
111	    "review": (
112	        "This is a literature review. Organize the verified findings by theme and "
113	        "synthesize across sources; foreground scope and how the evidence base fits "
114	        "together, not a chronology of individual studies."
115	    ),
116	    "systematic_review": (
117	        "This is a systematic review. Present the review question, then the evidence "
118	        "synthesis by theme, then the limitations of the evidence base; be methodical "
119	        "and explicit about coverage and gaps without asserting any procedure the "
120	        "evidence does not support."
121	    ),
122	    "memo": (
123	        "This is a decision memo. Lead bottom-line-first: state the upshot for the "
124	        "decision up front, then the options and their tradeoffs, in a direct, "
125	        "decision-oriented voice."
126	    ),
127	    "brief": (
128	        "This is a brief. Open with an executive summary and keep the prose concise "
129	        "and implication-focused for a time-constrained reader."
130	    ),
131	    "comparison": (
132	        "This is a comparison. Set out the scope and comparison criteria, then treat "
133	        "each option symmetrically across the same dimensions so the contrast is even-"
134	        "handed."
135	    ),
136	    "explainer": (
137	        "This is an explainer. Give an overview first, then build up the concepts and "
138	        "mechanisms in an accessible, explanatory voice for a non-expert reader."
139	    ),
140	}
141	
142	
143	def _norm(s: Any) -> str:
144	    return str(s or "").strip().lower()
145	
146	
147	def resolve_archetype_key(kind_value: Any) -> "tuple[str, bool, str]":
148	    """Resolve a raw ``deliverable.kind`` VALUE string onto an archetype KEY.
149	
150	    Returns ``(key, assumed, opaque_kind)`` mirroring :func:`resolve_archetype`
151	    but reading a bare kind string instead of a contract (so a projection that
152	    already carries ``doc_type`` need not reconstruct a contract). ``assumed`` is
153	    ``True`` when the default key was used (empty or unmapped kind); ``opaque_kind``
154	    is the verbatim unmapped kind (``""`` otherwise). PURE. Same longest-synonym-
155	    wins matching as :func:`resolve_archetype` — no new vocabulary, no per-kind
156	    branch."""
157	    raw = str(kind_value or "").strip()
158	    v = _norm(raw)
159	    if not v:
160	        return DEFAULT_ARCHETYPE, True, ""
161	    best_key = ""
162	    best_len = -1
163	    for syn, key in KIND_SYNONYMS.items():
164	        synl = syn.strip().lower()
165	        if not synl:
166	            continue
167	        if synl in v or v in synl:
168	            if len(synl) > best_len:
169	                best_key = key
170	                best_len = len(synl)
171	    if best_key and best_key in ARCHETYPES:
172	        return best_key, False, ""
173	    return DEFAULT_ARCHETYPE, True, raw
174	
175	
176	def compose_doc_type_directive(kind_value: Any) -> str:
177	    """The per-archetype doc-type framing directive for a raw ``deliverable.kind``
178	    VALUE (``""`` when the kind is empty).
179	
180	    Resolves ``kind_value`` -> archetype key (via :func:`resolve_archetype_key`)
181	    and returns the matching :data:`COMPOSE_DIRECTIVES` string. An EMPTY kind
182	    yields ``""`` (inert => byte-identical). An UNMAPPED kind falls back to the
183	    default archetype's directive (the caller discloses the assumption). DIRECTIVE
184	    ONLY: no fact, no digit, no ``ev_`` id, no heading. PURE."""
185	    if not str(kind_value or "").strip():
186	        return ""
187	    key, _assumed, _opaque = resolve_archetype_key(kind_value)
188	    return COMPOSE_DIRECTIVES.get(key, COMPOSE_DIRECTIVES[DEFAULT_ARCHETYPE])
189	
190	
191	def compose_section_role(kind_value: Any, section_title: Any) -> str:
192	    """A short per-section ORIENTATION directive: how ``section_title`` serves a
193	    deliverable of the resolved kind. ``""`` when the kind or title is empty.
194	
195	    Generic + DATA-driven: it names the archetype (from :data:`ARCHETYPES`, keyed
196	    by the resolved key) and the section, with NO per-kind literal in control flow
197	    and NO per-section role table to maintain. It states the section's job in the
198	    deliverable's shape in one line so the writer orients this section — DIRECTIVE
199	    ONLY (no fact, no digit, no ``ev_`` id, no heading). PURE. Kept to a single
200	    line: every extra sentence is prompt-hash + verbosity surface."""
201	    kind = str(kind_value or "").strip()
202	    title = str(section_title or "").strip()
203	    if not kind or not title:
204	        return ""
205	    key, _assumed, _opaque = resolve_archetype_key(kind_value)
206	    arch = ARCHETYPES.get(key, ARCHETYPES[DEFAULT_ARCHETYPE])
207	    return (
208	        f'Compose the "{title}" section so it does its job within this {arch.key} '
209	        "deliverable's overall shape, consistent with the framing above."
210	    )
211	
212	
213	def _first_deliverable_kind_value(contract: Any) -> str:
214	    """Read the ``deliverable.kind`` VALUE from a ResearchContract.
215	
216	    ``contract.deliverable`` is a ``list[ContractTerm]`` (not a scalar). We take
217	    the value of the first term whose ``dimension`` ends in ``deliverable.kind``
218	    (or bare ``kind``). Returns ``""`` when absent — the caller then assumes the
219	    default archetype and discloses it. Tolerant of a ``None`` contract."""
220	    if contract is None:
221	        return ""
222	    terms = getattr(contract, "deliverable", None) or []
223	    for term in terms:
224	        dim = _norm(getattr(term, "dimension", ""))
225	        if dim.endswith("deliverable.kind") or dim.endswith(".kind") or dim == "kind":
226	            val = getattr(term, "value", None)
227	            if val is not None and str(val).strip():
228	                return str(val)
229	    return ""
230	
231	
232	def resolve_archetype(contract: Any) -> "tuple[Archetype, bool, str]":
233	    """Map ``contract.deliverable[kind]`` onto a closed :class:`Archetype`.
234	
235	    Returns ``(archetype, assumed, opaque_kind)``:
236	      * ``archetype`` — the resolved shape (never ``None``).
237	      * ``assumed`` — ``True`` when the default was used because the contract
238	        named no kind OR named a kind matching no synonym (the caller records an
239	        assumption-ledger disclosure).
240	      * ``opaque_kind`` — the VERBATIM unmapped kind string when a named kind
241	        resolved to no archetype (``""`` otherwise), so it can be preserved as an
242	        opaque term in the disclosure rather than silently dropped.
243	
244	    Uses the SAME synonym-resolution shape as ``_resolve_facet_id`` (longest
245	    matching synonym wins, substring both ways to tolerate plurals). A value
246	    matching no synonym is OPAQUE + falls back to the default (fail-open)."""
247	    raw = _first_deliverable_kind_value(contract)
248	    v = _norm(raw)
249	    if not v:
250	        return ARCHETYPES[DEFAULT_ARCHETYPE], True, ""
251	    best_key = ""
252	    best_len = -1
253	    for syn, key in KIND_SYNONYMS.items():
254	        synl = syn.strip().lower()
255	        if not synl:
256	            continue
257	        if synl in v or v in synl:
258	            if len(synl) > best_len:
259	                best_key = key
260	                best_len = len(synl)
261	    if best_key and best_key in ARCHETYPES:
262	        return ARCHETYPES[best_key], False, ""
263	    # Named a kind we do not recognize: preserve verbatim as opaque, fall back.
264	    return ARCHETYPES[DEFAULT_ARCHETYPE], True, raw
265	
266	
267	def build_framing_md(objective: str, archetype: Archetype) -> str:
268	    """A short CLAIM-FREE, CITATION-FREE framing paragraph under the archetype's
269	    ``## {framing_title}`` heading, derived from the contract OBJECTIVE. It
270	    asserts NO finding and carries NO ``[N]`` citation — pure framing, the same
271	    faithfulness class as the abort-path H1s — so it can never reach the
272	    faithfulness gate as an unverified claim.
273	
274	    Returns ``""`` when the objective is empty (no empty heading) OR when the
275	    archetype has an empty ``framing_title`` (a BLUF memo emits no framing
276	    section). PURE (no I/O). Template-only — no per-kind literal in control flow;
277	    the heading is DATA from the archetype.
278	
279	    The prose is emitted DIRECTLY UNDER the H1 (its own ``## {framing_title}``
280	    subheading follows the lead paragraph) so the H1 title is never orphan-dropped
281	    by ``dedup_identical_paragraphs`` (which drops a header whose next non-blank
282	    block is another header)."""
283	    obj = (objective or "").strip().rstrip(".")
284	    if not obj:
285	        return ""
286	    title = (archetype.framing_title or "").strip()
287	    if not title:
288	        return ""
289	    return (
290	        f"This report reviews the available evidence on {obj}. It synthesizes the findings that "
291	        "survived span-level verification, organized by theme; each cited claim is carried verbatim "
292	        "from a source span. Methods, source-hygiene disclosures, and the reliability audit are "
293	        "collected in the appendix at the end.\n\n"
294	        f"## {title}\n\n"
295	        f"Scope: this review is bounded to the question of {obj}. It reports only claims that passed "
296	        "span-level verification; unverified or off-topic material is excluded from the findings and "
297	        "disclosed in the appendix.\n\n"
298	    )
299	
300	
301	def contract_requires_section(contract: Any, section_key: str) -> bool:
302	    """True iff a ``SectionRequirement`` in ``contract.sections`` names the given
303	    section (case-insensitive substring match on its title value). Used for the
304	    2-line ``methods_is_machinery`` carve-out: a systematic-review PRISMA Methods
305	    is SCORED content and stays in the body. Tolerant of a ``None`` contract /
306	    missing sections (=> False). PURE."""
307	    if contract is None:
308	        return False
309	    key = _norm(section_key)
310	    if not key:
311	        return False
312	    for sec in getattr(contract, "sections", None) or []:
313	        title_term = getattr(sec, "title", None)
314	        title_val = _norm(getattr(title_term, "value", "") if title_term is not None else "")
315	        if not title_val:
316	            # A section id can stand in when the title term carries no value.
317	            title_val = _norm(getattr(sec, "section_id", ""))
318	        if title_val and key in title_val:
319	            return True
320	    return False
321	
322	
323	def order_report_blocks(
324	    archetype: Archetype,
325	    *,
326	    key_findings_md: str,
327	    sections_concat: str,
328	    depth_layer_md: str,
329	    methods_md: str,
330	    biblio_section_md: str,
331	    cwf_disclosed_md: str,
332	    drop_disclosure_md: str,
333	    methods_is_machinery: bool = True,
334	) -> "tuple[str, str]":
335	    """Arrange the EXISTING render blocks into the archetype's order and split the
336	    audit MACHINERY into a trailing appendix. Returns ``(scored_body,
337	    machinery_appendix)``.
338	
339	    Position of the Key-Findings recap follows ``archetype.kf_position``:
340	      * ``lead``          — KF opens the scored body (BLUF: memo / brief).
341	      * ``after_framing`` — KF after the thematic sections + synthesis (review).
342	      * ``tail``          — KF after the synthesis, before the bibliography
343	                            (explainer). (The framing paragraph itself is emitted
344	                            by the caller into the TITLE block, so ``after_framing``
345	                            here means "after the thematic body", matching the
346	                            landed review order.)
347	
348	    ``machinery_appendix`` = ``cwf_disclosed_md + drop_disclosure_md`` plus
349	    ``methods_md`` UNLESS ``methods_is_machinery`` is False (a required
350	    ``contract.sections`` Methods stays in the scored body). The caller folds the
351	    appendix under the single typed ``## Appendix`` boundary alongside the
352	    reliability header.
353	
354	    POSITION ONLY: every input block is placed EXACTLY ONCE across the two
355	    returned strings; NOTHING is dropped or edited. A render-time multiset
356	    assertion (in the caller) confirms permutation-only. PURE (no I/O)."""
357	    methods_in_body = "" if methods_is_machinery else methods_md
358	    methods_in_appendix = methods_md if methods_is_machinery else ""
359	
360	    if archetype.kf_position == "lead":
361	        scored_body = (
362	            key_findings_md
363	            + methods_in_body
364	            + sections_concat
365	            + depth_layer_md
366	            + biblio_section_md
367	        )
368	    elif archetype.kf_position == "tail":
369	        scored_body = (
370	            methods_in_body
371	            + sections_concat
372	            + depth_layer_md
373	            + key_findings_md
374	            + biblio_section_md
375	        )
376	    else:  # "after_framing" (default review shape == landed order)
377	        scored_body = (
378	            methods_in_body
379	            + sections_concat
380	            + depth_layer_md
381	            + key_findings_md
382	            + biblio_section_md
383	        )
384	
385	    machinery_appendix = methods_in_appendix + cwf_disclosed_md + drop_disclosure_md
386	    return scored_body, machinery_appendix
387

1	"""I-perm-021 (#1213) — RequiredEntityLedger: report-level required-entity completeness
2	accounting + honest gap disclosure (Codex design-gate APPROVE; narrow scope).
3	
4	The completeness gap is largely UPSTREAM (retrieval/extraction); a reduce-time/report-level
5	ledger CANNOT manufacture coverage. Its job, per the Codex design ruling, is the NARROW
6	"inclusion + disclosure" surface (NO second LLM generation / recovery round — that is a
7	deferred follow-up):
8	
9	  - Phase B (THIS module, report-level, POST-strict_verify): given the pre-registered native
10	    `required_entities` and the set of entity ids that earned a VERIFIED binding (the UNION of
11	    every VERIFIED claim's ``covered_element_ids`` from
12	    ``native_gate_b_inputs.build_native_gate_b_inputs`` — the EXACT same coverage computation,
13	    reused, never re-implemented), mark each required slot VERIFIED or GAP_DISCLOSED and emit
14	    an HONEST, DETERMINISTIC, TEMPLATED disclosure (no LLM, no fabricated citation).
15	
16	§-1.1 SAFETY: this module assigns NO verdicts and adds NO coverage CREDIT. VERIFIED is set
17	ONLY from the caller's already-computed verified-binding set (which is itself gated by
18	strict_verify + the 4-role ``verdict==VERIFIED`` rule). It never touches strict_verify / the
19	4-role evaluator / D8 / the release decision. A gap disclosure is a templated "we could not
20	verify X" statement — it NEVER fills the gap with an unsupported claim.
21	
22	Required entities come ONLY from the native scope template (the contamination lock — never
23	``outputs/dr_benchmark/`` / the gold rubric). The caller reads ``PG_REQUIRED_ENTITY_LEDGER``
24	at CALL TIME (default OFF, byte-identical when off); this pure module is simply not invoked
25	when the flag is off.
26	"""
27	from __future__ import annotations
28	
29	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
30	
31	# ── slot state machine ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
32	# (Phase A states RETRIEVED/MAPPED are produced by the pre-gen retrieval feed, a separate PR;
33	#  this narrow disclosure module uses only the post-verify VERIFIED / GAP_DISCLOSED transition.)
34	STATE_MISSING = "missing"
35	STATE_RETRIEVED = "retrieved"
36	STATE_MAPPED = "mapped"
37	STATE_VERIFIED = "verified"
38	STATE_INCLUDED = "included"
39	STATE_GAP_DISCLOSED = "gap_disclosed"
40	
41	# native required-entity dict keys (mirrors native_gate_b_inputs; read-only here).
42	_KEY_ID = "id"
43	_KEY_SEVERITY = "severity"
44	_KEY_ANCHOR = "anchor"
45	_KEY_RENDERING_SLOT = "rendering_slot"
46	_KEY_S0_CATEGORY = "s0_category"
47	# canonical identifier keys, in disclosure-preference order.
48	_CANONICAL_KEYS = ("doi", "pmid", "url_pattern")
49	
50	
51	@dataclass
52	class RequiredSlot:
53	    """One required entity's completeness state + the metadata needed to disclose it."""
54	    entity_id: str
55	    severity: str
56	    s0_category: str | None
57	    anchor: str
58	    rendering_slot: str
59	    canonical_ids: dict           # {doi?, pmid?, url_pattern?} (only present keys)
60	    state: str
61	    note: str = ""                # disclosure qualifier (e.g. url_mismatch); never a claim
62	
63	    @property
64	    def is_url_pattern_only(self) -> bool:
65	        """True iff url_pattern is the ENTITY's only canonical id (no doi/pmid). Such an
66	        entity is structurally un-flippable by alternate-URL retrieval — coverage requires the
67	        VERIFIED claim to cite the exact canonical url_pattern (Codex design-gate q7)."""
68	        return "url_pattern" in self.canonical_ids and not (
69	            self.canonical_ids.get("doi") or self.canonical_ids.get("pmid")
70	        )
71	
72	
73	# Disclosure qualifier (Codex design-gate q7): public-facing, NOT raw internal artifact wording.
74	_URL_MISMATCH_NOTE = (
75	    "content for this topic may exist on an alternate source, but coverage remains unmet "
76	    "unless a verified claim cites the canonical source for this required entity"
77	)
78	
79	
80	@dataclass
81	class RequiredEntityLedger:
82	    slots: list[RequiredSlot] = field(default_factory=list)
83	
84	    def verified_slots(self) -> list[RequiredSlot]:
85	        return [s for s in self.slots if s.state in (STATE_VERIFIED, STATE_INCLUDED)]
86	
87	    def gap_slots(self) -> list[RequiredSlot]:
88	        return [s for s in self.slots if s.state == STATE_GAP_DISCLOSED]
89	
90	    def coverage_fraction(self) -> float:
91	        return (len(self.verified_slots()) / len(self.slots)) if self.slots else 0.0
92	
93	    def to_evidence_gaps(self) -> list[dict]:
94	        """Structured gap records for the manifest (machine-readable, audit trail)."""
95	        return [
96	            {
97	                "entity_id": s.entity_id,
98	                "severity": s.severity,
99	                "s0_category": s.s0_category,
100	                "anchor": s.anchor,
101	                "rendering_slot": s.rendering_slot,
102	                "reason": "no_verified_citation",
103	                "note": s.note,
104	            }
105	            for s in self.gap_slots()
106	        ]
107	
108	
109	def verified_covered_ids(
110	    audit_map: dict, final_verdicts: dict[str, str] | None,
111	) -> set[str]:
112	    """The set of required-entity ids covered by a 4-role FINAL-VERIFIED claim.
113	
114	    Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1 (§-1.1): `four_role_claim_audit.json`'s per-claim
115	    ``covered_element_ids`` is the PRE-D8 builder audit map — it records what a claim WOULD
116	    cover if verified. But the 4-role seam can DOWNGRADE that claim (UNSUPPORTED / PARTIAL /
117	    FABRICATED), and D8 only credits coverage for a claim whose final verdict is VERIFIED. So
118	    the covered set MUST be filtered through ``final_verdicts[claim_id] == "VERIFIED"`` —
119	    otherwise a downgraded claim would falsely mark its entity covered and SUPPRESS a real
120	    Coverage-gaps disclosure (hiding a coverage gap = over-claiming completeness, the lethal
121	    direction). When ``final_verdicts`` is empty/None (e.g. a seam-timeout path) NO claim is
122	    treated as verified -> every required entity is disclosed as a gap (fail-safe over-disclose,
123	    never under-disclose)."""
124	    fv = final_verdicts or {}
125	    covered: set[str] = set()
126	    rows = audit_map.items() if isinstance(audit_map, dict) else []
127	    for claim_id, row in rows:
128	        if isinstance(row, dict) and fv.get(claim_id) == "VERIFIED":
129	            covered.update(row.get("covered_element_ids", []) or [])
130	    return covered
131	
132	
133	def _canonical_ids(entity: dict) -> dict:
134	    return {k: entity[k] for k in _CANONICAL_KEYS if entity.get(k) not in (None, "")}
135	
136	
137	def _norm_id(value: "str | None") -> str:
138	    """Lowercase + strip a canonical identifier for comparison. PURE."""
139	    return str(value or "").strip().lower()
140	
141	
142	def citation_covered_entity_ids(
143	    required_entities: list[dict],
144	    cited_evidence_records: "list[dict] | tuple[dict, ...] | None",
145	) -> set[str]:
146	    """I-deepfix-001 S2 (#1213/#1344): the set of required-entity ids covered by the CITED evidence
147	    of the report's VERIFIED claims. The four-role ``covered_element_ids`` path under-credits a
148	    required entity when the D8 builder never bound the entity to a claim's element ids even though
149	    the BODY renders a verified, span-grounded claim CITING exactly that entity (drb_72:
150	    coverage_fraction 0.571 = 4/7). This credits an entity as covered when ANY cited-evidence record
151	    (a bibliography row / basket SUPPORTS member of a final-VERIFIED claim) matches the entity's
152	    canonical id: DOI or PMID (exact OR appearing as a substring of the record URL, so a
153	    doi.org/<doi> locator counts) or the entity's ``url_pattern`` (substring of the record URL).
154	
155	    §-1.1 SAFETY: this ADDS coverage credit ONLY from evidence the report actually cited — it never
156	    fabricates a citation, never touches strict_verify / the 4-role evaluator / D8, and is UNIONED
157	    with (never replaces) the existing ``covered_element_ids`` path. cited_evidence_records must be
158	    the evidence of VERIFIED claims (the caller passes the report's cited bibliography, which is
159	    built from verified composed sentences). PURE."""
160	    doi_index: dict[str, str] = {}
161	    pmid_index: dict[str, str] = {}
162	    url_patterns: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
163	    for entity in required_entities or []:
164	        entity_id = entity.get(_KEY_ID)
165	        if entity_id in (None, ""):
166	            continue
167	        entity_id = str(entity_id)
168	        cids = _canonical_ids(entity)
169	        if cids.get("doi"):
170	            doi_index[_norm_id(cids["doi"])] = entity_id
171	        if cids.get("pmid"):
172	            pmid_index[_norm_id(cids["pmid"])] = entity_id
173	        if cids.get("url_pattern"):
174	            url_patterns.append((_norm_id(cids["url_pattern"]), entity_id))
175	    covered: set[str] = set()
176	    for rec in cited_evidence_records or ():
177	        if not isinstance(rec, dict):
178	            continue
179	        r_doi = _norm_id(rec.get("doi"))
180	        r_pmid = _norm_id(rec.get("pmid"))
181	        r_url = _norm_id(rec.get("url") or rec.get("source_url"))
182	        for doi, ent_id in doi_index.items():
183	            if doi and (doi == r_doi or (r_url and doi in r_url)):
184	                covered.add(ent_id)
185	        for pmid, ent_id in pmid_index.items():
186	            if pmid and (pmid == r_pmid or (r_url and pmid in r_url)):
187	                covered.add(ent_id)
188	        if r_url:
189	            for pat, ent_id in url_patterns:
190	                if pat and pat in r_url:
191	                    covered.add(ent_id)
192	    return covered
193	
194	
195	def build_ledger(
196	    required_entities: list[dict],
197	    covered_entity_ids: set[str] | frozenset[str] | list[str],
198	    extra_covered_ids: "set[str] | frozenset[str] | list[str] | None" = None,
199	) -> RequiredEntityLedger:
200	    """Phase B: build the report-level ledger from the native required entities + the
201	    VERIFIED-binding set (``⋃ verified_claim.covered_element_ids``, computed by
202	    ``native_gate_b_inputs``). A required entity is VERIFIED iff its id is in
203	    ``covered_entity_ids`` (the exact existing coverage authority), else GAP_DISCLOSED.
204	    Assigns NO new credit; this is pure accounting + disclosure over an already-decided set.
205	
206	    I-deepfix-001 S2 (#1344): ``extra_covered_ids`` UNIONS an additional covered set (the
207	    citation→entity credit from ``citation_covered_entity_ids``) so an entity cited by a VERIFIED
208	    body claim is not falsely disclosed as a gap. Only ADDS coverage from cited evidence; it never
209	    removes a covered id and never touches the faithfulness engine."""
210	    covered = set(covered_entity_ids) | set(extra_covered_ids or ())
211	    slots: list[RequiredSlot] = []
212	    for entity in required_entities:
213	        entity_id = entity.get(_KEY_ID)
214	        if entity_id in (None, ""):
215	            # a config error elsewhere (validate_entity_severity is the fail-loud guard); skip
216	            # defensively here so the ledger never crashes on a malformed slot.
217	            continue
218	        canonical_ids = _canonical_ids(entity)
219	        verified = entity_id in covered
220	        slot = RequiredSlot(
221	            entity_id=str(entity_id),
222	            severity=str(entity.get(_KEY_SEVERITY, "")),
223	            s0_category=entity.get(_KEY_S0_CATEGORY),
224	            anchor=str(entity.get(_KEY_ANCHOR, "")),
225	            rendering_slot=str(entity.get(_KEY_RENDERING_SLOT, "")),
226	            canonical_ids=canonical_ids,
227	            state=STATE_VERIFIED if verified else STATE_GAP_DISCLOSED,
228	        )
229	        if not verified and slot.is_url_pattern_only:
230	            slot.note = _URL_MISMATCH_NOTE
231	        slots.append(slot)
232	    return RequiredEntityLedger(slots=slots)
233	
234	
235	def render_coverage_gaps_section(ledger: RequiredEntityLedger) -> str:
236	    """Deterministic, public-facing 'Coverage gaps' report section (NO LLM, NO fabricated
237	    citation, NO raw internal artifact wording — Codex design-gate q5 + P2). Returns "" when
238	    there are no gaps so the section is omitted entirely (and the OFF path stays byte-identical)."""
239	    gaps = ledger.gap_slots()
240	    if not gaps:
241	        return ""
242	    lines = [
243	        "## Coverage gaps",
244	        "",
245	        "The following pre-registered required topics could not be supported by a verified "
246	        "citation in this report. They are disclosed here rather than asserted without "
247	        "evidence:",
248	        "",
249	    ]
250	    for s in gaps:
251	        label = s.anchor or s.entity_id
252	        sev = f" ({s.severity})" if s.severity else ""
253	        note = f" — {s.note}" if s.note else ""
254	        lines.append(f"- **{label}**{sev}: not verified in this run{note}.")
255	    lines.append("")
256	    return "\n".join(lines)
257

1	"""I-deepfix-001 (#1344) Bug B — retraction grounding gate.
2	
3	A RETRACTED / WITHDRAWN source must NEVER be a grounding surface for generated
4	prose. A retracted RCT's dose / contraindication / effect size is exactly the
5	clinical-safety hazard the §-1.1 line-by-line standard guards against: a sentence
6	grounded on a retracted study can PASS strict_verify (the cited span really does
7	contain the number, the words overlap) and still be clinically wrong, because the
8	study was withdrawn. The faithfulness engine checks sentence<->span fidelity, NOT
9	whether the source itself was retracted — so the exclusion must happen BEFORE
10	grounding, at the evidence pool, where the credibility engine already knows the
11	retraction flag.
12	
13	§-1.3 posture (WEIGHT-not-FILTER): this is NOT a breadth cap. It is the
14	clinical-safety arm of the ONLY hard gate (faithfulness). A retracted source is
15	not a low-weight corroborator to keep in the basket — it is a withdrawn claim. But
16	the source is NOT silently vanished: every excluded row is RETURNED to the caller
17	and surfaced in disclosure telemetry + a LOUD log, so an auditor sees the pipeline
18	found it and excluded it (recorded, not dropped). The tier classifier's
19	``R0_retracted`` rule already states "Caller should filter retracted sources before
20	composition" — this module is that caller-side filter.
21	
22	Single source of truth: the retraction predicate REUSES
23	``authority.supersession._is_truthy`` — the EXACT truthiness rule the credibility
24	engine already applies to the retraction/withdrawal flags — so the grounding gate
25	and the credibility down-weight can never disagree about what "retracted" means. A
26	string ``'false'`` / ``'0'`` / ``'no'`` / ``''`` or a MISSING flag is NOT retracted
27	(fail-open: a source with no retraction info grounds normally).
28	
29	Pure leaf module: no network, no model import, no faithfulness-engine import. Env
30	kill-switch (LAW VI). DEFAULT ON — a clinical-safety gate. When the corpus has
31	zero retracted sources (the common case) the groundable pool is byte-identical to
32	the input, so default-ON changes nothing until a real retracted source appears.
33	"""
34	from __future__ import annotations
35	
36	import logging
37	import os
38	from typing import Any, Mapping
39	
40	# Single source of truth for the retraction predicate. We deliberately reuse the
41	# credibility engine's private truthiness helper (operator directive 2026-06-30:
42	# "reuse supersession._is_truthy") so the grounding gate and the credibility
43	# down-weight agree byte-for-byte. If supersession ever renames it, this import
44	# fails LOUDLY at module load (LAW II) — the shared predicate moved and BOTH
45	# call-sites must be reconciled, not silently forked.
46	from src.polaris_graph.authority.supersession import _is_truthy as _supersession_is_truthy
47	
48	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.retraction_gate")
49	
50	_ENV_FLAG = "PG_RETRACTION_GROUNDING_GATE"
51	_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"0", "false", "no", "off", "disabled", ""})
52	
53	# The retraction/withdrawal flag keys — IDENTICAL to authority.supersession's
54	# retraction check (``_is_truthy(row, "is_retracted", "retracted",
55	# "retraction_notice", "withdrawn")``), the single source of truth.
56	_RETRACTION_KEYS = ("is_retracted", "retracted", "retraction_notice", "withdrawn")
57	
58	
59	def retraction_gate_enabled() -> bool:
60	    """Kill-switch. DEFAULT ON (clinical-safety). Set ``PG_RETRACTION_GROUNDING_GATE``
61	    to a falsey value to disable — the groundable pool is then byte-identical to the
62	    input and no row is excluded."""
63	    return os.getenv(_ENV_FLAG, "1").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
64	
65	
66	def is_row_retracted(row: Mapping[str, Any]) -> bool:
67	    """True iff ``row`` carries a truthy retraction/withdrawal flag, using the SAME
68	    predicate the credibility engine uses. A string ``'false'`` / ``'0'`` / ``'no'``
69	    / ``''`` or a missing field is NOT retracted (fail-open). A predicate bug must
70	    NEVER exclude a source — on any internal error we treat the row as NOT retracted
71	    (loud-degrade, LAW II)."""
72	    try:
73	        return bool(_supersession_is_truthy(row, *_RETRACTION_KEYS))
74	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — never exclude a source on a predicate bug
75	        logger.warning(
76	            "[retraction_gate] retraction predicate errored (%s) — treating row as "
77	            "NOT retracted (fail-open, no exclusion on a bug).", str(exc)[:160],
78	        )
79	        return False
80	
81	
82	def partition_pool(
83	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
84	) -> tuple[dict[str, dict[str, Any]], list[dict[str, Any]]]:
85	    """Split an ``{evidence_id: row}`` grounding pool into
86	    ``(groundable_pool, retracted_rows)``.
87	
88	    * ``groundable_pool`` — the rows generation may ground against (retracted
89	      EXCLUDED). A NEW dict; the input is never mutated.
90	    * ``retracted_rows`` — the excluded retracted/withdrawn rows, RETURNED so the
91	      caller can disclose them (never silently dropped, §-1.3).
92	
93	    Order-preserving. Idempotent: re-running on an already-clean pool returns it
94	    unchanged with an empty retracted list (so the caller may safely re-apply the
95	    gate after an M-52 corpus-pull re-adds rows). When the kill-switch is OFF,
96	    returns ``(dict(evidence_pool), [])`` — a byte-identical groundable pool.
97	    """
98	    if not retraction_gate_enabled():
99	        return dict(evidence_pool), []
100	    groundable: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
101	    retracted: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
102	    for ev_id, row in evidence_pool.items():
103	        if isinstance(row, Mapping) and is_row_retracted(row):
104	            retracted.append(row)
105	        else:
106	            groundable[ev_id] = row
107	    return groundable, retracted
108	
109	
110	def partition_rows(
111	    rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
112	) -> tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], list[dict[str, Any]]]:
113	    """List form of :func:`partition_pool` for the RUN-LEVEL grounding pool
114	    (``evidence_for_gen``), so EVERY downstream grounding surface — the multi-section
115	    generator, the quantified-analysis ``_q_ev_pool``, and the resume corpus snapshot —
116	    sees the same retracted-free pool from one chokepoint.
117	
118	    Returns ``(groundable_rows, retracted_rows)``. Order-preserving; never mutates the
119	    input; idempotent. When the kill-switch is OFF, returns ``(list(rows), [])``.
120	    """
121	    if not retraction_gate_enabled():
122	        return list(rows), []
123	    groundable: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
124	    retracted: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
125	    for row in rows:
126	        if isinstance(row, Mapping) and is_row_retracted(row):
127	            retracted.append(row)
128	        else:
129	            groundable.append(row)
130	    return groundable, retracted
131	
132	
133	def disclosure_records(retracted_rows: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
134	    """Compact disclosure rows for the run telemetry (the audit trail that proves
135	    the pipeline found and excluded each retracted source). The ``retraction_flag``
136	    names WHICH key was truthy, resolved through the SAME shared predicate so the
137	    disclosure cannot disagree with the exclusion decision."""
138	    out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
139	    for row in retracted_rows:
140	        if not isinstance(row, Mapping):
141	            continue
142	        which = next(
143	            (k for k in _RETRACTION_KEYS if is_row_retracted({k: row.get(k)})), "",
144	        )
145	        out.append({
146	            "evidence_id": row.get("evidence_id", ""),
147	            "title": row.get("source_title") or row.get("title") or "",
148	            "url": row.get("url") or row.get("source_url") or "",
149	            "retraction_flag": which,
150	            "excluded_from_grounding": True,
151	        })
152	    return out
153

1	"""Script-aware content tokenization for the strict_verify grounding floor (L4).
2	
3	I-deepfix-001 L4 (GH #1344) — CJK / multilingual-aware strict_verify.
4	
5	WHY THIS EXISTS
6	---------------
7	The faithfulness engine's content-word-overlap floor was tuned on English: both
8	``provenance_generator._content_words`` and
9	``clinical_generator.strict_verify._content_words`` extract tokens with a
10	Latin-only regex (``[A-Za-z]...``). On a non-Latin claim that regex returns the
11	EMPTY set, which produced TWO unacceptable behaviours:
12	
13	  * recall loss — a genuine Chinese/Japanese/Korean/Arabic claim that IS grounded
14	    in its cited span mis-counts ZERO shared content words and is dropped
15	    spuriously; and, worse,
16	  * a FAITHFULNESS HOLE — where the caller SKIPS the overlap floor when the
17	    sentence has no Latin content words (``if sentence_content:``), a non-Latin
18	    claim could ride through with NO lexical grounding at all.
19	
20	The second is lethal in the Telus clinical context.
21	
22	WHAT THIS MODULE DOES
23	---------------------
24	It is an ADDITIVE, script-aware companion to each caller's existing Latin
25	extractor. The caller keeps its own Latin path byte-for-byte (English behaviour
26	is unchanged) and UNIONS the tokens produced here:
27	
28	  * CJK (Han ideographs, Hiragana, Katakana, Hangul) has no word spaces, so we
29	    emit overlapping CHARACTER BIGRAMS — the standard IR segmentation for CJK
30	    (cf. Lucene ``CJKBigramFilter``). Identical CJK substrings in a claim and its
31	    span therefore share bigrams and overlap correctly.
32	  * Other space-delimited non-Latin scripts (Arabic, Cyrillic, Greek, Hebrew,
33	    Armenian, Georgian, Devanagari, ...) are whitespace-tokenized into whole-word
34	    tokens the same way Latin is.
35	
36	FAIL-CLOSED, NEVER GUESS
37	------------------------
38	Scripts that have NO word spaces AND no bigram convention we trust (Thai, Lao,
39	Khmer, Myanmar, Tibetan) cannot be segmented without a language model / dictionary
40	we do not run here. For those, ``has_unsegmentable_content`` returns True so the
41	caller FAILS CLOSED (drops the sentence) rather than guessing a grounding it
42	cannot establish. This only ever TIGHTENS the engine; it relaxes nothing.
43	
44	All behaviour is env-gated (LAW VI) and DEFAULT-OFF: with
45	``PG_STRICT_VERIFY_SCRIPT_AWARE`` UNSET (the production default) this module is a
46	no-op that is byte-identical to the pre-L4 Latin-only engine. Set
47	``PG_STRICT_VERIFY_SCRIPT_AWARE=1`` (or ``true``/``on``/``yes``) to turn on the
48	multilingual grounding tightening. The default-OFF revert contract is what keeps
49	an English/production run's strict_verify behaviour unchanged until the run
50	config deliberately opts in.
51	"""
52	
53	from __future__ import annotations
54	
55	import os
56	import re
57	
58	# ── Env kill-switch (LAW VI) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
59	_ENV_SCRIPT_AWARE = "PG_STRICT_VERIFY_SCRIPT_AWARE"
60	
61	# ── CJK ranges (bigram-segmented; no word spaces) ───────────────────────────
62	# Han (Unified + Ext-A + Compat) + Hiragana/Katakana (+ phonetic ext + halfwidth)
63	# + Hangul syllables + Han Ext-B..F (astral). Anything here is grouped into
64	# overlapping character bigrams.
65	_CJK_CLASS = (
66	    "㐀-䶿"          # CJK Unified Ideographs Extension A
67	    "一-鿿"          # CJK Unified Ideographs
68	    "豈-﫿"          # CJK Compatibility Ideographs
69	    "぀-ヿ"          # Hiragana + Katakana
70	    "ㇰ-ㇿ"          # Katakana Phonetic Extensions
71	    "ｦ-ﾟ"          # Halfwidth Katakana
72	    "가-힣"          # Hangul Syllables
73	    "\U00020000-\U0003ffff"  # CJK Unified Ideographs Extension B..F (astral)
74	)
75	_CJK_RE = re.compile(f"[{_CJK_CLASS}]+")
76	_CJK_ANY = re.compile(f"[{_CJK_CLASS}]")
77	
78	# ── Unsegmentable ranges (no spaces, no trusted bigram convention) ──────────
79	# A run of >=2 of these letters => we cannot segment => fail-closed. A lone
80	# stray character does not trip the guard (avoids false-dropping an English
81	# sentence with a single decorative glyph).
82	_UNSEGMENTABLE_CLASS = (
83	    "฀-๿"  # Thai
84	    "຀-໿"  # Lao
85	    "ༀ-࿿"  # Tibetan
86	    "က-႟"  # Myanmar
87	    "ក-៿"  # Khmer
88	)
89	_UNSEGMENTABLE_RUN_RE = re.compile(f"[{_UNSEGMENTABLE_CLASS}]{{2,}}")
90	
91	# ── Word run of ANY Unicode letter (script-agnostic) ────────────────────────
92	# ``[^\W\d_]`` = a word character that is neither a digit nor underscore = a
93	# Unicode letter in any script. Used to pick out non-Latin, non-CJK
94	# space-delimited words (Arabic / Cyrillic / Greek / Hebrew / Devanagari / ...).
95	_LETTER_WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[^\W\d_]+", re.UNICODE)
96	_LATIN_ANY = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z]")
97	_UNSEG_ANY = re.compile(f"[{_UNSEGMENTABLE_CLASS}]")
98	
99	# Minimum codepoint length for a non-Latin space-delimited word to count as a
100	# content token (mirrors the Latin ">=3 chars / >=2 overlap" conservatism while
101	# allowing 2-char CJK-adjacent scripts a fair floor).
102	_MIN_OTHER_WORD_LEN = 2
103	
104	
105	def script_aware_enabled() -> bool:
106	    """``PG_STRICT_VERIFY_SCRIPT_AWARE`` master gate (default OFF => byte-identical).
107	
108	    With the env UNSET (the production default) this returns ``False`` and the
109	    module is a no-op: both public helpers return their empty/false identity so
110	    the caller's Latin-only path is byte-identical to the pre-L4 engine (LAW VI
111	    revert contract). Only an explicit ON value (``1``/``true``/``on``/``yes``/
112	    ``enabled``) activates the multilingual grounding tightening, which the run
113	    config opts into deliberately. Consistent with the other I-deepfix-001 W-fixes
114	    (all default-OFF, enabled via the run slate).
115	    """
116	    return os.getenv(_ENV_SCRIPT_AWARE, "0").strip().lower() in (
117	        "1", "true", "on", "yes", "enabled",
118	    )
119	
120	
121	def cjk_bigrams(text: str) -> set[str]:
122	    """Overlapping character bigrams for every CJK run in ``text``.
123	
124	    A single-character run yields the unigram (so a lone ideograph still
125	    produces a token). ``"糖尿病"`` -> ``{"糖尿", "尿病"}``.
126	    """
127	    out: set[str] = set()
128	    for run in _CJK_RE.findall(text):
129	        chars = list(run)
130	        if len(chars) == 1:
131	            out.add(chars[0])
132	            continue
133	        for i in range(len(chars) - 1):
134	            out.add(chars[i] + chars[i + 1])
135	    return out
136	
137	
138	def _other_script_words(text: str) -> set[str]:
139	    """Whole-word tokens for space-delimited NON-Latin, NON-CJK scripts.
140	
141	    Latin words are the caller's own responsibility (English byte-identical);
142	    CJK is handled by :func:`cjk_bigrams`; unsegmentable scripts are excluded
143	    here and handled by :func:`has_unsegmentable_content`.
144	    """
145	    out: set[str] = set()
146	    for m in _LETTER_WORD_RE.finditer(text):
147	        w = m.group(0)
148	        if _LATIN_ANY.search(w):
149	            continue          # Latin => caller handles it
150	        if _CJK_ANY.search(w):
151	            continue          # CJK => cjk_bigrams handles it
152	        if _UNSEG_ANY.search(w):
153	            continue          # unsegmentable => fail-closed path handles it
154	        wl = w.lower()
155	        if len(wl) >= _MIN_OTHER_WORD_LEN:
156	            out.add(wl)
157	    return out
158	
159	
160	def extra_script_tokens(text: str) -> set[str]:
161	    """Non-Latin content tokens to UNION with the caller's Latin content words.
162	
163	    Returns CJK bigrams plus space-delimited non-Latin whole words. Empty when
164	    the master gate is off or the text carries no non-Latin content — so a
165	    caller unioning this in keeps English behaviour byte-identical.
166	    """
167	    if not text or not script_aware_enabled():
168	        return set()
169	    return cjk_bigrams(text) | _other_script_words(text)
170	
171	
172	def has_unsegmentable_content(text: str) -> bool:
173	    """True iff ``text`` carries a run of >=2 letters in an UNSEGMENTABLE script.
174	
175	    The caller must FAIL CLOSED (drop the sentence) when this is True: we cannot
176	    establish lexical grounding for Thai/Lao/Khmer/Myanmar/Tibetan without a
177	    segmenter we do not run, and guessing a pass would be the lethal
178	    weakened-positive failure mode. Off when the master gate is off.
179	    """
180	    if not text or not script_aware_enabled():
181	        return False
182	    return bool(_UNSEGMENTABLE_RUN_RE.search(text))
183

1	"""I-bug-108 — Verifier-driven sentence repair loop.
2	
3	Per Codex strategic-review iter 1 path B + I-bug-108 brief APPROVE:
4	when strict_verify drops a sentence due to "drift" failures (entailment,
5	number, trial-name, content overlap), feed (sentence, cited spans,
6	failure reason) back to the generator and ask for a single rewrite that
7	makes the cited span actually entail the claim.
8	
9	Key design points:
10	
11	1. **Loop runs AFTER strict_verify** — does not modify the verifier;
12	   the verifier remains the source of truth. Repair is a downstream
13	   recovery layer that re-presents repaired candidates to the same
14	   verification chain.
15	
16	2. **Targets drift failures only** — no_provenance_token, invalid_token,
17	   span_out_of_range are NOT repairable (the sentence has no anchor or
18	   its anchor is invalid; rewriting won't help). See REPAIRABLE_REASONS.
19	
20	3. **Token-set preservation (Codex iter-1 P0)** — repaired output MUST
21	   carry exactly the same [#ev:id:start-end] markers as the original,
22	   in any order. Any change → treated as repair failure (preserves the
23	   audit invariant: kept prose never references evidence the original
24	   draft didn't cite).
25	
26	4. **Bounded** — 1 retry per sentence, cap MAX_PER_SECTION repairs.
27	   Deterministic order (original drop order) so MAX_PER_SECTION cap
28	   doesn't make runs noisy.
29	
30	5. **Drop accounting honest** — recovered sentences move from dropped
31	   to kept; failed-repair sentences stay dropped (no double-counting).
32	
33	6. **Off by default in tests, on by default in production** — gated by
34	   PG_REPAIR_LOOP_ENABLED env var. Tests autouse-fixture set =false to
35	   keep CI free of accidental network calls.
36	"""
37	
38	from __future__ import annotations
39	
40	import asyncio
41	import logging
42	import os
43	import re
44	from dataclasses import dataclass
45	from typing import Any
46	
47	from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import (
48	    SentenceVerification,
49	    parse_provenance_tokens,
50	    verify_sentence_provenance,
51	)
52	
53	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
54	
55	
56	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
57	# Tunables
58	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
59	
60	MAX_RETRIES_PER_SENTENCE = 1  # Codex iter-1 brief: bounded; cost discipline
61	MAX_PER_SECTION_DEFAULT = 10  # Codex iter-1 brief
62	NULL_DROP_SENTINEL = "NULL_DROP"
63	
64	# I-deepfix-001 B17 (#1361): marker-PRUNE — a repair may DROP a co-cited [#ev:...] marker IFF its span
65	# does not support the claim (a SUBSET of the original markers), never ADD a marker, never empty the
66	# set. The legacy contract forced marker-set EQUALITY, so when the repair reasoning itself concluded a
67	# co-cited span does NOT support the claim it could not drop that marker — it degraded the sentence into
68	# a vague multi-citation umbrella that "fits" every span, implying corroboration the reasoning denied
69	# (forensic Repair #23/#35). A marker must mean "this span supports this sentence"; allowing the
70	# unsupported one to be pruned (then CONFIRMED dropped via the entailment judge, fail-closed to KEEP)
71	# STRENGTHENS basket faithfulness (§-1.3). LAW VI kill-switch PG_REPAIR_MARKER_PRUNE_ENABLED (default
72	# ON) => the legacy equality contract is restored byte-for-byte.
73	_ENV_MARKER_PRUNE_ENABLED = "PG_REPAIR_MARKER_PRUNE_ENABLED"
74	
75	
76	def _marker_prune_enabled() -> bool:
77	    """True iff the default-ON B17 marker-prune (subset-accept + per-dropped-marker entailment confirm)
78	    is active. ``PG_REPAIR_MARKER_PRUNE_ENABLED=0/off/false/no`` => the legacy marker-set EQUALITY
79	    contract is restored byte-for-byte."""
80	    return os.environ.get(_ENV_MARKER_PRUNE_ENABLED, "1").strip().lower() in (
81	        "1", "true", "yes", "on", "enabled",
82	    )
83	
84	# Failures that benefit from rewriting. See module docstring point 2.
85	REPAIRABLE_REASON_PREFIXES: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
86	    "entailment_failed",
87	    "number_not_in_any_cited_span",
88	    "no_integer_overlap_any_cited_span",
89	    "trial_name_mismatch",
90	    "no_content_word_overlap_any_cited_span",
91	    "overlap_too_low",
92	})
93	
94	
95	REPAIR_SYSTEM_PROMPT = """You are repairing a single sentence that a strict verifier dropped because the cited span does NOT support the sentence's specific claims. Your job is to rewrite the sentence so the cited span actually entails it — drop unsupported specifics, keep the [#ev:...] markers EXACTLY as they appear in the original, output exactly one rewritten sentence (no preamble, no sign-off).
96	
97	CONSTRAINTS (STRICT — non-compliance = repair failure):
98	
99	1. **Preserve [#ev:...] markers EXACTLY**: same evidence_id, same start-end byte range. The number of markers and their content must match the original. Do NOT add new markers, do NOT remove existing markers, do NOT change the byte ranges.
100	
101	2. **No new specifics not in the cited span**: do NOT introduce numbers, study names, jurisdictions, mechanism details, or comparators that aren't literally in the cited span text. The verifier will reject again if you do.
102	
103	3. **Hedge generic where the original was specific**: if the original said "tirzepatide reduced HbA1c by 1.7%" and the span only says "tirzepatide reduced HbA1c", rewrite as "tirzepatide reduced HbA1c [#ev:...]" — the qualitative claim is in the span, the magnitude is not.
104	
105	4. **If you cannot make the claim entailed by the span**: output exactly NULL_DROP (capital, no quotes, nothing else). The original drop will be preserved.
106	
107	5. **Output format**: just the rewritten sentence on a single line, ending with the [#ev:...] markers preserved. No quotes, no preamble, no explanation."""
108	
109	
110	# I-deepfix-001 B17 (#1361): the marker-PRUNE variant of the repair prompt. Constraint #1 is relaxed
111	# from "keep every marker EXACTLY" to "keep every SUPPORTING marker, DROP a marker IFF its span does
112	# not support the claim, never ADD a marker" (a subset, never a superset, never empty). Used when
113	# PG_REPAIR_MARKER_PRUNE_ENABLED is ON; the dropped marker is independently CONFIRMED unsupported via
114	# the entailment judge before the prune is accepted (orchestrator), so the model cannot prune a
115	# genuinely-supporting span to dodge repair. STRENGTHENS faithfulness.
116	REPAIR_SYSTEM_PROMPT_MARKER_PRUNE = """You are repairing a single sentence that a strict verifier dropped because the cited span does NOT support the sentence's specific claims. Your job is to rewrite the sentence so every cited span actually entails it — drop unsupported specifics, output exactly one rewritten sentence (no preamble, no sign-off).
117	
118	CONSTRAINTS (STRICT — non-compliance = repair failure):
119	
120	1. **Markers must be a SUBSET of the original**: keep every [#ev:...] marker whose span SUPPORTS the rewritten claim, EXACTLY (same evidence_id, same start-end byte range). You MAY REMOVE a marker IF AND ONLY IF that span does NOT support the claim — do not keep a non-supporting marker by diluting the sentence into a vague umbrella that "fits" every span. NEVER ADD a new marker, NEVER change a byte range, and keep AT LEAST ONE marker. A marker means "this span supports this sentence."
121	
122	2. **No new specifics not in a kept cited span**: do NOT introduce numbers, study names, jurisdictions, mechanism details, or comparators that aren't literally in a KEPT cited span text. The verifier will reject again if you do.
123	
124	3. **Hedge generic where the original was specific**: if the original said "tirzepatide reduced HbA1c by 1.7%" and the span only says "tirzepatide reduced HbA1c", rewrite as "tirzepatide reduced HbA1c [#ev:...]" — the qualitative claim is in the span, the magnitude is not.
125	
126	4. **If you cannot make the claim entailed by any remaining span**: output exactly NULL_DROP (capital, no quotes, nothing else). The original drop will be preserved.
127	
128	5. **Output format**: just the rewritten sentence on a single line, ending with the kept [#ev:...] markers preserved. No quotes, no preamble, no explanation."""
129	
130	
131	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
132	# Repair invocation
133	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
134	
135	
136	def is_repairable(failure_reasons: list[str]) -> bool:
137	    """A SentenceVerification.failure_reasons is repairable if every
138	    reason has a prefix in REPAIRABLE_REASON_PREFIXES.
139	
140	    Mixed-failure sentences (e.g. invalid_token + entailment_failed)
141	    are NOT repairable — the invalid_token side won't fix from a
142	    rewrite. SKIP per the brief.
143	    """
144	    if not failure_reasons:
145	        return False
146	    for reason in failure_reasons:
147	        prefix = reason.split(":", 1)[0]
148	        if prefix not in REPAIRABLE_REASON_PREFIXES:
149	            return False
150	    return True
151	
152	
153	def _extract_token_signature(sentence: str) -> tuple[str, ...]:
154	    """Return a stable, sorted signature of all [#ev:id:start-end]
155	    tokens in the sentence, used for the Codex iter-1 token-set
156	    preservation check.
157	
158	    Order doesn't matter (a repair may rearrange tokens) but the SET
159	    must match exactly. Each token's full string form is the unit.
160	    """
161	    tokens = parse_provenance_tokens(sentence)
162	    return tuple(sorted(t.raw for t in tokens))
163	
164	
165	def _span_text_for_token(tok: Any, evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]]) -> str:
166	    """The cited span text for ONE provenance token (``direct_quote`` first, then ``statement``).
167	    Returns ``""`` when the evidence row / span is unavailable. PURE."""
168	    ev = evidence_pool.get(tok.evidence_id)
169	    if ev is None:
170	        return ""
171	    direct = ev.get("direct_quote") or ev.get("statement") or ""
172	    return direct[tok.start:tok.end] if tok.end <= len(direct) else direct[tok.start:]
173	
174	
175	def _confirm_marker_droppable(
176	    claim_text: str, span_text: str, judge: "Any | None" = None,
177	) -> bool:
178	    """I-deepfix-001 B17: confirm a PRUNED marker's span genuinely does NOT support the claim, via the
179	    entailment judge — so the model cannot prune a genuinely-supporting span to dodge repair. Returns
180	    True (prune confirmed) ONLY on a clean NEUTRAL / CONTRADICTED verdict. FAIL-CLOSED: a judge_error
181	    (the ``(ENTAILED, "judge_error: ...")`` sentinel), an ENTAILED verdict, an empty span, OR any
182	    exception => False (KEEP the marker — the drop is rejected, never a faithfulness relaxation).
183	
184	    ``judge`` is injectable for offline tests; None lazily builds the production ``_EntailmentJudge``.
185	    A missing/uninstantiable judge fail-CLOSES to False (keep the marker)."""
186	    if not span_text.strip():
187	        return False  # no span to test -> cannot confirm the drop -> KEEP (fail-closed)
188	    if judge is None:
189	        try:
190	            from src.polaris_graph.llm.entailment_judge import _EntailmentJudge
191	            judge = _EntailmentJudge()
192	        except Exception as exc:  # pragma: no cover — judge resolved at runtime on the paid path
193	            logger.warning("[sentence_repair] entailment judge unavailable (keep marker): %s", exc)
194	            return False
195	    try:
196	        verdict, reason = judge.judge(claim_text, span_text)
197	    except Exception as exc:
198	        logger.warning("[sentence_repair] entailment judge raised (keep marker, fail-closed): %s", exc)
199	        return False
200	    # The fail-closed transport sentinel is ('ENTAILED', 'judge_error: ...'); treat ANY judge_error as
201	    # KEEP. Prune ONLY on a genuine NEUTRAL / CONTRADICTED (the span does not support the claim).
202	    if str(reason or "").startswith("judge_error:"):
203	        return False
204	    return str(verdict).upper() in ("NEUTRAL", "CONTRADICTED")
205	
206	
207	def _build_cited_spans_block(
208	    tokens: list[Any],
209	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
210	) -> str:
211	    """Render the cited spans the original sentence pointed to, so the
212	    repair model can read the actual text it must entail.
213	    """
214	    lines = []
215	    for tok in tokens:
216	        ev = evidence_pool.get(tok.evidence_id)
217	        direct = ""
218	        if ev is not None:
219	            direct = ev.get("direct_quote") or ev.get("statement") or ""
220	        span_text = direct[tok.start:tok.end] if tok.end <= len(direct) else direct[tok.start:]
221	        lines.append(
222	            f"  [#ev:{tok.evidence_id}:{tok.start}-{tok.end}]:\n"
223	            f"    {span_text!r}"
224	        )
225	    return "\n".join(lines)
226	
227	
228	async def repair_sentence(
229	    *,
230	    dropped: SentenceVerification,
231	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
232	    model: str = "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro",
233	    max_tokens: int = 400,
234	    temperature: float = 0.2,
235	) -> tuple[str, str | None, int, int]:
236	    """Single repair attempt on one dropped sentence.
237	
238	    Returns (outcome, repaired_text, in_tokens, out_tokens). Iter-2 P1
239	    fix per Codex review: outcome is an explicit string, not inferred
240	    from token counts — reliable telemetry classification.
241	
242	    outcome values:
243	      - "text": the LLM produced a candidate rewrite (in repaired_text);
244	                caller must re-verify before kept[].
245	      - "null_drop": the LLM responded NULL_DROP — accept the original drop.
246	      - "api_failure": exception / network error / no response from API.
247	      - "skipped": failure_reasons aren't repairable.
248	    """
249	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (
250	        OpenRouterClient,
251	        set_reasoning_call_context,
252	    )
253	
254	    if not is_repairable(dropped.failure_reasons):
255	        return "skipped", None, 0, 0
256	
257	    cited_block = _build_cited_spans_block(dropped.tokens, evidence_pool)
258	    reason_block = "\n".join(f"  - {r}" for r in dropped.failure_reasons)
259	
260	    prompt = (
261	        f"ORIGINAL SENTENCE (was dropped by strict_verify):\n"
262	        f"{dropped.sentence}\n\n"
263	        f"CITED SPAN(S) FROM THE EVIDENCE:\n"
264	        f"{cited_block}\n\n"
265	        f"VERIFIER REASON FOR DROPPING:\n"
266	        f"{reason_block}\n\n"
267	        f"TASK: Rewrite the sentence per the rules. If the claim cannot be "
268	        f"made entailed by the cited span, output NULL_DROP."
269	    )
270	
271	    client = OpenRouterClient(model=model)
272	    try:
273	        # I-gen-004 (#496): tag the sentence-repair call for the trace sink.
274	        set_reasoning_call_context(section="_repair", call_type="repair")
275	        response = await client.generate(
276	            prompt=prompt,
277	            # I-deepfix-001 B17: the marker-PRUNE prompt (subset-accept) when the flag is ON; the
278	            # legacy EQUALITY prompt otherwise (byte-identical legacy behaviour).
279	            system=(
280	                REPAIR_SYSTEM_PROMPT_MARKER_PRUNE
281	                if _marker_prune_enabled()
282	                else REPAIR_SYSTEM_PROMPT
283	            ),
284	            # I-wire-009 (#1323): the repair max_tokens defaults to 400 -> floored to
285	            # PG_GLM5_MIN_MAX_TOKENS=4096 on the GLM-5.2 _ALWAYS_REASON path. Bound the reasoning
286	            # pool and floor CONTENT so a single repaired sentence is never starved to empty by an
287	            # effort=high prelude (which would otherwise surface as a silent api_failure below).
288	            # LAW VI env-tunable; faithfulness-neutral (every repaired sentence is re-verified by
289	            # the UNCHANGED strict_verify downstream).
290	            max_tokens=max(
291	                max_tokens, int(os.getenv("PG_REPAIR_MIN_MAX_TOKENS", "4096"))
292	            ),
293	            temperature=temperature,
294	            reasoning_max_tokens=int(
295	                os.getenv("PG_REPAIR_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS", "2048")
296	            ),
297	        )
298	        text = (response.content or "").strip()
299	        in_tok = response.input_tokens
300	        out_tok = response.output_tokens
301	    except Exception as exc:
302	        logger.warning("[sentence_repair] LLM call failed: %s", exc)
303	        return "api_failure", None, 0, 0
304	    finally:
305	        try:
306	            await client.close()
307	        except Exception:
308	            pass
309	
310	    # Strip any wrapping quotes the model may have added
311	    text = text.strip().strip('"').strip("'").strip()
312	
313	    if not text or text.upper().startswith(NULL_DROP_SENTINEL):
314	        return "null_drop", None, in_tok, out_tok
315	
316	    return "text", text, in_tok, out_tok
317	
318	
319	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
320	# Section orchestrator
321	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
322	
323	
324	@dataclass
325	class RepairTelemetry:
326	    attempts: int = 0
327	    successes: int = 0
328	    null_drops: int = 0           # model said NULL_DROP
329	    token_set_violations: int = 0  # repaired output changed token set (ADDED a marker / emptied the set)
330	    re_verify_failures: int = 0    # repaired output still failed verifier
331	    api_failures: int = 0
332	    input_tokens: int = 0
333	    output_tokens: int = 0
334	    # I-deepfix-001 B17 (#1361): co-cited markers PRUNED because the repaired claim no longer cites
335	    # them AND the entailment judge confirmed (NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED) the dropped span does not support
336	    # the claim. A legitimate subset-prune, never a token_set_violation. STRENGTHENS faithfulness.
337	    markers_pruned: int = 0
338	
339	    @property
340	    def recovery_rate(self) -> float:
341	        return self.successes / self.attempts if self.attempts else 0.0
342	
343	
344	def _repair_loop_enabled() -> bool:
345	    raw = os.environ.get("PG_REPAIR_LOOP_ENABLED", "true").lower().strip()
346	    return raw in ("true", "1", "yes", "on")
347	
348	
349	def _max_per_section() -> int:
350	    raw = os.environ.get(
351	        "PG_REPAIR_LOOP_MAX_PER_SECTION", str(MAX_PER_SECTION_DEFAULT),
352	    )
353	    try:
354	        return max(0, int(raw))
355	    except ValueError:
356	        return MAX_PER_SECTION_DEFAULT
357	
358	
359	async def repair_dropped_section_sentences(
360	    *,
361	    kept: list[SentenceVerification],
362	    dropped: list[SentenceVerification],
363	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
364	    model: str = "deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro",
365	    max_tokens: int = 400,
366	    temperature: float = 0.2,
367	    # I-deepfix-001 B17 (#1361): injectable entailment judge for the per-dropped-marker prune confirm
368	    # (``(claim, span) -> (verdict, reason)``). None (default) => the production ``_EntailmentJudge`` is
369	    # lazily built per prune; a test injects a deterministic fake.
370	    marker_prune_judge: "Any | None" = None,
371	) -> tuple[list[SentenceVerification], list[SentenceVerification], RepairTelemetry]:
372	    """Repair-loop orchestrator: walk the dropped list in order, attempt
373	    repair on the first MAX_PER_SECTION repairable items, return
374	    augmented (kept, dropped) lists + telemetry.
375	
376	    Per Codex iter-1 brief P0 #2: recovered sentences MOVE from dropped
377	    to kept (no double-count). Failed-repair sentences STAY in dropped.
378	
379	    Per Codex iter-1 brief P0 #3: deterministic order (input list
380	    order). MAX_PER_SECTION cap applies to ATTEMPT count, not failure
381	    count, so a section with 30 drops and cap 10 attempts the first 10
382	    repairable drops in order.
383	
384	    PT12 safety filter (added after I-bug-108 first-run abort): only
385	    attempt repair on dropped sentences whose evidence_ids are ALL
386	    already cited by some pre-repair kept sentence. Otherwise the
387	    recovered sentence can introduce evidence_ids that the post-section
388	    bibliography builder won't include, causing PT12
389	    rule_pt12_invalid_citation_marker to fire downstream.
390	    """
391	    tel = RepairTelemetry()
392	
393	    if not _repair_loop_enabled():
394	        return list(kept), list(dropped), tel
395	
396	    cap = _max_per_section()
397	    new_kept = list(kept)
398	    final_dropped: list[SentenceVerification] = []
399	    attempted = 0
400	
401	    # PT12 safety: build set of evidence_ids cited by the original
402	    # kept sentences. Repaired sentences must reference only these.
403	    kept_ev_ids: set[str] = set()
404	    for sv in kept:
405	        for tok in sv.tokens:
406	            kept_ev_ids.add(tok.evidence_id)
407	
408	    # Codex iter-1 P0: original-order iteration
409	    for sv in dropped:
410	        if attempted >= cap or not is_repairable(sv.failure_reasons):
411	            final_dropped.append(sv)
412	            continue
413	        # PT12 safety: skip if dropped sentence cites evidence the
414	        # original kept set doesn't reference.
415	        sv_ev_ids = {tok.evidence_id for tok in sv.tokens}
416	        if not sv_ev_ids.issubset(kept_ev_ids):
417	            final_dropped.append(sv)
418	            continue
419	
420	        attempted += 1
421	        tel.attempts += 1
422	
423	        original_tokens = _extract_token_signature(sv.sentence)
424	        if not original_tokens:
425	            # No tokens to preserve → cannot meaningfully repair
426	            final_dropped.append(sv)
427	            continue
428	
429	        outcome, repaired_text, in_tok, out_tok = await repair_sentence(
430	            dropped=sv,
431	            evidence_pool=evidence_pool,
432	            model=model,
433	            max_tokens=max_tokens,
434	            temperature=temperature,
435	        )
436	        tel.input_tokens += in_tok
437	        tel.output_tokens += out_tok
438	
439	        # Iter-2 P1 fix per Codex review: explicit outcome classification
440	        # instead of inferring API failure vs NULL_DROP from token counts.
441	        if outcome == "api_failure":
442	            tel.api_failures += 1
443	            final_dropped.append(sv)
444	            continue
445	        if outcome == "null_drop":
446	            tel.null_drops += 1
447	            final_dropped.append(sv)
448	            continue
449	        # outcome == "text": fall through to token-set preservation check
450	        if repaired_text is None:
451	            # Defensive: should not happen given outcome="text" but keep dropped
452	            final_dropped.append(sv)
453	            continue
454	
455	        # Token-set preservation check (Codex iter-1 P0 #1)
456	        repaired_tokens = _extract_token_signature(repaired_text)
457	        if _marker_prune_enabled():
458	            # I-deepfix-001 B17 (#1361): accept a non-empty SUBSET (a confirmed marker PRUNE), reject
459	            # an ADDITION (a marker not in the original) or an EMPTY set. A marker may only be dropped
460	            # when the entailment judge CONFIRMS its span does not support the claim (NEUTRAL /
461	            # CONTRADICTED); a judge_error / ENTAILED / judge-unavailable fail-CLOSES to KEEP the
462	            # marker (=> the prune is rejected as a token_set_violation), so the model can never prune a
463	            # genuinely-supporting span to dodge repair. STRENGTHENS faithfulness; never relaxes it.
464	            orig_set = set(original_tokens)
465	            repaired_set = set(repaired_tokens)
466	            added = repaired_set - orig_set
467	            dropped_markers = orig_set - repaired_set
468	            if added or not repaired_set:
469	                # ADDED a marker OR emptied the set — a real violation (never an honest prune).
470	                tel.token_set_violations += 1
471	                logger.warning(
472	                    "[sentence_repair] repaired output ADDED markers or emptied the set "
473	                    "(orig=%s, repaired=%s) — keeping original drop", original_tokens, repaired_tokens,
474	                )
475	                final_dropped.append(sv)
476	                continue
477	            if dropped_markers:
478	                # Confirm EACH dropped marker's span genuinely does not support the repaired claim.
479	                _orig_tok_by_raw = {t.raw: t for t in parse_provenance_tokens(sv.sentence)}
480	                # The claim text with provenance tokens stripped (the entailment judge tests the bare
481	                # claim against the dropped span). Reuses the SAME cleaner the verifier uses.
482	                from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import _verifier_cleaned_text
483	                _claim_for_confirm = _verifier_cleaned_text(repaired_text)
484	                _all_confirmed = True
485	                for _raw in dropped_markers:
486	                    _tok = _orig_tok_by_raw.get(_raw)
487	                    _span = _span_text_for_token(_tok, evidence_pool) if _tok is not None else ""
488	                    if not _confirm_marker_droppable(
489	                        _claim_for_confirm, _span, judge=marker_prune_judge
490	                    ):
491	                        _all_confirmed = False
492	                        break
493	                if not _all_confirmed:
494	                    # A dropped marker could NOT be confirmed unsupported (or the judge fail-closed) —
495	                    # reject the prune (treat as a token-set violation), keep the original drop.
496	                    tel.token_set_violations += 1
497	                    logger.warning(
498	                        "[sentence_repair] marker prune NOT confirmed unsupported (orig=%s, "
499	                        "repaired=%s) — keeping original drop", original_tokens, repaired_tokens,
500	                    )
501	                    final_dropped.append(sv)
502	                    continue
503	                tel.markers_pruned += len(dropped_markers)
504	                logger.info(
505	                    "[sentence_repair] B17 confirmed-pruned %d non-supporting marker(s) "
