190	# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
191	# pure prompt build (verbatim design.md / judge_prompt.txt, flattened to one message)
192	# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
193	_SYSTEM = (
194	    "You are an EXTRACTION-INTEGRITY judge for a research pipeline. Given ONE candidate "
195	    "finding unit extracted from a scraped web page, decide whether it is a real, "
196	    "self-contained declarative finding sentence OR page-furniture / broken-extraction "
197	    "garbage. You judge STRUCTURE ONLY (was this cleanly extracted prose), NOT "
198	    "credibility, source quality, or topical value. A low-quality but well-formed claim "
199	    "is is_real_finding=true. If ANY chrome, masthead, heading, truncation, or "
200	    "orphan-citation fragment is glued into an otherwise real sentence, the unit is "
201	    "is_real_finding=false (contamination rule)."
202	)
203	# Precision-narrowed contamination rule (benchmark key-finding #2) — carried in the prompt
204	# so the judge applies it; the deterministic post-pass backstops the separable shapes.
205	_NARROWED_RULE = (
206	    "Apply this NARROWED contamination rule to avoid over-flagging clean prose: "
207	    "(a) truncation ONLY for a MID-WORD cut (a word split by internal punctuation such as "
208	    "'employ.; ment', or a unit that ends mid-word); a complete declarative sentence is "
209	    "is_real_finding=true even if it reads as one clause of a larger argument. "
210	    "(b) orphan_citation ONLY for a unit that is ESSENTIALLY a bare citation fragment "
211	    "(e.g. '.[4][5]'); a real finding that merely ends with a trailing citation cluster "
212	    "(e.g. '...member countries.[11][12]') is is_real_finding=true. "
213	    "(c) a real finding that merely carries a leading section-word prefix "
214	    "(e.g. '**Foundational Theory.** <real finding>') is is_real_finding=true."
215	)
216	_SCHEMA_LINE = (
217	    'Return STRICT JSON only, no prose, no code fence: '
218	    '{"is_real_finding": bool, "failure_class": '
219	    '"clean|scraped_heading|masthead|truncation|orphan_citation", '
220	    '"offending_span": "verbatim offending substring or empty", '
221	    '"confidence": 0.0-1.0}'
222	)
223	# Balanced few-shot: 4 failure classes + 1 clean (illustrative, NOT from any test report)
224	# so the judge does not collapse to "always reject" (fatal in a consolidate-don't-drop pipeline).
225	_FEWSHOT = (
226	    ("Frequently Asked Questions. How does machine learning work.[7][8]",
227	     '{"is_real_finding": false, "failure_class": "scraped_heading", '
228	     '"offending_span": "Frequently Asked Questions. How does machine learning work", '
229	     '"confidence": 0.95}'),
230	    ("Received 12 March 2021; accepted 4 August 2021; published online 19 September 2021. "
231	     "Corresponding author: j.smith@univ.edu.[3]",
232	     '{"is_real_finding": false, "failure_class": "masthead", '
233	     '"offending_span": "Received 12 March 2021; accepted 4 August 2021", '
234	     '"confidence": 0.93}'),
235	    ("the model predicts employ.; ment growth across all sectors.[5][6]",
236	     '{"is_real_finding": false, "failure_class": "truncation", '
237	     '"offending_span": "employ.; ment", "confidence": 0.92}'),
238	    (".[4][5]",
239	     '{"is_real_finding": false, "failure_class": "orphan_citation", '
240	     '"offending_span": ".[4][5]", "confidence": 0.97}'),
241	    ("A 2020 OECD study estimates that 14% of jobs are at high risk of automation across "
242	     "member countries.[11]",
243	     '{"is_real_finding": true, "failure_class": "clean", '
244	     '"offending_span": "", "confidence": 0.9}'),
245	)
246	
247	
248	def build_judge_prompt(unit_text: str) -> str:
249	    """Pure: render the single-message span-quality prompt for ONE candidate unit.
250	
251	    Flattened to one user message (system instructions + narrowed rule + balanced few-shot
252	    + task turn) because the injected ``call_llm(prompt) -> text`` surface (the proven
253	    credibility-caller control surface) sends a single user message and no ``response_format``.
254	    """
255	    parts = [_SYSTEM, "", _NARROWED_RULE, "", _SCHEMA_LINE, "",
256	             "EXAMPLES (illustrative, not from the report under review):"]
257	    for inp, out in _FEWSHOT:
258	        parts.append(f"CANDIDATE UNIT:\n{inp}")
259	        parts.append(out)
260	    parts.append(f"CANDIDATE UNIT:\n{unit_text}")
261	    parts.append("JSON:")
262	    return "\n".join(parts)
263	
264	
265	# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
266	# pure verdict parse (brace-walk; reasoning models emit JSON + trailing reasoning prose)
267	# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
268	def parse_verdict(text: str) -> dict | None:
269	    """Pure: extract the verdict object from a possibly-prose-wrapped reasoning response.
270	
271	    Returns a normalised dict ``{is_real_finding, failure_class, offending_span, confidence}``
272	    or ``None`` on ANY malformed / out-of-scheme output (the caller then treats the unit as
273	    a pass-through error — fail-honest, never fabricate a junk flag). Mirrors
274	    ``entailment_judge._extract_first_json_object``'s technique: ``json.JSONDecoder().raw_decode``
275	    walks each top-level '{' and selects the FIRST complete object carrying the required
276	    ``"is_real_finding"`` key, so a valid verdict followed by trailing reasoning text (the
277	    GLM-5.2 reasoning-model norm) is recovered while a greedy regex would mis-grab.
278	    """
279	    if not isinstance(text, str) or not text.strip():
280	        return None
281	    decoder = json.JSONDecoder()
282	    search_from = 0
283	    while True:
284	        start = text.find("{", search_from)
285	        if start == -1:
286	            return None
287	        try:
288	            value, end = decoder.raw_decode(text, start)
289	        except json.JSONDecodeError:
290	            # not a complete object at this '{' — advance one char and keep scanning.
291	            search_from = start + 1
292	            continue
293	        if isinstance(value, dict) and "is_real_finding" in value:
294	            return _normalise_verdict(value)
295	        search_from = end
296	
297	
298	def _normalise_verdict(obj: dict) -> dict | None:
299	    """Coerce a raw verdict dict to the schema, or None if it is out-of-scheme."""
300	    is_real = obj.get("is_real_finding")
301	    if not isinstance(is_real, bool):
302	        return None
303	    failure_class = str(obj.get("failure_class", "")).strip()
304	    if failure_class not in _VALID_CLASSES:
305	        # §-1.3 FAIL-HONEST: an out-of-scheme / unknown failure_class is malformed judge
306	        # output. NEVER coerce it into a junk class — flagging a real unit as junk on
307	        # malformed/uncertain output is the wrong direction (a clinical-safety drop signal).
308	        # Return None so the caller routes the unit through the pass-through error path
309	        # (is_junk=False, junk_class=None, source="error").
310	        return None
311	    offending_span = str(obj.get("offending_span", "") or "")
312	    try:
313	        confidence = float(obj.get("confidence", 0.0))
314	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
315	        confidence = 0.0
316	    confidence = min(1.0, max(0.0, confidence))
317	    return {
318	        "is_real_finding": is_real,
319	        "failure_class": failure_class if not is_real else _CLASS_CLEAN,
320	        "offending_span": offending_span,
321	        "confidence": confidence,
322	    }
323	
324	
325	# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
326	# deterministic precision narrowing (ONLY ever flips junk -> clean; never clean -> junk)
327	# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
328	def _ends_complete_sentence(text: str) -> bool:
329	    """True iff ``text`` ends like a complete sentence (terminal punct), ignoring trailing
330	    citation clusters and closing quotes/parens."""
331	    s = _TRAILING_CITES.sub("", text.rstrip()).rstrip()
332	    s = _TRAILING_CLOSERS.sub("", s).rstrip()
333	    s = _TRAILING_CITES.sub("", s).rstrip()  # cites may sit inside closers, e.g. .[11])
334	    return bool(s) and s[-1] in _TERMINAL_PUNCT
335	
336	
337	def _has_midword_glue(text: str) -> bool:
338	    """True iff ``text`` contains the '.;' internal mid-word glue (e.g. 'employ.; ment')."""
339	    return bool(_MIDWORD_CUT.search(text))
340	
341	
342	def _is_bracket_only(text: str) -> bool:
343	    """True iff the unit is essentially a bare citation fragment: no alphabetic content
344	    remains once citation clusters are removed (e.g. '.[19][20]', '[9][10][8]')."""
345	    return not re.search(r"[A-Za-z]", _CITES_ANYWHERE.sub("", text))
346	
347	
348	def _is_section_prefixed_finding(text: str) -> bool:
349	    """True iff the unit is a benign section-word prefix followed by a SUBSTANTIAL real
350	    finding (a bold '**Section.**' label, or a short Title-case 'Section.' label, then
351	    >= _SECTION_PREFIX_MIN_TRAILING_WORDS words of prose)."""
352	    if _BOLD_SECTION_PREFIX.match(text):
353	        trailing = re.sub(r"^\s*\*\*[^*]+\*\*\s*", "", text)
354	    elif _PLAIN_SECTION_PREFIX.match(text):
355	        trailing = re.sub(r"^\s*[A-Z][A-Za-z]*(?:[_ ][A-Z][A-Za-z]*){0,3}\.\s+", "", text)
356	    else:
357	        return False
358	    return len(trailing.split()) >= _SECTION_PREFIX_MIN_TRAILING_WORDS
359	
360	
361	def _apply_precision_narrowing(
362	    unit_text: str, failure_class: str, offending_span: str
363	) -> bool:
364	    """Return True iff a JUNK verdict SURVIVES the deterministic narrowing (stays junk).
365	
366	    Demotes only the three deterministically-separable FP shapes the leaderboard names
367	    (orphan trailing-cite, complete-sentence truncation, section-word-prefix). The clause-cut
368	    truncation FPs are NOT separable from clause-cut TPs and are left to the prompt + judge.
369	    INVARIANT: only ever returns False (demote junk -> clean); it is only ever called on an
370	    already-junk verdict, so it can never flip clean -> junk.
371	    """
372	    if failure_class == "orphan_citation":
373	        # Keep only a genuine bare-citation fragment; demote a real finding carrying a
374	        # trailing multi-citation cluster.
375	        return _is_bracket_only(unit_text)
376	    if failure_class == "truncation":
377	        # Keep only mid-word evidence: the '.;' glue (in the span or unit) OR a unit that
378	        # ends mid-word (no terminal punctuation). Demote a complete declarative sentence.
379	        return (
380	            _has_midword_glue(offending_span)
381	            or _has_midword_glue(unit_text)
382	            or not _ends_complete_sentence(unit_text)
383	        )
384	    if failure_class == "scraped_heading":
385	        # Demote a benign section-word prefix that carries a substantial real finding.
386	        return not _is_section_prefixed_finding(unit_text)
387	    # masthead (and any other class): no deterministic narrowing — trust the judge.
388	    return True
389	
390	
391	# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
392	# single-unit judging
393	# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
394	def judge_unit(
395	    unit_text: str,
396	    unit_index: int,
397	    primary_call_llm: Callable[[str], str],
398	    *,
399	    escalation_call_llm: Callable[[str], str] | None = None,
400	    escalate_below: float | None = None,
401	) -> SpanQualityVerdict:
402	    """Judge ONE candidate unit. Never raises into the caller (LAW II fail-honest): any
403	    error from the injected caller / parse degrades to a pass-through (is_junk=False,
404	    source="error") so the §-1.3 contract holds (a real finding is never flagged on
405	    uncertainty)."""
406	    floor = _escalate_below() if escalate_below is None else escalate_below
407	    verdict = _judge_once(unit_text, primary_call_llm)
408	    source = _SOURCE_PRIMARY
409	    if verdict is None:
410	        return SpanQualityVerdict(
411	            unit_index=unit_index, is_junk=False, junk_class=_CLASS_CLEAN,
412	            confidence=0.0, offending_span="", source=_SOURCE_ERROR,
413	        )
414	    # Confidence-escalation: re-judge a low-confidence primary verdict on the escalation tier.
415	    if escalation_call_llm is not None and verdict["confidence"] < floor:
416	        esc = _judge_once(unit_text, escalation_call_llm)
417	        if esc is not None:
418	            verdict = esc
419	            source = _SOURCE_ESCALATION
420	    is_junk = not verdict["is_real_finding"]
421	    junk_class = verdict["failure_class"]
422	    if is_junk:
423	        # Deterministic precision narrowing — only ever demotes junk -> clean.
424	        survives = _apply_precision_narrowing(
425	            unit_text, junk_class, verdict["offending_span"]
426	        )
427	        if not survives:
428	            return SpanQualityVerdict(
429	                unit_index=unit_index, is_junk=False, junk_class=_CLASS_CLEAN,
430	                confidence=verdict["confidence"], offending_span="",
431	                source=_SOURCE_NARROWED,
432	            )
433	    return SpanQualityVerdict(
434	        unit_index=unit_index, is_junk=is_junk,
435	        junk_class=junk_class if is_junk else _CLASS_CLEAN,
436	        confidence=verdict["confidence"],
437	        offending_span=verdict["offending_span"] if is_junk else "",
438	        source=source,
439	    )
440	
441	
442	def _judge_once(unit_text: str, call_llm: Callable[[str], str]) -> dict | None:
443	    """One judge call + parse. Returns the normalised verdict dict or None on any fault."""
444	    prompt = build_judge_prompt(unit_text)
445	    try:
446	        text = call_llm(prompt)
447	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-honest: degrade to pass-through
448	        logger.warning("[span_gate] judge_error for unit (len=%d): %s",
449	                       len(unit_text or ""), exc)
450	        return None
451	    verdict = parse_verdict(text)
452	    if verdict is None:
453	        logger.warning("[span_gate] malformed/out-of-scheme verdict for unit (len=%d)",
454	                       len(unit_text or ""))
455	    return verdict
456	
457	
458	# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
459	# production default caller (lazy — keeps the OFF path free of httpx + authority pkg)
460	# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
461	def _default_primary_caller() -> Callable[[str], str]:
462	    """Bind the production GLM-5.2 primary caller via the proven credibility control surface
463	    (family-segregation, mirror provider-pin, budget + wall-deadline). Lazy import keeps the
464	    OFF path / offline tests free of httpx + the authority package."""
465	    from src.polaris_graph.authority.credibility_judge_caller import (
466	        make_openrouter_credibility_caller,
467	    )
468	
469	    return make_openrouter_credibility_caller(model=primary_model())
470	
471	
472	# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
473	# batch entry — the (deferred) render-seam caller's public surface
474	# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
475	def screen_finding_units(
476	    units: list[str],
477	    *,
478	    primary_call_llm: Callable[[str], str] | None = None,
479	    escalation_call_llm: Callable[[str], str] | None = None,
480	    max_workers: int | None = None,
481	) -> list[SpanQualityVerdict]:
482	    """Bounded-parallel span-quality screen over candidate finding units.
483	
484	    THE PUBLIC SURFACE the (deferred) render-seam caller uses. Returns a list of
485	    ``SpanQualityVerdict`` aligned 1:1 with ``units`` (FLAG-not-drop: no unit is ever
486	    removed — the caller decides what to do with the flags out-of-band).
487	
488	    Flag-gated: when PG_SPAN_QUALITY_GATE is OFF this makes ZERO LLM calls and returns
489	    disabled verdicts (is_junk=False, source="disabled") so the caller can invoke it
490	    unconditionally. When ON, the primary judge runs bounded-parallel; a low-confidence
491	    verdict is re-judged by ``escalation_call_llm`` if one is provided (default None ->
492	    no escalation, matching the 0/471-escalations benchmark). On any judge_error / malformed
493	    output a unit degrades to a pass-through (is_junk=False, source="error").
494	
495	    ``primary_call_llm`` defaults to the production GLM-5.2 credibility caller; inject a stub
496	    for offline tests. ``escalation_call_llm`` is injectable — the production Qwen escalation
497	    must be pinned to the JUDGE chain by the caller (the default credibility factory pins to
498	    the MIRROR/GLM chain, which a Qwen model must not inherit), so it is intentionally not
499	    defaulted here.
500	    """
501	    n = len(units)
502	    if n == 0:
503	        return []
504	    if not is_span_quality_gate_enabled():
505	        logger.info(
506	            "[span_gate] DISABLED (PG_SPAN_QUALITY_GATE off): %d units passed through "
507	            "unjudged (zero LLM calls)", n,
508	        )
509	        return [
510	            SpanQualityVerdict(
511	                unit_index=i, is_junk=False, junk_class=_CLASS_CLEAN,
512	                confidence=0.0, offending_span="", source=_SOURCE_DISABLED,
513	            )
514	            for i in range(n)
515	        ]
516	    if primary_call_llm is not None:
517	        primary = primary_call_llm
518	    else:
519	        # §-1.3 FAIL-SAFE: the default GLM-5.2 caller is built via the credibility control
520	        # surface, whose `check_family_segregation` RAISES at construction when the span-gate
521	        # side-judge (GLM mirror per §9.1.8) shares the generator family unless the process
522	        # has PG_PERMIT_GENERATOR_EVALUATOR_SAME_FAMILY set (and also when OPENROUTER_API_KEY
523	        # is absent). We do NOT globally mutate that permit (a side-judge must not flip a
524	        # crown-jewel two-family invariant for the whole process). Instead, if the default
525	        # judge cannot be constructed, the gate fails SAFE: every unit passes through
526	        # unflagged (is_junk=False, source="error"). Flagging-on-unavailability would be the
527	        # §-1.3 wrong direction (a real finding dropped because the side-judge could not run).
528	        try:
529	            primary = _default_primary_caller()
530	        except RuntimeError as exc:
531	            logger.warning(
532	                "[span_gate] default judge unavailable (%s) -> %d units passed through "
533	                "unflagged (fail-safe, no flag on uncertainty)", exc, n,
534	            )
535	            return [
536	                SpanQualityVerdict(
537	                    unit_index=i, is_junk=False, junk_class=_CLASS_CLEAN,
538	                    confidence=0.0, offending_span="", source=_SOURCE_ERROR,
539	                )
540	                for i in range(n)
541	            ]
542	    workers = max_workers if max_workers is not None else _workers()
543	    floor = _escalate_below()
544	
545	    def _one(idx: int) -> SpanQualityVerdict:
546	        try:
547	            return judge_unit(
548	                units[idx], idx, primary,
549	                escalation_call_llm=escalation_call_llm, escalate_below=floor,
550	            )
551	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — defensive: judge_unit is contracted not to raise
552	            logger.warning("[span_gate] unexpected error judging idx=%d: %s", idx, exc)
553	            return SpanQualityVerdict(
554	                unit_index=idx, is_junk=False, junk_class=_CLASS_CLEAN,
555	                confidence=0.0, offending_span="", source=_SOURCE_ERROR,
556	            )
557	
558	    by_idx: dict[int, SpanQualityVerdict] = {}
559	    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=workers) as pool:
560	        for verdict in pool.map(_one, range(n)):
561	            by_idx[verdict.unit_index] = verdict
562	    out = [by_idx[i] for i in range(n)]  # gather-then-sort: order-preserving
563	
564	    # ── behavioral canary — REAL runtime counts, fired only after the fan-out ran ──
565	    flagged = [v for v in out if v.is_junk]
566	    class_counts = {c: 0 for c in sorted(_VALID_JUNK_CLASSES)}
567	    for v in flagged:
568	        if v.junk_class in class_counts:
569	            class_counts[v.junk_class] += 1
570	    escalated = sum(1 for v in out if v.source == _SOURCE_ESCALATION)
571	    narrowed = sum(1 for v in out if v.source == _SOURCE_NARROWED)
572	    errors = sum(1 for v in out if v.source == _SOURCE_ERROR)
573	    logger.info(
574	        "[span_gate] judged=%d flagged=%d (scraped_heading=%d masthead=%d "
575	        "truncation=%d orphan_citation=%d) escalated=%d narrowed=%d errors=%d",
576	        n, len(flagged), class_counts["scraped_heading"], class_counts["masthead"],
577	        class_counts["truncation"], class_counts["orphan_citation"],
578	        escalated, narrowed, errors,
579	    )
580	    return out
581

1	"""I-perm-004 (#1198) span resolver — re-anchor a claim to the best ENTAILING span.
2	
3	The verifier drops a large share of real claims because the [#ev] token points at a span that
4	does not entail the sentence even though a genuinely-entailing span sits ELSEWHERE in the SAME row
5	(drb_76: 40 verified / 41 dropped of 81, 29 of them `entailment_failed` with in-row support). The
6	existing recovery (`provenance_generator._try_reanchor`, #1189) accepts the FIRST passing candidate
7	with no boilerplate filter and no best-span ranking — so drb_76 re-anchored to the row TITLE
8	(`reanchored:...0-76`). This module replaces first-passing with a BOILERPLATE-AWARE ARGMAX over the
9	candidate spans, choosing the best genuinely-entailing prose span (RARR re-point; ALCE precision;
10	MiniCheck max-over-evidence).
11	
12	SAFETY — the §-1.1-lethal failure here is MANUFACTURING support (re-pointing to a span that does not
13	actually entail the sentence). Three structural guards make that impossible:
14	  1. The resolver NEVER decides entailment itself — the injected ``judge_fn`` (the SAME binding
15	     entailment gate the pipeline uses, run with ``allow_local_window_fallback=False``) does. The
16	     resolver only CHOOSES among candidates the judge already accepted; a non-entailing span can
17	     never be returned.
18	  2. Boilerplate (title / header / affiliation / nav-link / url / altmetric / reference-list) is a
19	     DEPRIORITIZATION + confidence PENALTY, never a fabrication path: a claim supported only by a
20	     title span is returned LABELED low-confidence with ``provenance_quality="title"`` so the caller
21	     ships it caveated, never silently VERIFIED.
22	  3. Confidence is dominated by entailment + numeric-verbatim agreement, NOT lexical similarity, so a
23	     lexically-similar-but-boilerplate span cannot inflate a claim.
24	
25	PURE: no network, no LLM, no global state. The judge and (optional) numeric-match predicates are
26	INJECTED, so this module is deterministic and unit-testable with a stub judge. Default behaviour is
27	opt-in at the call site (the wiring slice gates it behind ``PG_PROVENANCE_REANCHOR``); this module
28	itself only computes.
29	"""
30	
31	from __future__ import annotations
32	
33	import re
34	from dataclasses import dataclass
35	from typing import Callable, Optional
36	
37	# --- provenance_quality vocabulary (Decision B schema) -------------------------------------
38	QUALITY_PROSE = "prose"
39	QUALITY_TITLE = "title"
40	QUALITY_HEADER = "header"
41	QUALITY_AFFILIATION = "affiliation"
42	QUALITY_NAV_LINK = "nav_link"
43	QUALITY_URL = "url"
44	QUALITY_ALTMETRIC = "altmetric"
45	QUALITY_REFERENCE_LIST = "reference_list"
46	
47	# Boilerplate qualities — supported-only-here => LABELED low confidence, never silently verified.
48	_BOILERPLATE_QUALITIES = frozenset(
49	    {
50	        QUALITY_TITLE,
51	        QUALITY_HEADER,
52	        QUALITY_AFFILIATION,
53	        QUALITY_NAV_LINK,
54	        QUALITY_URL,
55	        QUALITY_ALTMETRIC,
56	        QUALITY_REFERENCE_LIST,
57	    }
58	)
59	
60	# Per-quality confidence penalty (subtracted from the entailment-dominated base). Prose = no penalty;
61	# the further a span is from real prose, the larger the penalty so a boilerplate-supported claim can
62	# never read as high-confidence. Ranking prefers the SMALLEST penalty (best quality) among entailing
63	# candidates.
64	_QUALITY_PENALTY = {
65	    QUALITY_PROSE: 0.0,
66	    QUALITY_AFFILIATION: 0.35,
67	    QUALITY_TITLE: 0.45,
68	    QUALITY_HEADER: 0.50,
69	    QUALITY_REFERENCE_LIST: 0.55,
70	    QUALITY_NAV_LINK: 0.60,
71	    QUALITY_ALTMETRIC: 0.60,
72	    QUALITY_URL: 0.65,
73	}
74	
75	# --- deterministic boilerplate detection patterns -----------------------------------------
76	_URL_RE = re.compile(r"https?://|www\.\w|\bdoi\.org/|\b10\.\d{4,9}/", re.IGNORECASE)
77	_ALTMETRIC_RE = re.compile(
78	    r"\baltmetric\b|\bmendeley\b|\btweeted\b|\bdimensions\b|\bcited by\b|"
79	    r"\breaders on\b|\bsocial media\b|\bbadge\b",
80	    re.IGNORECASE,
81	)
82	# A reference-list line: leading numbered/bracketed ref marker, or a dense author-year + doi/pp block.
83	_REFERENCE_RE = re.compile(
84	    r"^\s*(?:\[\d+\]|\d+\.)\s+\w|(?:\bet al\.).{0,80}?(?:\(\d{4}\)|doi:|pp?\.\s?\d)",
85	    re.IGNORECASE,
86	)
87	_AFFILIATION_RE = re.compile(
88	    r"\bdepartment of\b|\buniversity of\b|\bfaculty of\b|\binstitute of\b|"
89	    r"\bcorrespondence\b|\bcorresponding author\b|\borcid\b|\b[\w.]+@[\w.]+\b|"
90	    r"\bhospital\b.{0,40}\b(?:road|street|ave|avenue)\b",
91	    re.IGNORECASE,
92	)
93	# Nav chrome KEYWORDS: "skip to", "menu", cookie banners, journal-site link labels.
94	_NAV_RE = re.compile(
95	    r"\bskip to (?:main )?content\b|\bcookie\b.{0,30}\b(?:consent|policy|accept)\b|"
96	    r"\bsign in\b|\bmain menu\b|\btable of contents\b|\bback to top\b|\bsubmit manuscript\b|"
97	    r"\bauthor guidelines\b|\bfor authors\b|\beditorial board\b|\bcurrent issue\b|"
98	    r"\bcontact us\b|\babout the journal\b",
99	    re.IGNORECASE,
100	)
101	# Nav chrome STRUCTURE: a pipe/bullet-separated link list ("home | articles | archives | about" OR
102	# the compact "home|articles|archive|podcasts"). Two or more separators is a link bar, not prose
103	# (Codex slice-1 P2 — the keyword set alone missed the pipe-list form, and the whitespace-required
104	# form missed the compact bar; both would classify as prose@conf-1.0). Whitespace around the
105	# separator is optional.
106	_NAV_SEPARATOR_RE = re.compile(r"\s*[|•·▪»]\s*")
107	_SENTENCE_TERMINATOR_RE = re.compile(r"[.!?](?:\s|$)|[:;]\s")
108	_WORD_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z'-]+")
109	# A real prose sentence is reasonably long AND ends like a sentence; a title/header is short and has
110	# no terminal punctuation. These thresholds only steer the prose/title/header split — boilerplate
111	# patterns above take precedence.
112	_TITLE_MAX_CHARS = 140
113	_PROSE_MIN_WORDS = 8
114	
115	
116	def classify_span(text: str) -> str:
117	    """Deterministically classify a candidate span's TEXT into a ``provenance_quality`` bucket.
118	
119	    Boilerplate patterns (url / altmetric / reference-list / affiliation / nav) take precedence;
120	    otherwise a short, terminator-free fragment is a title/header and a longer sentence-shaped
121	    fragment is prose. Never raises; empty/whitespace -> ``header`` (uninformative boilerplate).
122	    """
123	    stripped = (text or "").strip()
124	    if not stripped:
125	        return QUALITY_HEADER
126	    # Specific boilerplate patterns first (a reference line / affiliation may itself embed a DOI/URL,
127	    # so the URL bucket is checked LAST among boilerplate).
128	    if _ALTMETRIC_RE.search(stripped):
129	        return QUALITY_ALTMETRIC
130	    if _NAV_RE.search(stripped) or len(_NAV_SEPARATOR_RE.findall(stripped)) >= 2:
131	        return QUALITY_NAV_LINK
132	    if _REFERENCE_RE.search(stripped):
133	        return QUALITY_REFERENCE_LIST
134	    if _AFFILIATION_RE.search(stripped):
135	        return QUALITY_AFFILIATION
136	    words = _WORD_RE.findall(stripped)
137	    if _URL_RE.search(stripped):
138	        # A prose sentence that merely contains a trailing citation URL is still prose; a span whose
139	        # bulk IS the URL is url-boilerplate. Heuristic: URL match AND few sentence words / mostly
140	        # non-letters.
141	        letters = sum(c.isalpha() for c in stripped)
142	        if len(words) < _PROSE_MIN_WORDS or letters < len(stripped) * 0.55:
143	            return QUALITY_URL
144	    has_terminator = bool(_SENTENCE_TERMINATOR_RE.search(stripped))
145	    # Title-case ratio: titles/headers are Title Case (most content words capitalized); prose is
146	    # sentence case (only the first word + proper nouns). This is the primary prose/title signal —
147	    # it separates an 8-word TITLE ("Saccharomyces boulardii Fungemia: A Safety Concern.") from an
148	    # 8-word prose sentence even when both carry punctuation.
149	    alpha = [w for w in words if len(w) > 2]
150	    cap_ratio = (sum(1 for w in alpha if w[0].isupper()) / len(alpha)) if alpha else 0.0
151	    is_title_case = cap_ratio >= 0.45
152	    if is_title_case and len(stripped) <= _TITLE_MAX_CHARS:
153	        return QUALITY_HEADER if stripped.endswith(":") or len(words) <= 3 else QUALITY_TITLE
154	    if len(words) >= _PROSE_MIN_WORDS and (has_terminator or len(stripped) > _TITLE_MAX_CHARS):
155	        return QUALITY_PROSE
156	    # Short, terminator-free fragment: a heading if it has a colon / is very short, else a title.
157	    if len(stripped) <= _TITLE_MAX_CHARS and not has_terminator:
158	        return QUALITY_HEADER if stripped.endswith(":") or len(words) <= 3 else QUALITY_TITLE
159	    return QUALITY_PROSE
160	
161	
162	def _content_words(text: str) -> set[str]:
163	    return {w.lower() for w in _WORD_RE.findall(text or "") if len(w) > 2}
164	
165	
166	def _lexical_overlap(sentence: str, span_text: str) -> float:
167	    """Cheap content-word Jaccard for PRE-ranking only (never the binding signal)."""
168	    a, b = _content_words(sentence), _content_words(span_text)
169	    if not a or not b:
170	        return 0.0
171	    return len(a & b) / len(a | b)
172	
173	
174	@dataclass(frozen=True)
175	class SpanResolution:
176	    """The argmax entailing span for one claim (or ``None`` when nothing in-row entails)."""
177	
178	    best_span: tuple[int, int]
179	    span_text: str
180	    provenance_quality: str
181	    confidence: float
182	    entailed: bool
183	
184	
185	# Entailment-dominated confidence base, then subtract the boilerplate penalty and (optionally) a
186	# numeric-mismatch penalty, then add a small lexical-overlap nudge that can never by itself promote
187	# a span. Clamped to [0, 1]. Tuned so prose-entailed >= ~0.7 (moderate/high) and boilerplate-entailed
188	# <= ~0.55 (low), keeping the §-1.1 invariant that a title-only-supported claim is never high.
189	_ENTAILED_BASE = 1.0
190	_NUMERIC_MISMATCH_PENALTY = 0.25
191	_LEXICAL_NUDGE = 0.05
192	
193	
194	def _span_confidence(
195	    quality: str,
196	    lexical: float,
197	    *,
198	    numeric_ok: bool,
199	) -> float:
200	    confidence = _ENTAILED_BASE - _QUALITY_PENALTY.get(quality, 0.6)
201	    if not numeric_ok:
202	        confidence -= _NUMERIC_MISMATCH_PENALTY
203	    confidence += _LEXICAL_NUDGE * max(0.0, min(1.0, lexical))
204	    return max(0.0, min(1.0, confidence))
205	
206	
207	def resolve_best_entailing_span(
208	    direct_quote: str,
209	    sentence: str,
210	    candidate_spans: list[tuple[int, int]],
211	    *,
212	    judge_fn: Callable[[str, tuple[int, int], str], bool],
213	    numeric_match_fn: Optional[Callable[[str, str], bool]] = None,
214	    top_k: int = 4,
215	) -> Optional[SpanResolution]:
216	    """Choose the best genuinely-ENTAILING span for ``sentence`` within ``direct_quote``.
217	
218	    ``candidate_spans`` are ``(start, end)`` slices of ``direct_quote`` (caller supplies them, e.g.
219	    via ``provenance_generator._reanchor_candidate_spans``). The candidates are PRE-ranked cheaply by
220	    (prose-first, lexical overlap); only the top ``top_k`` are handed to ``judge_fn`` (bounded judge
221	    calls). ``judge_fn(sentence, span, span_text) -> bool`` is the BINDING gate — the wiring passes a
222	    closure that RE-BINDS the [#ev] token to ``span`` and runs the SAME full faithfulness gate
223	    (content + numeric + entailment, ``allow_local_window_fallback=False``), so the resolver returns
224	    ONLY a span the gate accepted and a non-entailing span can never be manufactured into support.
225	    The span OFFSETS are passed (not just the text) precisely so the judge can re-bind. Among
226	    accepted candidates the ARGMAX prefers higher provenance quality (prose over boilerplate) then
227	    higher confidence. Returns ``None`` when no candidate is accepted (caller keeps the drop /
228	    labels "no grounded source").
229	
230	    ``numeric_match_fn(sentence, span_text) -> bool`` (optional) lets the caller require the
231	    sentence's numbers to appear verbatim in the chosen span; a mismatch lowers confidence (never
232	    promotes). When omitted, numeric agreement is treated as satisfied (no adjustment).
233	    """
234	    n = len(direct_quote or "")
235	    # Each entry: (prerank_key, (start, end), span_text, quality, lexical).
236	    scored: list[tuple[tuple[float, float], tuple[int, int], str, str, float]] = []
237	    for start, end in candidate_spans:
238	        if not (0 <= start < end <= n):
239	            continue
240	        span_text = direct_quote[start:end]
241	        if not span_text.strip():
242	            continue
243	        quality = classify_span(span_text)
244	        lexical = _lexical_overlap(sentence, span_text)
245	        # PRE-rank key: prose first (smallest penalty), then lexical overlap. This only orders which
246	        # top_k reach the judge; it never decides entailment.
247	        prerank = (-_QUALITY_PENALTY.get(quality, 0.6), lexical)
248	        scored.append((prerank, (start, end), span_text, quality, lexical))
249	
250	    # Pre-rank descending and judge ONLY the top_k (bounded judge calls).
251	    scored.sort(key=lambda r: r[0], reverse=True)
252	    best: Optional[SpanResolution] = None
253	    best_key: tuple[float, float] = (-1.0, -1.0)
254	    for _prerank, span, span_text, quality, lexical in scored[: max(1, top_k)]:
255	        if not judge_fn(sentence, span, span_text):
256	            continue
257	        numeric_ok = True if numeric_match_fn is None else numeric_match_fn(sentence, span_text)
258	        confidence = _span_confidence(quality, lexical, numeric_ok=numeric_ok)
259	        # ARGMAX: prefer best quality (smallest penalty), then highest confidence.
260	        key = (-_QUALITY_PENALTY.get(quality, 0.6), confidence)
261	        if key > best_key:
262	            best_key = key
263	            best = SpanResolution(
264	                best_span=span,
265	                span_text=span_text,
266	                provenance_quality=quality,
267	                confidence=confidence,
268	                entailed=True,
269	            )
270	    return best
271	
272	
273	def is_boilerplate_quality(quality: str) -> bool:
274	    """True if ``quality`` is a boilerplate bucket (supported-only-here => label low, never verify)."""
275	    return quality in _BOILERPLATE_QUALITIES
276

content.content:
1	"""Deterministic, verified-only SUMMARY TABLE renderer (I-deepfix-001 P7, #1344).
2	
3	Some research prompts explicitly ask the report to end with a summary TABLE and name
4	the exact column headers, e.g. drb_72:
5	
6	    "... please create a summary table ... The table column headers should be
7	    "Research Literature", "Country/Region", "Application Area/Occupation",
8	    "Specific Applications and Impacts", and "Key Risks and Limitations"."
9	
10	The multi-section generator produces span-verified narrative prose but does not emit
11	this requested GFM table, so ``report.md`` renders zero table rows and the DRB-II
12	"presentation" rubric fails on "article does not contain a summary table".
13	
14	This module renders that table WITHOUT any LLM call, purely from ALREADY-verified
15	content, so it introduces NO new claim (CLAUDE.md §-1.3: a summary table is a
16	presentation of already-verified findings). Faithfulness contract:
17	
18	* Every row is one CITED source that carries at least one strict-verify-passed claim.
19	* The "Research Literature" cell is bibliographic metadata (author / title / ``[N]``)
20	  drawn from the numbered bibliography — an identity, not a claim. Its source_title and
21	  authors are CHROME-SCREENED with the same predicate as the claim cell (a chrome /
22	  interstitial title or license/boilerplate author is dropped, the label falling back to a
23	  clean citation form) so no page furniture renders in the cell; the claim/row is kept.
24	* The "Specific Applications and Impacts" (claim-role) cell is the source's own
25	  verified sentence, verbatim (whitespace-normalised, excerpted with an ellipsis),
26	  carrying its ``[N]`` citation.
27	* The Country/Region, Application Area/Occupation and Key Risks cells surface ONLY
28	  short terms that appear VERBATIM in that source's verified spans (high-precision
29	  curated vocabularies). A cell with no verbatim match renders an em dash ``—`` — an
30	  honest DISCLOSED GAP; nothing is inferred or invented.
31	
32	The faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role D8 / provenance / span-grounding)
33	is UNTOUCHED — this reads the finished, verified bibliography + kept-sentence objects
34	and emits markdown. Behaviour is gated behind the default-ON kill-switch
35	``PG_RENDER_SUMMARY_TABLE`` (LAW VI); OFF => no table => byte-identical report.
36	"""
37	
38	from __future__ import annotations
39	
40	import logging
41	import os
42	import re
43	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
44	from typing import Any, Callable, Iterable
45	
46	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
47	
48	# --- canary / marker --------------------------------------------------------
49	CANARY_TAG = "[summary_table]"
50	# Distinctive idempotency marker embedded once in a rendered table (HTML comment,
51	# invisible in a markdown viewer). A resume / re-finalize that re-reads a report
52	# already carrying this marker is a no-op.
53	TABLE_MARKER = "<!-- polaris:summary_table -->"
54	
55	# --- env kill-switch (default ON; LAW VI) -----------------------------------
56	_ENABLE_FLAG = "PG_RENDER_SUMMARY_TABLE"
57	_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"0", "false", "off", "no", ""})
58	
59	# --- disclosed-gap marker ---------------------------------------------------
60	GAP_CELL = "—"
61	
62	# --- excerpt caps -----------------------------------------------------------
63	_CLAIM_CELL_MAX_CHARS = 220
64	_LITERATURE_TITLE_MAX_CHARS = 60
65	_MAX_TERMS_PER_CELL = 3
66	
67	# --- provenance / citation tokens to strip from a claim sentence ------------
68	_EV_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:[^\]]*\]")
69	_NUM_MARKER_RE = re.compile(r"\[\d+\]")
70	_WS_RE = re.compile(r"\s+")
71	
72	# --- header -> column ROLE ---------------------------------------------------
73	ROLE_LITERATURE = "literature"
74	ROLE_GEOGRAPHY = "geography"
75	ROLE_RISK = "risk"
76	ROLE_DOMAIN = "domain"
77	ROLE_CLAIM = "claim"
78	ROLE_GAP = "gap"
79	
80	# Ordered so a header with several signals resolves to the most specific role.
81	# "Application Area/Occupation" hits DOMAIN (occupation/area) before CLAIM; "Specific
82	# Applications and Impacts" carries no DOMAIN token so it falls through to CLAIM.
83	_ROLE_KEYWORDS: tuple[tuple[str, tuple[str, ...]], ...] = (
84	    (ROLE_LITERATURE, ("literature", "studies", "study", "reference", "paper",
85	                        "source", "author", "citation", "title", "research")),
86	    (ROLE_GEOGRAPHY, ("country", "countries", "region", "geograph", "nation",
87	                      "location", "jurisdiction")),
88	    (ROLE_RISK, ("risk", "limitation", "concern", "challenge", "caveat",
89	                 "weakness", "danger", "threat", "harm")),
90	    (ROLE_DOMAIN, ("occupation", "industry", "sector", "profession", "job",
91	                   "application area", "area", "domain", "field", "use case", "task")),
92	    (ROLE_CLAIM, ("application", "impact", "effect", "finding", "result", "use",
93	                  "contribution", "specific", "outcome", "evidence")),
94	)
95	
96	# --- header-list detection cues (prompt asked for a table with named columns) -
97	# I-wire-001 F1 (missing-summary-table wiring): the cue also matches the run-bound
98	# paraphrase form "... with columns: <a>, <b>, ..." / a bare "columns: <a>, <b>, ..."
99	# so a prompt that names the headers AFTER a colon (no quotes) is still detected. The
100	# authoritative header source is the per-task output CONTRACT (threaded as
101	# ``contract_headers``); this parser is the belt-and-suspenders fallback.
102	_HEADER_CUE_RE = re.compile(
103	    r"(column\s+headers?|table\s+columns?|columns?\s+(?:should\s+be|are)|"
104	    r"headers?\s+(?:should\s+be|are)|with\s+columns?\s*:?|columns?\s*:)",
105	    re.IGNORECASE,
106	)
107	_SMART_QUOTED_RE = re.compile(r"“(.+?)”")
108	_STRAIGHT_QUOTED_RE = re.compile(r"\"(.+?)\"")
109	# Connector words that stay lowercase inside a header phrase when the belt-and-suspenders
110	# UNQUOTED fallback title-cases an all-lowercase header (so "specific applications and
111	# impacts" -> "Specific Applications and Impacts", never "... And ..."). A header that
112	# already carries its own casing is preserved verbatim (see :func:`_smart_titlecase`).
113	_UNQUOTED_HEADER_SMALL_WORDS = frozenset(
114	    {"and", "or", "of", "the", "in", "to", "a", "an", "for", "on", "with", "by"}
115	)
116	# The unquoted header list runs from the cue to the END of its sentence (first ``. `` /
117	# trailing period); a header list is comma-separated (with an optional ", and" before the
118	# last item), so we split on ',' ONLY — never on a bare " and " (which lives INSIDE a real

395	def summary_table_enabled() -> bool:
396	    """LAW VI kill-switch (default ON). OFF => no table => byte-identical report."""
397	    return os.environ.get(_ENABLE_FLAG, "1").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES

400	def _smart_titlecase(phrase: str) -> str:
401	    """Title-case an ALL-lowercase header phrase (keeping connector words lowercase after
402	    the first word); a phrase that ALREADY carries any uppercase letter is returned
403	    VERBATIM (so "Country/Region" / "Specific Applications and Impacts" are preserved
404	    byte-for-byte and never mangled by a naive ``str.title``). PURE."""
405	    phrase = phrase.strip()
406	    if not phrase or any(c.isupper() for c in phrase):
407	        return phrase
408	    words = phrase.split()
409	    out: list[str] = []
410	    for i, w in enumerate(words):
411	        if i > 0 and w in _UNQUOTED_HEADER_SMALL_WORDS:
412	            out.append(w)
413	        else:
414	            out.append(w[:1].upper() + w[1:])
415	    return " ".join(out)
416	
417	
418	def parse_requested_headers(research_question: str) -> list[str]:
419	    """The exact column headers the prompt requests, or ``[]`` when the prompt does
420	    not ask for a titled summary table. Detection is CUE-anchored (a "column
421	    headers"/"columns should be"/"with columns:" phrase) so ordinary quoted text
422	    elsewhere in the prompt is never mistaken for a header list. Handles smart and
423	    straight quotes, then an UNQUOTED comma-list fallback (I-wire-001 F1). PURE."""
424	    if not research_question:
425	        return []
426	    cue = _HEADER_CUE_RE.search(research_question)
427	    if not cue:
428	        return []
429	    # Window from the cue to the end of its sentence/paragraph (bounded).
430	    start = cue.end()
431	    tail = research_question[start:start + 800]
432	    # Stop at a hard paragraph break if one occurs inside the window.
433	    para = tail.find("\n\n")
434	    if para != -1:
435	        tail = tail[:para]
436	    smart = [h.strip() for h in _SMART_QUOTED_RE.findall(tail) if h.strip()]
437	    if len(smart) >= 2:
438	        return smart
439	    straight = [h.strip() for h in _STRAIGHT_QUOTED_RE.findall(tail) if h.strip()]
440	    if len(straight) >= 2:
441	        return straight
442	    # I-wire-001 F1 — UNQUOTED fallback (belt-and-suspenders; the CONTRACT path is
443	    # primary). The cue matched but quote-extraction found < 2 header names, so the
444	    # prompt named the headers as a plain comma list ("... with columns: A, B, C, and
445	    # D."). Bound to the cue's SENTENCE, split on ',' ONLY (a bare " and " lives inside a
446	    # real header), drop a leading ", and", strip a trailing period, smart-title-case.
447	    sentence_end = _UNQUOTED_SENTENCE_END_RE.search(tail)
448	    sentence_tail = tail[: sentence_end.start()] if sentence_end else tail
449	    unquoted: list[str] = []
450	    for raw in sentence_tail.split(","):
451	        piece = _UNQUOTED_LEADING_AND_RE.sub("", raw.strip()).strip().rstrip(".").strip()
452	        if piece:
453	            unquoted.append(_smart_titlecase(piece))
454	    if len(unquoted) >= 2:
455	        return unquoted
456	    return []
457	
458	
459	def _classify_header(header: str) -> str:
460	    low = header.strip().lower()
461	    for role, keywords in _ROLE_KEYWORDS:
462	        if any(kw in low for kw in keywords):
463	            return role
464	    return ROLE_GAP
465	
466	
467	def assign_header_roles(headers: list[str]) -> list[str]:
468	    """Map each requested header to a column ROLE, then guarantee EXACTLY ONE
469	    claim-role column carries the verified sentence: if several headers classify as
470	    CLAIM only the first keeps it (the rest become disclosed-gap columns); if none
471	    classify as CLAIM the last non-literature column is promoted so the verified
472	    finding always has a home. PURE."""
473	    roles = [_classify_header(h) for h in headers]
474	    claim_idxs = [i for i, r in enumerate(roles) if r == ROLE_CLAIM]
475	    if claim_idxs:
476	        for i in claim_idxs[1:]:
477	            roles[i] = ROLE_GAP
478	    else:
479	        # Promote the last non-literature column (or the last column) to CLAIM.
480	        promote = None
481	        for i in range(len(roles) - 1, -1, -1):
482	            if roles[i] != ROLE_LITERATURE:
483	                promote = i
484	                break
485	        if promote is None and roles:
486	            promote = len(roles) - 1
487	        if promote is not None:
488	            roles[promote] = ROLE_CLAIM
489	    return roles
490

492	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
493	# Verified-content extraction
494	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
495	def extract_section_claims(sections: Iterable[Any]) -> list[dict]:
496	    """Flatten the multi-section generator's kept, span-verified sentences into a
497	    simple, testable list of ``{"evidence_id", "sentence", "span_verdict",
498	    "is_verified"}`` rows. Duck-typed (``getattr``) so a fake object or a plain dict
499	    works in tests. Only sentences whose FIRST provenance token names a source are
500	    kept (that first token is the sentence's primary citation). PURE."""
501	    out: list[dict] = []
502	    for sr in sections or []:
503	        if getattr(sr, "dropped_due_to_failure", False):
504	            continue
505	        kept = getattr(sr, "kept_sentences_pre_resolve", None)
506	        if kept is None and isinstance(sr, dict):
507	            kept = sr.get("kept_sentences_pre_resolve")
508	        for sv in kept or []:
509	            sentence = _get(sv, "sentence", "")
510	            if not sentence:
511	                continue
512	            eid = _primary_evidence_id(sv)
513	            if not eid:
514	                continue
515	            out.append({
516	                "evidence_id": eid,
517	                "sentence": sentence,
518	                "span_verdict": _get(sv, "span_verdict", ""),
519	                "is_verified": bool(_get(sv, "is_verified", False)),
520	            })
521	    return out
522	
523	
524	def _get(obj: Any, name: str, default: Any) -> Any:
525	    if isinstance(obj, dict):
526	        return obj.get(name, default)
527	    return getattr(obj, name, default)
528	
529	
530	def _primary_evidence_id(sv: Any) -> str:
531	    tokens = _get(sv, "tokens", None) or []
532	    for tok in tokens:
533	        eid = str(_get(tok, "evidence_id", "") or "")
534	        if eid:
535	            return eid
536	    # Fallback: parse a [#ev:<id>:..] token from the sentence text itself.
537	    m = re.search(r"\[#ev:([^:\]]+):", str(_get(sv, "sentence", "") or ""))
538	    return m.group(1) if m else ""
539

541	def _clean_claim_text(sentence: str) -> str:
542	    text = _EV_TOKEN_RE.sub("", sentence or "")
543	    text = _NUM_MARKER_RE.sub("", text)
544	    text = _WS_RE.sub(" ", text).strip()
545	    return text
546

548	def _excerpt(text: str, max_chars: int) -> tuple[str, bool]:
549	    """Word-boundary excerpt of ``text`` to ``max_chars``. Returns (excerpt,
550	    truncated). Never splits mid-word on the trailing edge. PURE."""
551	    if len(text) <= max_chars:
552	        return text, False
553	    cut = text[:max_chars]
554	    sp = cut.rfind(" ")
555	    if sp > int(max_chars * 0.6):
556	        cut = cut[:sp]
557	    return cut.rstrip(" ,;:"), True

560	# Page-furniture / chrome signals that make an otherwise-verified span read badly in a
561	# table cell. Used ONLY to RANK already-verified candidate sentences (never to drop a
562	# source) — choosing the cleanest verified sentence to display is faithfulness-neutral.
563	_CHROME_SIGNALS: tuple[str, ...] = (
564	    "http", "](", "#author", "blog post", "category:", "cookie", "login",
565	    "©", "volume ", "all rights reserved", "click here", "see this",
566	)
567	
568	
569	def _claim_quality(clean: str) -> tuple[int, int, int]:
570	    """Ranking key for a cleaned verified sentence (higher is better): prefer a clean
571	    prose start with no chrome signal, then a quantitative sentence, then a longer one.
572	    PURE."""
573	    low = clean.lower()
574	    chrome = any(sig in low for sig in _CHROME_SIGNALS)
575	    first = clean[:1]
576	    clean_start = (first.isalpha() and first.isupper()) or first in "“\"'"
577	    if clean_start and not chrome:
578	        tier = 2
579	    elif not chrome:
580	        tier = 1
581	    else:
582	        tier = 0
583	    has_num = 1 if re.search(r"\d", clean) else 0
584	    return (tier, has_num, min(len(clean), 400))
585	
586	
587	def _pick_best_claim(sentences: list[str]) -> str:
588	    """Deterministically pick the cleanest, most impact-bearing verified sentence for a
589	    source (see :func:`_claim_quality`); stable tie-break by original order. Every
590	    candidate is already span-verified, so this only chooses which verified sentence to
591	    DISPLAY — it never admits an unverified claim. PURE."""
592	    best = ""
593	    best_key: tuple[int, int, int, int] = (-1, -1, -1, 1)
594	    for idx, s in enumerate(sentences):
595	        clean = _clean_claim_text(s)
596	        if not clean:
597	            continue
598	        tier, has_num, length = _claim_quality(clean)
599	        key = (tier, has_num, length, -idx)
600	        if key > best_key:

content.text: Continuing to read the rest of this large file.

content.content:
600	        if key > best_key:
601	            best_key = key
602	            best = clean
603	    return best

606	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
607	# Source consolidation (I-deepfix-001 UNIT-2). Multiple rows that are the SAME source
608	# document restating the SAME verified finding collapse into ONE multi-citation row —
609	# CONSOLIDATE-KEEP-ALL (CLAUDE.md §-1.3): every source stays cited, none is dropped. This
610	# touches ONLY row grouping/presentation; the faithfulness engine is untouched (each row
611	# already carries a strict-verify-passed claim). Distinct number-sets from the SAME document
612	# stay SEPARATE rows (a real second finding is never merged away).
613	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
614	_SOURCE_CONSOLIDATE_FLAG = "PG_SUMMARY_TABLE_SOURCE_CONSOLIDATE"
615	_CONSOLIDATE_JACCARD_ENV = "PG_SUMMARY_TABLE_CONSOLIDATE_JACCARD"
616	_CONSOLIDATE_JACCARD_DEFAULT = 0.6
617	# I-deepfix-001 (#1369) FIX 5 — a STRICTER Jaccard bar applied ONLY when BOTH
618	# claims are NUMBERLESS (empty salient-number set). A numberless qualitative
619	# claim has no numeric discriminator, so two DISTINCT findings that merely share
620	# vocabulary could pass the 0.6 bar and lose a real second row. Requiring a much
621	# higher overlap (default 0.85) keeps distinct numberless findings SEPARATE.
622	_NUMBERLESS_JACCARD_ENV = "PG_SUMMARY_TABLE_NUMBERLESS_JACCARD"
623	_NUMBERLESS_JACCARD_DEFAULT = 0.85
624	
625	# Salient numeric tokens (percentages / decimals / integers). Two rows can consolidate ONLY
626	# when they carry the IDENTICAL salient-number set, so "43%" and "2.5% of GDP" never merge.
627	_NUM_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\d+(?:[.,]\d+)?%?")
628	
629	# A small, local stopword set for the claim-token Jaccard overlap (LAW VI: no external dep).
630	_CONSOLIDATE_STOPWORDS = frozenset({
631	    "a", "an", "and", "are", "as", "at", "be", "been", "being", "but", "by", "for", "from",
632	    "had", "has", "have", "in", "into", "is", "it", "its", "of", "on", "or", "over", "per",
633	    "that", "the", "their", "them", "then", "these", "they", "this", "those", "to", "was",
634	    "were", "which", "who", "whose", "with", "we", "our", "not", "no", "than", "such", "also",
635	    "between", "across", "under", "about", "after", "before", "during", "while",
636	})

639	def _doc_identity(b: dict) -> str:
640	    """Normalized SOURCE-DOCUMENT identity for a bibliography row. Prefers the URL host+path
641	    (``url`` or ``source_url``, lowercased, scheme/query/fragment/trailing-slash stripped);
642	    falls back to the lowercased whitespace-normalized ``source_title``/``statement``. Returns
643	    ``""`` when neither is present — an empty key is NEVER consolidated (fail-closed: a row with
644	    no identifiable document stays its own row). PURE."""
645	    if not isinstance(b, dict):
646	        return ""
647	    url = str(b.get("url") or b.get("source_url") or "").strip().lower()
648	    if url:
649	        url = re.sub(r"^[a-z][a-z0-9+.\-]*://", "", url)  # strip scheme
650	        url = url.split("?", 1)[0].split("#", 1)[0]        # strip query + fragment
651	        url = url.rstrip("/")
652	        if url:
653	            return url
654	    title = str(b.get("source_title") or b.get("statement") or "").strip().lower()
655	    return _WS_RE.sub(" ", title).strip()
656	
657	
658	def _salient_numbers(clean: str) -> frozenset[str]:
659	    """The set of salient numeric tokens (percentages / decimals / integers) in a cleaned claim.
660	    The consolidation gate requires an EXACT match of this set, so two different quantitative
661	    findings from one document (e.g. "43%" vs "2.5% of GDP") are NEVER merged. PURE."""
662	    return frozenset(_NUM_TOKEN_RE.findall(clean or ""))
663	
664	
665	def _claim_tokens(clean: str) -> frozenset[str]:
666	    """Lowercased content-word token set of a cleaned claim, minus the local stopword set. Used
667	    for the Jaccard overlap half of :func:`_same_finding`. PURE."""
668	    out: set[str] = set()
669	    for raw in re.split(r"\s+", (clean or "").lower()):
670	        tok = raw.strip(".,;:!?()[]{}\"'`—–…%")
671	        if not tok or tok in _CONSOLIDATE_STOPWORDS:
672	            continue
673	        out.add(tok)
674	    return frozenset(out)

676	
677	def _consolidate_jaccard() -> float:
678	    """The claim-token Jaccard threshold for consolidation (env ``PG_SUMMARY_TABLE_CONSOLIDATE_
679	    JACCARD``, default 0.6; LAW VI). FAIL-LOUD parse: a malformed value is logged at WARNING (not
680	    swallowed silently) and the documented safe default 0.6 is used; a parsed value is clamped to
681	    ``[0.0, 1.0]``."""
682	    raw = os.environ.get(_CONSOLIDATE_JACCARD_ENV)
683	    if raw is None or raw.strip() == "":
684	        return _CONSOLIDATE_JACCARD_DEFAULT
685	    try:
686	        val = float(raw)
687	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
688	        logger.warning(
689	            "[activation] summary_table_source_consolidate: invalid %s=%r; using default %.2f",
690	            _CONSOLIDATE_JACCARD_ENV, raw, _CONSOLIDATE_JACCARD_DEFAULT,
691	        )
692	        return _CONSOLIDATE_JACCARD_DEFAULT
693	    if val < 0.0:
694	        return 0.0
695	    if val > 1.0:
696	        return 1.0
697	    return val
698	
699	
700	def _numberless_consolidate_jaccard() -> float:
701	    """The STRICTER claim-token Jaccard threshold applied when BOTH claims are
702	    NUMBERLESS (env ``PG_SUMMARY_TABLE_NUMBERLESS_JACCARD``, default 0.85; LAW
703	    VI). Same fail-loud parse + ``[0.0, 1.0]`` clamp as :func:`_consolidate_jaccard`.
704	    A numberless claim has no numeric discriminator, so consolidating two such
705	    claims demands a much higher vocabulary overlap before they are treated as
706	    the SAME finding — otherwise a distinct qualitative row is silently lost."""
707	    raw = os.environ.get(_NUMBERLESS_JACCARD_ENV)
708	    if raw is None or raw.strip() == "":
709	        return _NUMBERLESS_JACCARD_DEFAULT
710	    try:
711	        val = float(raw)
712	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
713	        logger.warning(
714	            "[activation] summary_table_source_consolidate: invalid %s=%r; using default %.2f",
715	            _NUMBERLESS_JACCARD_ENV, raw, _NUMBERLESS_JACCARD_DEFAULT,
716	        )
717	        return _NUMBERLESS_JACCARD_DEFAULT
718	    if val < 0.0:
719	        return 0.0
720	    if val > 1.0:
721	        return 1.0
722	    return val

725	def _source_consolidate_enabled() -> bool:
726	    """LAW VI kill-switch for source consolidation (default ON). OFF => rows pass through
727	    unchanged (byte-identical to the one-row-per-eid behaviour)."""
728	    return os.environ.get(_SOURCE_CONSOLIDATE_FLAG, "1").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES

731	def _same_finding(a: str, b: str) -> bool:
732	    """True iff two cleaned claim strings state the SAME finding: IDENTICAL salient-number set
733	    AND claim-token Jaccard overlap >= the configured threshold. Both halves must hold, so a
734	    different quantitative result is never merged even when the surrounding prose is similar.
735	    PURE."""
736	    sa, sb = _salient_numbers(a), _salient_numbers(b)
737	    if sa != sb:
738	        return False
739	    ta, tb = _claim_tokens(a), _claim_tokens(b)
740	    if not ta and not tb:
741	        return True
742	    if not ta or not tb:
743	        return False
744	    jaccard = len(ta & tb) / len(ta | tb)
745	    # I-deepfix-001 (#1369) FIX 5 — a NUMBERLESS-vs-NUMBERLESS pair (both empty
746	    # salient-number sets) has no numeric discriminator, so it must clear the
747	    # STRICTER threshold; a numbered pair keeps the standard bar.
748	    threshold = (
749	        _numberless_consolidate_jaccard() if not sa else _consolidate_jaccard()
750	    )
751	    return jaccard >= threshold
752	
753	
754	def _union_terms(term_lists: Iterable[list[str]]) -> list[str]:
755	    """Order-stable, case-insensitive-deduped union of curated cell terms across cluster members,
756	    capped at ``_MAX_TERMS_PER_CELL``. Each input term is ALREADY verbatim-verified for its own
757	    source, so the union only surfaces already-verified terms (never fabricates). PURE."""
758	    seen: set[str] = set()
759	    out: list[str] = []
760	    for terms in term_lists:
761	        for t in terms or []:
762	            key = t.lower()
763	            if key not in seen:
764	                seen.add(key)
765	                out.append(t)
766	    return out[:_MAX_TERMS_PER_CELL]
767	
768	
769	# I-deepfix-001 (#1369) FIX 1 — summary-table duplicate rows (10/36 rows in box-2 were dups of 3 docs:
770	# SERBE×6, Deloitte [10][11], laweconcenter [25][26]). Root cause (Fable): (a) doc_key splits the SAME
771	# document across a URL-keyed row and title-keyed rows (blank-URL bibliography entries), so they never
772	# unite; (b) _same_finding's Jaccard>=0.6 bar rejects the composer's synonym-swap paraphrases (0.53-0.56).
773	# A SECOND additive pass groups the FINAL rows by their VISIBLE source label (Literature minus trailing
774	# [N] citations) and merges, within a label, rows with an identical non-empty salient-number set OR high
775	# claim-token CONTAINMENT — catching URL-vs-title splits and paraphrases without lowering the primary bar.
776	# CONSOLIDATE-KEEP-ALL: every citation survives on the merged row. Default-ON PG_SUMMARY_TABLE_LABEL_REGROUP.
777	_LABEL_CITE_SUFFIX_RE = re.compile(r"(?:\s*\[\d+\])+\s*$")
778	_ENV_LABEL_REGROUP = "PG_SUMMARY_TABLE_LABEL_REGROUP"
779	_REGROUP_CONTAINMENT_WITH_NUMBERS = 0.60  # even with an identical number set, require real token overlap
780	# I-deepfix-001 (#1369) iter2 (Codex + Fable P2): a NUMBERLESS pair has no numeric discriminator, so the
781	# label-regroup second pass must clear the SAME strict bar as the first-pass numberless Jaccard (0.85),
782	# not 0.60 — else two DISTINCT same-label qualitative findings at 60% containment over-merge and a real
783	# second finding is lost from the table (§-1.3 no-drop). Number-matched pairs keep the 0.60 bar (the
784	# identical salient-number set is the real discriminator there).
785	_REGROUP_CONTAINMENT_NUMBERLESS = 0.85
786

788	def _merge_cluster(cluster: list[_RowData]) -> _RowData:
789	    """Collapse a >=2-member same-document same-finding cluster into ONE multi-citation row.
790	    ``num`` = min member num (stable sort anchor); ``cite_nums`` = sorted union of ALL members'
791	    citations (CONSOLIDATE-KEEP-ALL — every eid stays cited); claim = cleanest member claim; the
792	    curated cells = order-stable capped union; literature = the min-num member's author+title base
793	    followed by ALL ``[N]`` citations. PURE."""
794	    ordered = sorted(cluster, key=lambda r: r.num)
795	    min_row = ordered[0]
796	    cite_nums = sorted({n for m in ordered for n in (m.cite_nums or [m.num])})
797	    best_claim = _pick_best_claim([m.claim for m in ordered])
798	    rep = min_row
799	    for m in ordered:
800	        if _clean_claim_text(m.claim) == best_claim:
801	            rep = m
802	            break
803	    # I-deepfix-001 (#1369) FIX 1: strip ALL trailing [N] citations (robust to an already-consolidated
804	    # multi-cite row entering the label-regroup second pass), then re-append the full keep-all set. For a
805	    # single-cite row "X [8]" this is byte-identical to the prior single-suffix strip.
806	    base = _LABEL_CITE_SUFFIX_RE.sub("", min_row.literature).rstrip()
807	    literature = base + "".join(f"[{n}]" for n in cite_nums)
808	    return _RowData(
809	        num=min_row.num,
810	        literature=literature,
811	        claim=best_claim,
812	        claim_truncated=rep.claim_truncated,
813	        geography=_union_terms([m.geography for m in ordered]),
814	        domain=_union_terms([m.domain for m in ordered]),
815	        risk=_union_terms([m.risk for m in ordered]),
816	        doc_key=min_row.doc_key,
817	        cite_nums=cite_nums,
818	    )
819	
820	
821	def _consolidate_rows_by_source(rows: list[_RowData]) -> list[_RowData]:
822	    """Group rows by non-empty ``doc_key`` and greedily cluster same-finding restatements from the
823	    SAME document into one multi-citation row (CONSOLIDATE-KEEP-ALL). Empty-``doc_key`` rows and
824	    singletons pass through unchanged. Deterministic: members are clustered in ascending ``num``
825	    order and each new row's greedy match is against the cluster SEED. Emits the realized-effect
826	    ``[activation]`` marker (anti-dark: a live consolidation path always logs). PURE apart from the
827	    marker log."""
828	    rows_in = len(rows)
829	    passthrough: list[_RowData] = []
830	    by_doc: dict[str, list[_RowData]] = {}
831	    for r in rows:
832	        if r.doc_key:
833	            by_doc.setdefault(r.doc_key, []).append(r)
834	        else:
835	            passthrough.append(r)  # no document identity => never consolidated
836	
837	    out: list[_RowData] = list(passthrough)
838	    clusters = 0
839	    for group in by_doc.values():
840	        ordered = sorted(group, key=lambda r: r.num)
841	        used = [False] * len(ordered)
842	        for i, seed in enumerate(ordered):
843	            if used[i]:
844	                continue
845	            cluster = [seed]
846	            used[i] = True
847	            for j in range(i + 1, len(ordered)):
848	                if used[j]:
849	                    continue
850	                if _same_finding(seed.claim, ordered[j].claim):
851	                    cluster.append(ordered[j])
852	                    used[j] = True
853	            if len(cluster) >= 2:
854	                out.append(_merge_cluster(cluster))
855	                clusters += 1
856	            else:
857	                out.append(cluster[0])  # singleton unchanged
858	
859	    logger.info(
860	        "[activation] summary_table_source_consolidate: clusters=%d rows_in=%d rows_out=%d",
861	        clusters, rows_in, len(out),
862	    )
863	    return out
864

866	def _label_regroup_enabled() -> bool:
867	    """LAW VI kill-switch ``PG_SUMMARY_TABLE_LABEL_REGROUP`` (default ON). OFF => the second pass is a
868	    no-op and rows pass through byte-identically."""
869	    return os.environ.get(_ENV_LABEL_REGROUP, "1").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES

872	def _row_label_key(r: "_RowData") -> str:
873	    """The row's VISIBLE-source grouping key: the Literature cell with its trailing ``[N]`` citations
874	    stripped, whitespace-normalized and lowercased. Groups a document that split across a URL-keyed row
875	    and title-keyed rows (which ``doc_key`` kept apart). PURE."""
876	    lit = _LABEL_CITE_SUFFIX_RE.sub("", r.literature or "")
877	    return _WS_RE.sub(" ", lit).strip().lower()

879	
880	def _same_finding_regroup(a: str, b: str) -> bool:
881	    """Relaxed same-finding test for the SAME-LABEL second pass: a DIFFERENT salient-number set is never
882	    merged (different quantitative result); an identical number set (or both numberless) merges when the
883	    claim-token CONTAINMENT (|A∩B|/min(|A|,|B|)) clears the bar — a lower bar when an identical NON-EMPTY
884	    number set already anchors the pair, the stricter bar when there is no numeric discriminator. Uses
885	    containment (not Jaccard) so synonym swaps + length asymmetry do not defeat it. PURE."""
886	    sa, sb = _salient_numbers(a), _salient_numbers(b)
887	    if sa != sb:
888	        return False
889	    ta, tb = _claim_tokens(a), _claim_tokens(b)
890	    if not ta and not tb:
891	        return True
892	    if not ta or not tb:
893	        return False
894	    containment = len(ta & tb) / min(len(ta), len(tb))
895	    bar = _REGROUP_CONTAINMENT_WITH_NUMBERS if sa else _REGROUP_CONTAINMENT_NUMBERLESS
896	    return containment >= bar
897	
898	
899	def _regroup_rows_by_label(rows: list["_RowData"]) -> list["_RowData"]:
900	    """SECOND additive consolidation pass (FIX 1): group by VISIBLE source label and merge same-label
901	    rows that state the same finding under the relaxed :func:`_same_finding_regroup` test. Empty/`source`
902	    labels and singletons pass through unchanged; merge is CONSOLIDATE-KEEP-ALL via ``_merge_cluster``.
903	    Emits the realized-effect ``[activation]`` marker (anti-dark). Order-stable. PURE apart from the log."""
904	    if not _label_regroup_enabled():
905	        return rows
906	    rows_in = len(rows)
907	    by_label: dict[str, list[_RowData]] = {}
908	    order: list[str] = []
909	    passthrough: list[_RowData] = []
910	    for r in rows:
911	        key = _row_label_key(r)
912	        if not key or key == "source":
913	            passthrough.append(r)  # no identifiable label => never regrouped (fail-closed)
914	            continue
915	        if key not in by_label:
916	            by_label[key] = []
917	            order.append(key)
918	        by_label[key].append(r)
919	    out: list[_RowData] = []
920	    groups = 0
921	    for key in order:
922	        group = by_label[key]
923	        if len(group) < 2:
924	            out.append(group[0])
925	            continue
926	        ordered = sorted(group, key=lambda r: r.num)
927	        used = [False] * len(ordered)
928	        for i, seed in enumerate(ordered):
929	            if used[i]:
930	                continue
931	            cluster = [seed]
932	            used[i] = True
933	            for j in range(i + 1, len(ordered)):
934	                if used[j]:
935	                    continue
936	                if _same_finding_regroup(seed.claim, ordered[j].claim):
937	                    cluster.append(ordered[j])
938	                    used[j] = True
939	            if len(cluster) >= 2:
940	                out.append(_merge_cluster(cluster))
941	                groups += 1
942	            else:
943	                out.append(cluster[0])
944	    out.extend(passthrough)
945	    logger.info(
946	        "[activation] summary_table_label_regroup: groups=%d rows_in=%d rows_out=%d",
947	        groups, rows_in, len(out),
948	    )
949	    return out

952	def _word_boundary_search(needle_regex_body: str, text: str, *, ignore_case: bool) -> bool:
953	    """True iff ``needle_regex_body`` matches ``text`` bounded on BOTH sides by a NON-word
954	    character (or a string edge) — i.e. as a WHOLE token, never as a substring inside a
955	    larger word. This is the anti-fabrication core: "mining" must NOT match "examining"
956	    and "india" must NOT match "indiana". ``needle_regex_body`` is an already regex-ready
957	    body (``re.escape`` it for a literal phrase). PURE."""
958	    flags = re.IGNORECASE if ignore_case else 0
959	    return re.search(r"(?<!\w)(?:" + needle_regex_body + r")(?!\w)", text, flags) is not None
960	
961	
962	def _dedupe_morphological(terms: list[str]) -> list[str]:
963	    """Collapse morphological near-duplicates within one cell — a term whose letters are a
964	    prefix of an already-kept term's letters (e.g. "Displace" vs "Displacement",
965	    "Freelance" vs "Freelancer"). Order-stable, keeps the FIRST-seen surface form. This is
966	    a PRESENTATION de-dup over terms that are EACH already verbatim-verified — it only
967	    removes a redundant sibling, never adds or fabricates a term. PURE."""
968	    kept: list[str] = []
969	    for t in terms:
970	        tl = re.sub(r"[^a-z]", "", t.lower())
971	        is_dup = False
972	        for k in kept:
973	            kl = re.sub(r"[^a-z]", "", k.lower())
974	            if not tl or not kl:
975	                continue
976	            short, long = (tl, kl) if len(tl) <= len(kl) else (kl, tl)
977	            if len(short) >= 4 and long.startswith(short):
978	                is_dup = True
979	                break
980	        if not is_dup:
981	            kept.append(t)
982	    return kept
983

985	def _match_terms_ci(text_lower: str, phrases: Iterable[str]) -> list[str]:
986	    """Distinct display terms whose curated phrase appears as a WHOLE WORD/token in the
987	    lowercased text. Hyphens are normalised to spaces on BOTH sides so "customer-support"
988	    matches "customer support"; matching is word-boundary anchored (never a substring), so
989	    "mining" is NOT surfaced from "examining"/"determining"/"undermining". Order-stable,
990	    deduped, morphological near-duplicates collapsed. PURE."""
991	    norm = text_lower.replace("-", " ")
992	    seen: set[str] = set()
993	    out: list[str] = []
994	    for phrase in phrases:
995	        p = phrase.replace("-", " ").lower().strip()
996	        if not p or not _word_boundary_search(re.escape(p), norm, ignore_case=False):
997	            continue
998	        display = phrase.replace("-", " ").strip()
999	        key = display.lower()
1000	        if key not in seen:
1001	            seen.add(key)
1002	            out.append(display[:1].upper() + display[1:])
1003	    return _dedupe_morphological(out)
1004	
1005	
1006	def _match_geography(text: str) -> list[str]:
1007	    """Distinct canonical geographies whose curated phrase/abbreviation appears as a WHOLE
1008	    WORD in ``text``. Whole-token bounded (never substring), so "india" is NOT surfaced
1009	    from "indiana"/"Indiana" and "US" is NOT surfaced from "USA"/"GAUSS". Full-name phrases
1010	    match case-insensitively; bare uppercase abbreviations match case-sensitively (so the
1011	    English pronoun "us" is never read as the United States). Order-stable, deduped. PURE."""
1012	    out: list[str] = []
1013	    seen: set[str] = set()
1014	    for phrase, display in _GEO_PHRASES:
1015	        if display.lower() in seen:
1016	            continue
1017	        if _word_boundary_search(re.escape(phrase), text, ignore_case=True):
1018	            seen.add(display.lower())
1019	            out.append(display)
1020	    for body, display in _GEO_ABBREV:
1021	        if display.lower() in seen:
1022	            continue
1023	        if _word_boundary_search(body, text, ignore_case=False):
1024	            seen.add(display.lower())
1025	            out.append(display)
1026	    return out

1029	def _format_authors(authors: Any) -> str:
1030	    names = [str(a).strip() for a in (authors or []) if str(a).strip()]
1031	    if not names:
1032	        return ""
1033	    if len(names) == 1:
1034	        return names[0]
1035	    if len(names) == 2:
1036	        return f"{names[0]}, {names[1]}"
1037	    return f"{names[0]} et al."
