1040	def _build_rows(
1041	    bibliography: list[dict],
1042	    section_claims: list[dict] | None,
1043	    chrome_screen: Callable[[str], bool] | None = None,
1044	) -> list[_RowData]:
1045	    """One row per numbered bibliography source that carries at least one verified,
1046	    NON-chrome claim. Claim text prefers the CLEAN kept-sentence (``section_claims``); it
1047	    falls back to THIS source's OWN verified basket member's VERBATIM ``direct_quote``
1048	    (span_verdict==SUPPORTS) only when no clean sentence was captured — NEVER the basket's
1049	    synthesized/consolidated ``claim_text`` (which can carry a DIFFERENT source's
1050	    subject/predicate). A source whose only verified spans are chrome / interstitial
1051	    furniture (per ``chrome_screen``) yields NO row — it has no research finding to
1052	    present (it remains in the bibliography). PURE."""
1053	    def screen(text: str) -> bool:
1054	        # ALWAYS run the built-in interstitial screen; OR-in the injected canonical
1055	        # predicate (which adds masthead / mid-word-truncation coverage). Fail-open on
1056	        # an injected-predicate error => treat as NOT chrome (never drop a real claim).
1057	        if _default_chrome_screen(text):
1058	            return True
1059	        if chrome_screen is not None:
1060	            try:
1061	                return bool(chrome_screen(text))
1062	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — additive screen; keep the claim on error
1063	                return False
1064	        return False
1065	    # eid -> bibliography row (numbered, cited sources only).
1066	    eid_to_bib: dict[str, dict] = {}
1067	    for b in bibliography or []:
1068	        if not isinstance(b, dict):
1069	            continue
1070	        eid = str(b.get("evidence_id") or "")
1071	        num = b.get("num")
1072	        if eid and isinstance(num, int) and eid not in eid_to_bib:
1073	            eid_to_bib[eid] = b
1074	
1075	    # eid -> its STRICT-VERIFY-PASSED clean sentences (``is_verified`` only).
1076	    eid_to_sentences: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
1077	    for c in section_claims or []:
1078	        eid = str(c.get("evidence_id") or "")
1079	        if eid not in eid_to_bib:
1080	            continue
1081	        # Require strict-verify-PASSED (``is_verified``). A raw ``span_verdict == SUPPORTS``
1082	        # is NOT sufficient (Codex iter-2 P2): a span can isolate-SUPPORT while the kept
1083	        # sentence still FAILS strict_verify (numeric-match / >=2 content-word overlap /
1084	        # provenance). Only a strict-verify-passed kept sentence may seed a claim cell — this
1085	        # matches the module's stated contract ("one strict-verify-passed claim per row").
1086	        if not c.get("is_verified"):
1087	            continue
1088	        eid_to_sentences.setdefault(eid, []).append(str(c.get("sentence") or ""))
1089	
1090	    rows: list[_RowData] = []
1091	    for eid, b in eid_to_bib.items():
1092	        num = int(b["num"])
1093	        sentences = eid_to_sentences.get(eid, [])
1094	        # THIS source's OWN verified basket-member verbatim direct_quotes (the member's own
1095	        # evidence_id AND its own isolated span_verdict==SUPPORTS). Never another source's
1096	        # member, never the basket's synthesized claim_text.
1097	        own_quotes = _own_verified_member_quotes(b)
1098	        claim_source_texts = list(sentences)
1099	        used_fallback = False

1100	        if not claim_source_texts:
1101	            if own_quotes:
1102	                claim_source_texts = list(own_quotes)
1103	                used_fallback = True
1104	        if not claim_source_texts:
1105	            continue  # no verified claim for this source => no row (don't invent)
1106	
1107	        # Drop chrome/interstitial spans (CAPTCHA walls, mastheads) — not research
1108	        # findings — before selecting the claim to display, consistent with the body's
1109	        # render-seam. A source left with no real claim yields no row.
1110	        usable = [t for t in claim_source_texts if not screen(_clean_claim_text(t))]
1111	        if not usable:
1112	            continue
1113	        claim_source_texts = usable
1114	
1115	        best = _pick_best_claim(claim_source_texts)
1116	        if not best:
1117	            continue
1118	        excerpt, truncated = _excerpt(best, _CLAIM_CELL_MAX_CHARS)
1119	        if used_fallback and excerpt[:1].islower():
1120	            excerpt = "…" + excerpt  # signal a mid-span basket excerpt honestly
1121	
1122	        # Attribute-extraction scans ONLY THIS source's OWN verified text — its clean
1123	        # kept-sentences AND its OWN verified basket-member direct_quotes (span_verdict==
1124	        # SUPPORTS). It NEVER scans the basket's synthesized/other-source claim_text, so a
1125	        # surfaced geography/domain/risk term can only come from THIS row's own verified
1126	        # evidence (no cross-source leak). The curated vocabularies are high-precision and
1127	        # WHOLE-WORD matched, so scanning this source's own verified text lifts recall
1128	        # without admitting anything unverified (CLAUDE.md §-1.3 surface-more-verified).
1129	        blob_parts = list(claim_source_texts)
1130	        for _q in own_quotes:
1131	            if _q not in blob_parts:
1132	                blob_parts.append(_q)
1133	        span_blob = " ".join(blob_parts)
1134	        geography = _match_geography(span_blob)[:_MAX_TERMS_PER_CELL]
1135	        domain = _match_terms_ci(span_blob.lower(), _DOMAIN_PHRASES)[:_MAX_TERMS_PER_CELL]
1136	        risk = _match_terms_ci(span_blob.lower(), _RISK_PHRASES)[:_MAX_TERMS_PER_CELL]
1137	
1138	        # Chrome-screen the Research-Literature cell metadata (source_title / authors) with
1139	        # the SAME predicate the claim cell uses (CLAUDE.md §-1.3: EVERY rendered cell must be
1140	        # verified-clean, not only the claim cell). A source can carry a CLEAN verified claim
1141	        # yet a chrome/interstitial source_title (e.g. "Just a moment…", "Access denied") or
1142	        # license/boilerplate author furniture ("© … all rights reserved"). Screen each field
1143	        # independently; a chrome field is DROPPED (metadata only) and the label safely falls
1144	        # back to a clean citation form — author — title, else "Source [N]". This NEVER drops
1145	        # the verified claim/row: it only prevents chrome from rendering in the cell (a
1146	        # screened-out title/author still lives in the numbered bibliography, NOT a §-1.3 drop).
1147	        raw_title = str(b.get("source_title") or b.get("statement") or "").strip()
1148	        if raw_title and screen(raw_title):
1149	            raw_title = ""  # chrome title => drop metadata; fall back to a clean citation form
1150	        title, title_trunc = _excerpt(raw_title, _LITERATURE_TITLE_MAX_CHARS)
1151	        if title_trunc:
1152	            title += "…"
1153	        # Normalize the authors value to a LIST before the per-author screening loop.
1154	        # Provenance may supply ``authors`` as a single STRING (not a list); iterating a bare
1155	        # string would screen it CHARACTER-by-character — corrupting a clean "Bloom N" into
1156	        # "B et al." and defeating the whole-phrase chrome screen (a chrome author would slip
1157	        # past char-by-char). A str => a ONE-element list (screened as ONE author entry); a
1158	        # list/tuple is kept as-is; None/other keeps the existing empty fallback.
1159	        raw_authors = b.get("authors")
1160	        if isinstance(raw_authors, str):
1161	            raw_authors = [raw_authors]
1162	        elif isinstance(raw_authors, (list, tuple)):
1163	            pass
1164	        else:
1165	            raw_authors = []
1166	        clean_authors = [
1167	            a for a in raw_authors
1168	            if str(a).strip() and not screen(str(a))  # drop per-author chrome/boilerplate
1169	        ]
1170	        author_str = _format_authors(clean_authors)
1171	        # Fall back to a CLEAN single-cited label: the ``[{num}]`` wrapper below appends the
1172	        # citation, so use bare "Source" here (never "Source [N]" which would double-cite).
1173	        literature = " — ".join(p for p in (author_str, title) if p) or "Source"
1174	
1175	        rows.append(_RowData(
1176	            num=num,
1177	            literature=f"{literature} [{num}]",
1178	            claim=excerpt,
1179	            claim_truncated=truncated,
1180	            geography=geography,
1181	            domain=domain,
1182	            risk=risk,
1183	            doc_key=_doc_identity(b),
1184	            cite_nums=[num],
1185	        ))
1186	    # Source-consolidation post-pass (CONSOLIDATE-KEEP-ALL, CLAUDE.md §-1.3). Gated ON by default
1187	    # (LAW VI kill-switch); OFF => the one-row-per-eid list is byte-identical. Runs BEFORE the sort
1188	    # so the collapsed multi-citation rows and the untouched singletons order together by ``num``.
1189	    if _source_consolidate_enabled():
1190	        rows = _consolidate_rows_by_source(rows)
1191	        # I-deepfix-001 (#1369) FIX 1: SECOND label-regroup pass catches the URL-vs-title splits and
1192	        # synonym-swap paraphrases the first (doc_key + Jaccard) pass leaves as duplicate rows. Kept
1193	        # UNDER the same master consolidation gate so PG_SUMMARY_TABLE_SOURCE_CONSOLIDATE=0 stays
1194	        # byte-identical to the one-row-per-eid legacy output.
1195	        rows = _regroup_rows_by_label(rows)
1196	    rows.sort(key=lambda r: r.num)
1197	    return rows

1200	def _own_verified_member_quotes(bib_row: dict) -> list[str]:
1201	    """Verbatim ``direct_quote`` texts from THIS bibliography row's OWN verified basket
1202	    members — the ONLY faithful fallback claim source when no clean kept-sentence was
1203	    captured for the source.
1204	
1205	    A member contributes iff BOTH hold: (a) it is THIS row's own source
1206	    (``member.evidence_id == bib_row.evidence_id``), and (b) its OWN isolated
1207	    ``span_verdict`` is ``SUPPORTS`` (the binding per-member verified gate — the very
1208	    members counted in ``verified_support_origin_count``). The rendered text is the
1209	    member's VERBATIM ``direct_quote``; the basket's synthesized/consolidated
1210	    ``claim_text`` is NEVER used (it can carry a DIFFERENT source's subject/predicate — a
1211	    cross-source fabrication). A member of another origin, or this source's own member
1212	    whose span is NOT SUPPORTS, contributes nothing. Order-stable, deduped. PURE."""
1213	    row_eid = str(bib_row.get("evidence_id") or "")
1214	    if not row_eid:
1215	        return []
1216	    out: list[str] = []
1217	    seen: set[str] = set()
1218	    for basket in (bib_row.get("baskets") or []):
1219	        if not isinstance(basket, dict):
1220	            continue
1221	        for member in (basket.get("supporting_members") or []):
1222	            if not isinstance(member, dict):
1223	                continue
1224	            if str(member.get("evidence_id") or "") != row_eid:
1225	                continue  # a co-basket OTHER source — never borrow its span
1226	            if str(member.get("span_verdict") or "").upper() != "SUPPORTS":
1227	                continue  # this source's own span is not verified-SUPPORTS => no fallback
1228	            quote = str(member.get("direct_quote") or "").strip()
1229	            if quote and quote not in seen:
1230	                seen.add(quote)
1231	                out.append(quote)
1232	    return out
1233

1235	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1236	# Markdown rendering
1237	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
1238	def _escape_cell(text: str) -> str:
1239	    return text.replace("\n", " ").replace("|", "\\|").strip()
1240	
1241	
1242	def _cell_for_role(role: str, row: _RowData) -> str:
1243	    if role == ROLE_LITERATURE:
1244	        return _escape_cell(row.literature) or GAP_CELL
1245	    if role == ROLE_CLAIM:
1246	        claim = row.claim + ("…" if row.claim_truncated else "")
1247	        return f"{_escape_cell(claim)} [{row.num}]"
1248	    if role == ROLE_GEOGRAPHY:
1249	        return _escape_cell("; ".join(row.geography)) or GAP_CELL
1250	    if role == ROLE_DOMAIN:
1251	        return _escape_cell("; ".join(row.domain)) or GAP_CELL
1252	    if role == ROLE_RISK:
1253	        return _escape_cell("; ".join(row.risk)) or GAP_CELL
1254	    return GAP_CELL

1257	def build_summary_table_markdown(
1258	    headers: list[str], rows: list[_RowData], include_heading: bool = True
1259	) -> str:
1260	    """Render the GFM table (header, separator, one row per verified source) plus a
1261	    faithfulness disclosure note and the idempotency marker. PURE.
1262	
1263	    ``include_heading`` (default True) preserves the legacy standalone block that carries
1264	    its own generic ``## Summary table`` heading (every existing caller is byte-identical).
1265	    When False (N3 anchor path, PG_SUMMARY_TABLE_ANCHOR_SECTION), ONLY that heading line and
1266	    its trailing blank separator are omitted so the block can be inserted DIRECTLY under the
1267	    report's own body heading that names the summary table; TABLE_MARKER, the disclosure note
1268	    and the table itself are byte-identical to the include_heading=True form."""
1269	    roles = assign_header_roles(headers)
1270	    esc_headers = [_escape_cell(h) for h in headers]
1271	    lines: list[str] = []
1272	    if include_heading:
1273	        lines.append("## Summary table")
1274	        lines.append("")
1275	    lines.append(TABLE_MARKER)
1276	    lines.append(
1277	        "_Built only from verified findings (CLAUDE.md §-1.3: a presentation of "
1278	        "already-verified content, no new claim). The Research-Literature and "
1279	        "Specific-Applications-and-Impacts cells trace to a strict-verify-passed "
1280	        "claim cited [N]. The Country/Region, Application-Area/Occupation and Key-Risks "
1281	        "cells list only terms that appear VERBATIM as whole words in that source's own "
1282	        "verified spans; a "
1283	        "— marks that no such term was found in the verified evidence (disclosed gap — "
1284	        "nothing was inferred)._"
1285	    )
1286	    lines.append("")
1287	    lines.append("| " + " | ".join(esc_headers) + " |")
1288	    lines.append("| " + " | ".join(["---"] * len(headers)) + " |")
1289	    for row in rows:
1290	        cells = [_cell_for_role(role, row) for role in roles]
1291	        lines.append("| " + " | ".join(cells) + " |")
1292	    return "\n".join(lines)
1293

1295	def _insert_before_appendix(report_md: str, table_md: str, appendix_boundary_marker: str) -> str:
1296	    """Insert the table at the END of the scored body — immediately before the audit
1297	    machinery appendix boundary when present, else appended at the end of the report.
1298	    PURE."""
1299	    block = "\n\n" + table_md.rstrip() + "\n"
1300	    if appendix_boundary_marker and appendix_boundary_marker in report_md:
1301	        idx = report_md.index(appendix_boundary_marker)
1302	        head = report_md[:idx].rstrip()
1303	        tail = report_md[idx:]
1304	        return head + block + "\n\n" + tail
1305	    return report_md.rstrip() + block

1308	# I-deepfix-001 (#1369) FIX 3 (iter3) — off-topic summary-table rows. Root cause (Fable): the promotion
1309	# topical gate is wired ONLY to the CWF single-source promotion partition, never to summary-table row
1310	# building, so an off-topic-but-span-verified source (materials-science MWCNT row [28] in a Gen-AI-and-jobs
1311	# report) rows-ifies unchecked. WEIGHT-NOT-FILTER / no-drop (Codex+Fable P1): a row whose claim shares EXACTLY
1312	# ZERO content-word overlap with the research question is DEMOTED to the bottom of the table — NEVER dropped
1313	# (lexical overlap is not a reliable off-topic signal; an on-topic synonym row has zero literal overlap too).
1314	# Every verified row survives; skips (no demotion) when the question yields no tokens. Default-ON
1315	# PG_SUMMARY_TABLE_TOPICAL_GATE.
1316	_ENV_TOPICAL_GATE = "PG_SUMMARY_TABLE_TOPICAL_GATE"
1317	
1318	
1319	def _topical_gate_enabled() -> bool:
1320	    """LAW VI kill-switch (default ON). OFF => rows pass through byte-identically."""
1321	    return os.environ.get(_ENV_TOPICAL_GATE, "1").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
1322	
1323	
1324	def _topical_tokens(text: str) -> frozenset[str]:
1325	    """Content-word tokens for the topical-overlap test, splitting on whitespace AND hyphens/slashes so a
1326	    compound like "employment-to-population" contributes "employment" (else an on-topic row that never
1327	    uses the question's exact surface form would be wrongly dropped). Stopword-stripped. PURE."""
1328	    out: set[str] = set()
1329	    for raw in re.split(r"[\s\-/]+", (text or "").lower()):
1330	        tok = raw.strip(".,;:!?()[]{}\"'`—–…%")
1331	        if not tok or tok in _CONSOLIDATE_STOPWORDS:
1332	            continue
1333	        out.add(tok)
1334	    return frozenset(out)
1335	
1336	
1337	def _topical_gate_rows(rows: list["_RowData"], research_question: str) -> list["_RowData"]:
1338	    """WEIGHT-NOT-FILTER / no-drop (Codex + Fable P1, §-1.3): NEVER drop a verified summary-table row on
1339	    topical grounds — a vocabulary-mismatch on-topic row ('Automation exposure is highest in clerical
1340	    occupations' vs a 'generative AI employment effects' question) has zero LITERAL question-token overlap
1341	    yet is genuinely on-topic, and lexical overlap is not a reliable off-topic signal. Instead DEMOTE: keep
1342	    EVERY row, but order the zero-overlap rows AFTER the overlapping ones (stable within each group) so
1343	    on-topic findings surface first without deleting any content. Emits the realized-effect marker. PURE
1344	    apart from the log."""
1345	    if not _topical_gate_enabled():
1346	        return rows
1347	    qtokens = _topical_tokens(research_question or "")
1348	    if not qtokens:
1349	        logger.info(
1350	            "[activation] summary_table_topical_gate: demoted=0 kept=%d (no question tokens -> skip)",
1351	            len(rows),
1352	        )
1353	        return rows
1354	    on_topic: list[_RowData] = []
1355	    off_topic: list[_RowData] = []
1356	    for r in rows:
1357	        ctoks = _topical_tokens(r.claim or "")
1358	        if ctoks and not (ctoks & qtokens):
1359	            off_topic.append(r)  # zero literal overlap -> DEMOTE to the bottom, never drop
1360	        else:
1361	            on_topic.append(r)
1362	    logger.info(
1363	        "[activation] summary_table_topical_gate: demoted=%d kept=%d (no-drop, on-topic-first)",
1364	        len(off_topic), len(on_topic) + len(off_topic),
1365	    )
1366	    return on_topic + off_topic
1367

1369	# I-deepfix-001 wave-2 (#1370) N3 — ANCHOR the verified summary table INTO the body section
1370	# whose heading NAMES it (e.g. "### Industry Application Cases and Risk Summary Table"), instead
1371	# of the detached generic "## Summary table" heading dropped before the Bibliography. Placement /
1372	# anchoring only: the row building, verification gating, chrome screen and disclosed-gap ("—")
1373	# cells are UNTOUCHED (they are correct honest behaviour). Default-OFF flag (LAW VI): unset/"0"
1374	# => byte-identical current output (the legacy _insert_before_appendix path).
1375	_ENV_ANCHOR_SECTION = "PG_SUMMARY_TABLE_ANCHOR_SECTION"
1376	
1377	# A body heading (2..5 ``#``) whose text names a "summary table". Anchored to line starts
1378	# (MULTILINE) so only headings match; IGNORECASE so "SUMMARY TABLE" / "Summary Table" all anchor.
1379	_SUMMARY_TABLE_HEADING_RE = re.compile(
1380	    r"^#{2,5}\s+.*summary\s+table", re.IGNORECASE | re.MULTILINE
1381	)
1382	
1383	
1384	def _anchor_section_enabled() -> bool:
1385	    """LAW VI flag (default OFF). OFF => the legacy detached-heading path => byte-identical."""
1386	    return os.environ.get(_ENV_ANCHOR_SECTION, "0").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
1387

1389	def _find_summary_table_heading(report_md: str, appendix_boundary_marker: str) -> int | None:
1390	    """Scan ONLY the body BEFORE the appendix boundary for the FIRST markdown heading line whose
1391	    text names a summary table; return the offset just PAST that heading line (and its single
1392	    trailing blank line, if any) — the insertion point directly under the heading, before the
1393	    section's existing prose. Return None when no such heading exists in the body. PURE."""
1394	    if not report_md:
1395	        return None
1396	    if appendix_boundary_marker and appendix_boundary_marker in report_md:
1397	        body_limit = report_md.index(appendix_boundary_marker)
1398	    else:
1399	        body_limit = len(report_md)
1400	    for match in _SUMMARY_TABLE_HEADING_RE.finditer(report_md):
1401	        if match.start() >= body_limit:
1402	            break  # the heading is inside the appendix machinery, not the scored body
1403	        line_end = report_md.find("\n", match.end())
1404	        if line_end == -1:
1405	            return len(report_md)  # heading is the last line; append point is EOF
1406	        pos = line_end + 1
1407	        next_nl = report_md.find("\n", pos)
1408	        if next_nl == -1:
1409	            # only a trailing (possibly blank) remnant after the heading line
1410	            return len(report_md) if report_md[pos:].strip() == "" else pos
1411	        # skip a SINGLE blank line immediately after the heading (the heading's separator)
1412	        if report_md[pos:next_nl].strip() == "":
1413	            return next_nl + 1
1414	        return pos
1415	    return None
1416

1418	def render_requested_summary_table(
1419	    *,
1420	    research_question: str,
1421	    bibliography: list[dict],
1422	    section_claims: list[dict] | None = None,
1423	    existing_report_md: str = "",
1424	    appendix_boundary_marker: str = "",
1425	    chrome_screen: Callable[[str], bool] | None = None,
1426	    contract_headers: list[str] | None = None,
1427	) -> SummaryTableResult:
1428	    """Build and insert the prompt-requested summary table into ``existing_report_md``.
1429	
1430	    No-op (``changed=False``, text unchanged) when the kill-switch is OFF, the prompt
1431	    does not request a titled table, the report already carries the table marker
1432	    (idempotent / resume-safe), or no source carries a verified claim. Otherwise
1433	    returns the report text with the table inserted at the end of the scored body.
1434	    PURE (reads only the env kill-switch).
1435	
1436	    I-wire-001 F1 — the header source is resolved as ``contract_headers or
1437	    parse_requested_headers(research_question)``: when the caller supplies the per-task
1438	    output CONTRACT's ``required_table.columns`` (>= 2 clean names) those are
1439	    AUTHORITATIVE (they are transcribed verbatim from the gold prompt and are exactly
1440	    what the run-validity gate matches against), otherwise the parser recovers the
1441	    headers from the bound question. None/empty ``contract_headers`` => byte-identical to
1442	    the prior parse-from-question behaviour."""
1443	    if not summary_table_enabled():
1444	        return SummaryTableResult(text=existing_report_md, changed=False, canary="disabled")
1445	    if existing_report_md and TABLE_MARKER in existing_report_md:
1446	        return SummaryTableResult(text=existing_report_md, changed=False, canary="already_present")
1447	    # CONTRACT path is primary (authoritative when it yields >= 2 clean columns); the
1448	    # bound-question parser is the fallback.
1449	    headers = [str(h).strip() for h in (contract_headers or []) if str(h).strip()]
1450	    if len(headers) < 2:
1451	        headers = parse_requested_headers(research_question)
1452	    if len(headers) < 2:
1453	        return SummaryTableResult(text=existing_report_md, changed=False, canary="no_table_requested")
1454	    rows = _build_rows(bibliography or [], section_claims, chrome_screen)
1455	    # I-deepfix-001 (#1369) FIX 3 (iter3): DEMOTE off-topic table rows (literal zero question-overlap) to the
1456	    # bottom — never drop (weight-not-filter / §-1.3 no-drop). Every verified row survives.
1457	    rows = _topical_gate_rows(rows, research_question)
1458	    if not rows:
1459	        return SummaryTableResult(
1460	            text=existing_report_md, changed=False, canary="no_verified_rows", headers=headers
1461	        )
1462	    # N3 (#1370): when enabled AND the report body carries a heading that NAMES the summary
1463	    # table, anchor the (heading-less) table DIRECTLY under that heading, keeping the section's
1464	    # existing narrative prose below it (§-1.3: never drop content). Otherwise keep the exact
1465	    # legacy path — the detached "## Summary table" block inserted before the appendix boundary.
1466	    anchor_pos: int | None = None
1467	    if _anchor_section_enabled() and existing_report_md:
1468	        anchor_pos = _find_summary_table_heading(existing_report_md, appendix_boundary_marker)
1469	    if anchor_pos is not None:
1470	        table_md = build_summary_table_markdown(headers, rows, include_heading=False)
1471	        new_text = (
1472	            existing_report_md[:anchor_pos]
1473	            + table_md.rstrip()
1474	            + "\n\n"
1475	            + existing_report_md[anchor_pos:]
1476	        )
1477	        anchored = True
1478	    else:
1479	        table_md = build_summary_table_markdown(headers, rows)
1480	        if not existing_report_md:
1481	            new_text = table_md
1482	        else:
1483	            new_text = _insert_before_appendix(
1484	                existing_report_md, table_md, appendix_boundary_marker
1485	            )
1486	        anchored = False
1487	    n_geo = sum(1 for r in rows if r.geography)
1488	    n_dom = sum(1 for r in rows if r.domain)
1489	    n_risk = sum(1 for r in rows if r.risk)
1490	    canary = (
1491	        f"{CANARY_TAG} rows={len(rows)} cols={len(headers)} "
1492	        f"geo_filled={n_geo} domain_filled={n_dom} risk_filled={n_risk} "
